nodes.label: Account details, customization, and connected domains
nodes.snippet:
# Account details, customization, and connected domains

 

Admins can view and manage these settings from your profile picture (top right of the home page) > **Account administration** > **Account settings**. From here you can view your account name and domain, customize your logo and theme, brand the mobile app, and connect additional email domains to your account.

 
 Access to Account settings depends on your [admin role](/docs/admin/users/user-roles-and-permissions). Roles with account-level administration access can reach this page.
 

## Account details

### Account name and domain

Your account name and domain are automatically assigned based on the email domain you signed up with. You can change the domain at any time, as long as no other Kissflow organization is already using it.

To change your domain:

1. Go to your profile picture > **Account administration** > **Account settings** > **Account details**.
2. Click the domain field and enter your new domain.
3. Confirm the change.

 
 Changing your domain logs everyone out of their current session, including you. You're redirected to the new domain shortly after; other users need to navigate to the new domain themselves to sign back in — share the new domain with your team before you switch. Once you move off your old domain, it becomes available for another organization to claim.
 

![](/migration-assets/account-details-customization-and-connected-domains/1.png)

 
 Want a fully custom domain instead of ` .kissflo

nodes.label: Admin notification settings
nodes.snippet:
# Admin notification settings

 

**Notification settings** lets Super Admins control how notifications go out across the entire account — which channels are on by default, which server sends the emails, and what those emails look like.

## Global notifications

1. Click your profile picture > **Account administration** > **Notification settings**.

 ![Account administration menu with Notification settings highlighted](/migration-assets/admin-notification-settings/2.png)
 

2. Under **Global notifications**, turn the following toggles on or off for every user on the account:
 - **Email notifications** — sends notifications by email.
 - **Web app notifications** — sends notifications within the web application.
 - **Push notifications** — sends push notifications.
 - **Bot notifications** — sends notifications through the Kissflow bot in Slack, Microsoft Teams, or Google Chat.

 
 Essential system notifications — such as those related to account security and user management — are prioritized and sent regardless of your channel settings, to make sure nobody misses critical updates.
 

 
 The examples below illustrate the kind of system notifications that bypass channel settings. The exact list may vary and isn't limited to these events.
 

| Notification | Trigger |
| --- | --- |
| User activated | A user's account is activated. |
| User deactivated | A user's account is deactivated. |
| Resend email verification | The verification email is resent. |
| Email address updated | A

nodes.label: Bot settings
nodes.snippet:
# Bot settings

 

As a Super Admin, you can enable the **Kissflow Bot** for your organization's chat app — Slack, Microsoft Teams, or Google Chat. Once enabled, members can sign in to Kissflow from the chat app and receive real-time notifications about their processes and boards without switching tabs, then act on them (view, approve, reject, add notes) right from the chat.

 

## Enable the Kissflow Bot

1. Click your profile picture in the top right corner and go to **Account administration** > **Account settings** > **Bot settings**.
2. Turn on the toggle for each chat app your organization uses: **Google Chat**, **Slack**, or **Microsoft Teams**.

![Bot settings page with toggles for Google Chat, Slack, and Microsoft Teams](/migration-assets/bot-settings/1.png)

Once a chat app is turned on, members can sign in to it using the steps below.

## Sign in to Kissflow from Google Chat

1. Install the Kissflow app:
 1. Go to the [Google Workspace Marketplace](https://workspace.google.com/marketplace?pann=ogb).
 2. Find the **Kissflow** app and install it.

 ![Kissflow app listing in the Google Workspace Marketplace](/migration-assets/bot-settings/3.png)
 

2. Look for **Kissflow** in your Google Chat list.
3. Sign in to your Kissflow account:
 1. Type **/login** inside the Kissflow app and press **Enter** to sign in. A login URL is generated automatically.
 2. Click the login URL, enter your Kissflow account domain, and click **Submit**.
 3. Click **Allow** in the permissions

nodes.label: File upload settings
nodes.snippet:
# File upload settings

 

As a [Super Admin, or an IAM Admin](/docs/admin/users/user-roles-and-permissions), you control which file attachment services account users can upload files from in Kissflow.

## Choose your attachment services

1. Click your profile picture in the upper-right corner of the Home screen.
2. Click **Account administration** > **Account settings** > **File upload settings**.
3. Turn the toggle on or off for each service you want to allow:
 - Google Drive
 - Dropbox
 - Box
 - OneDrive
 - Local files

![File upload settings page with toggles for Google Drive, Dropbox, Box, OneDrive, and Local files](/migration-assets/file-upload-settings/1.png)

 
 At least one service must stay enabled. Kissflow won't let you turn off the last remaining toggle, since users need at least one way to upload attachments.
 

## How Kissflow secures your attachments

All Kissflow attachments are hosted in Google Cloud Storage (GCS). Data is [encrypted in transit and at rest](https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/introduction#securing_your_data) using GCS's standard security policies, with Kissflow's application and GCS mutually authenticating each other.

To upload or download an attachment, Kissflow generates a secure link so the file transfer happens directly between the client and GCS instead of routing through additional hops.

Kissflow also blocks manipulated or disguised files from being uploaded. For example, if only JPGs are allowed for a field and someone tries to up

nodes.label: Flow settings
nodes.snippet:
# Flow settings

 

[Super Admins and IAM Admins](/docs/admin/users/user-roles-and-permissions) can control who's allowed to create new flows in your account, enable or disable flow types, and set account-wide print, download, and email action permissions.

## Access flow settings

1. Click your profile picture at the top right.
2. Select **Account administration** > **Flow settings**.

All flow types — process, board, dataset, and integration — are enabled by default.

## Enable or disable a flow type

1. On the **Flow settings** page, click the flow type you want to configure.
2. Toggle the flow type **on** or **off**.

 
 If you disable a flow type, its existing data becomes temporarily inaccessible and no one can create a new flow of that type until you enable it again.
 

## Control who can create a new flow

1. On the **Flow settings** page, click the flow type you want to configure.
2. Under **Who can create a new flow?**, choose one of three options:
 - **Anyone** — every user in the organization can create a new flow of this type without approval.
 - **Super Admins** — only the Account Owner and Super Admins can create a new flow.
 - **Anyone, after the approval of** — any user can request a new flow, but it's created only after the approvers you specify approve the request. Use the dropdown to add or remove approvers.

![Flow settings page showing the who-can-create options for a flow type](/migration-assets/flow-settings/1.png)

 
 Changing this setting only affect

nodes.label: Format settings
nodes.snippet:
# Format settings

 

As a Super Admin — or any admin granted the Format Settings permission — you control your account's default time zone, language, and date format. These settings determine how dates, times, and flow data are displayed across your organization.

 
 Individual users can override the account's time zone, date format, and language for their own account from **My settings**. See [Personal info, format settings, and task delegation](/docs/use/personal-settings/personal-info-format-settings-and-task-delegation).
 

## Update format settings

1. Click your profile picture in the upper-right corner of the Home screen.
2. Click **Account administration** > **Format settings**.
3. Set your account's time zone, language, and date format (details below).
4. Click **Save**.

![Format settings page with the timezone map, language, and date format options](/migration-assets/format-settings/1.png)

### Time zone

Your organization's time zone is used when a scheduled action in an integration runs, or when a [deadline](/docs/build/processes/workflow-design/configuring-deadline) in a workflow is evaluated — all flow data is matched to this time zone.

Select a time zone directly from the dropdown, or use the map below it: click a region to select its time zone, and use the zoom controls to find smaller zones more precisely.

### Language

Choose the display language for your account. Kissflow supports more than 20 languages, including English, French, Spanish, German, Itali

nodes.label: Geolocation settings
nodes.snippet:
# Geolocation settings

 

The [Geolocation field](/docs/build/forms/field-types/geolocation-field) lets users capture and view locations on a form using Google Maps. Before anyone can use it, an Account Admin needs to add a Google Maps API key in **Geolocation settings** — without a valid key, the field can't load Google Maps.

![Geolocation settings page with the API key input box](/migration-assets/geolocation-settings/2.png)

## Access geolocation settings

1. Sign in to Kissflow.
2. Click your profile picture in the upper-right corner of the page.
3. Click **Account administration > Account settings > Geolocation settings**.

## Add or update the Google Maps API key

1. On the **Geolocation settings** page, enter your Google Maps API key in the input box.

 
 You can use an existing Google Maps API key or create a new one. Click the **Google Maps API key** link on the page for instructions on Google's developer documentation site.
 

2. Save your changes.

 
 After you add or update the API key, notify your users to reload Kissflow so the change takes effect.
 

## When the API key is missing or invalid

If Kissflow can't load Google Maps for the Geolocation field, the message shown depends on where the form is being filled out:

- On the **Kissflow mobile app**, users see a message asking them to contact their Super Admin to add the Google Maps API key.
- On **web**, users see a general connection error instead, without a direct mention of the API key.

![Geolocation fi

nodes.label: Subscription and billing
nodes.snippet:
# Subscription and billing

 

Your account's subscription details — plan status, user license count, and invoices — are all available from Account administration.

 
 Only Super Admins and Billing Admins can access this page. Learn more about [roles in Kissflow](/docs/admin/users/user-roles-and-permissions).
 

## View your subscription details

1. Click your profile picture in the top-right corner and select **Account administration**.
2. Go to **Subscription & billing**.

 ![Subscription and billing page showing plan status and user license details](/migration-assets/subscription-and-billing/1.png)
 

This page shows the current status of your plan and the number of user licenses available under it.

 
 Kissflow offers several plan tiers beyond the basics. To purchase additional licenses, change plans, or adjust your billing frequency, click **Chat for help** under **Subscription details** — see [Deprecation of self-serve option in Kissflow subscription & billing](/docs/whats-new/discontinued-services/important-announcement-deprecation-of-self-serve-option-in-kissflow-subscription-billing).
 

## View and download an invoice

1. Go to **Account administration** > **Subscription & billing**.
2. Click **View invoice**.

 ![Subscription and billing page with the View invoice action](/migration-assets/subscription-and-billing/2.png)
 

From here, you can view and download the invoice for your account.

## What's next

- [User roles and permissions](/docs/admin/users/user-roles

nodes.label: Task delegation
nodes.snippet:
# Task delegation

 

From **Account Administration**, [Super Admins](/docs/admin/users/user-roles-and-permissions) can delegate a user's future process tasks to another user — for example, when someone is out of office. You can delegate all the processes a user is part of, or only specific ones.

 
 Only new process tasks are delegated. Tasks already assigned to the user when you set up the delegation stay with them.
 

## Task delegation table

The task delegation table lists every delegation in the account, split across two tabs:

- **Set by admins** — delegations Super Admins have created.
- **Set by users** — delegations users have set up for themselves.

From this table you can create a new delegation, and view, edit, or cancel any existing one.

## Add a new delegation

1. Click your profile picture > **Account administration** > **Task delegation** > **+ New**.
2. Select the user whose tasks you want to delegate.
3. Select the start and end dates for the delegation.
4. Choose whether to delegate tasks from **all processes** the user is part of, or only **specific processes**.
5. Select who the tasks should go to:
 - If you chose **All processes**, select the single user to delegate to.
 - If you chose **Specific processes**, you can select a different user for each process.
6. To delegate several processes to the same user in one go, select their checkboxes, click **Delegate to**, and choose the user.
7. Click **Create**.

 
 Notifications for delegated process steps

nodes.label: Weekends and holidays
nodes.snippet:
# Weekends and holidays

 

As a [Super Admin](/docs/admin/users/user-roles-and-permissions), you can set your organization's workweek and holidays so they're factored in when [deadlines](/docs/build/processes/workflow-design/configuring-deadline) and SLAs are calculated for workflow activities.

## Set your organization's weekend

1. Click your profile picture in the top right corner and go to **Account administration** > **Account settings** > **Weekends and holidays**.
2. Under **Weekends**, click each day your organization doesn't work to select it.

 
 No weekend days are selected by default. Set your weekend here before it's needed for deadline or SLA calculations.
 

## Add office holidays

1. Click **Add holiday**.
2. Select the date.
3. Give the holiday a name.

![Weekends and holidays settings page showing the weekly working days and holiday list](/migration-assets/weekends-and-holidays/1.png)

 
 Holidays don't recur automatically. Your holiday list is for a single year, so you'll need to add next year's holidays yourself when the time comes.
 

## How this affects deadlines and SLAs

Weekend days and holidays you've configured here are excluded from [deadline](/docs/build/processes/workflow-design/configuring-deadline) and SLA calculations for workflow activities. If part of a task's allotted time falls on a non-working day, that day isn't counted — the deadline extends to give the assignee the full amount of working time you intended.

- [Configur

nodes.label: Audit log
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic ✓ Enterprise

## Overview

The audit log is a centralized record of all activities that take place in your Kissflow account. It helps administrators track who did what, when, and from where by capturing events across your account, such as, user activity, security-related events or changes made to account settings and flows. 
 
Audit logs preserve the record of actions taken in your account, giving admins clarity, traceability, and oversight. You can also filter for specific events and export logs for compliance or analysis purposes. 

The account-level audit log is available to the [Super Admins,](/t/35h4ak036h4gg/user-roles-permissions) [IAM Admins](/t/35h4ak0/user-roles-permissions#iam-admin) (account management logs) and [User Admins](/t/35h4ak036h4gg/user-roles-permissions) (user and group management logs). To view audit log, go to **Profile > Account administration >** **Audit log**. 
 
Similarly, audit logs for flows is available to flow admins across [process](/t/g9h9qtg), boards, datasets, [integrations](/t/y4h4akm), apps and [portals](/t/y4ymrjg). They can be accessed by clicking the **More options** button ( ![](/migration-assets/audit-log/2.svg)) > **Audit log**.

![](/migration-assets/audit-log/1.png)

You can get the following details from the **Audit log** section of your account:

- **Timestamp**: The exact time when a specific action happened or was performed.
- **Acted by**: The account user who acted.
- **[Event](/t/60ytnn6/event-cat

nodes.label: Event categories and actions
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

**Event category** is a group of actions, to help admins monitor, review, and audit operations efficiently from the [audit log](/t/h7h4akc/admin-history) page. These categories include process administration, board administration and the like. 
 
An **action** refers to the specific activity carried out by users in an account like dataset permission changes, user activation, or field modifications. Each set of action is mapped to an event category, making it easier to track for events.

This documentation lists all event categories and their corresponding actions.

> **Note:**
> 
> Other actions not listed in this documentation fall under the **unclassified event** category.

nodes.label: Backing up data to Amazon S3
nodes.snippet:
# Backing up data to Amazon S3

 

Amazon Simple Storage Service (S3) is one of the storage destinations you can configure for Kissflow's data backup feature, alongside Google Drive, Google Cloud Storage, and email delivery. Once configured, Kissflow exports your account data as JSON files, packages them into ZIP files, and uploads them directly to the S3 bucket you specify.

 
 Data backup is available on request. Contact Kissflow support to enable it for your account.
 

To configure backup preferences, you need admin access to the **Data backup** section of your account — see [User roles and permissions](/docs/admin/users/user-roles-and-permissions) for how backup access is granted.

## Before you start

Grant the following Amazon S3 bucket permissions in your IAM policy:

- **s3:PutObject** — allows Kissflow to add backup files to your bucket.
- **s3:GetObject** — allows Kissflow to fetch objects from your bucket.
- **s3:ListBucket** — allows Kissflow to list the objects in your bucket.

You'll also need an [AWS access key ID and secret access key](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_credentials_access-keys.html#Using_CreateAccessKey) for authentication. Copy these to your clipboard, or download them as a CSV file — either works when you authenticate inside Kissflow.

## Start a backup configuration

1. Sign in to Kissflow.
2. Click your profile picture at the top right of the page, then click **Account administration** > **Data backup**.
3. Click **Start

nodes.label: Backing up data to Google Cloud Storage (GCS)
nodes.snippet:
# Backing up data to Google Cloud Storage (GCS)

 

 
 Data backup is available on request. Contact Kissflow support to enable it on your account.
 

Google Cloud Storage is one of the storage destinations you can configure for account data backups, alongside Amazon S3, Google Drive, and Kissflow's own storage. Once configured, Kissflow exports your account data as JSON files, packages them into ZIP files, and uploads them directly to the GCS bucket you specify.

As a [Super Admin](/docs/admin/users/user-roles-and-permissions#super-admin), you can configure Kissflow to back up your account data to a Google Cloud Storage (GCS) bucket you control.

## Prerequisites

Before you start configuration, grant these permissions in your Google Cloud IAM policy for the target bucket:

- **storage.buckets.get** — returns metadata for the bucket.
- **storage.objects.create** — allows Kissflow to write backup files to the bucket.
- **storage.objects.get** — allows Kissflow to read object metadata from the bucket.

You'll also need a [service account key](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/keys-create-delete#iam-service-account-keys-create-console) downloaded as a JSON file — you upload this file inside Kissflow to authenticate.

![Animation of creating a Google Cloud service account key](/migration-assets/backing-up-data-to-google-cloud-service-gcs/6.gif)

## Start data backup configuration

1. Sign in to Kissflow.
2. Click your profile picture at the top right of the page, then click **Acc

nodes.label: Backing up data to Google Drive
nodes.snippet:
# Backing up data to Google Drive

 

Google Drive is one of the storage destinations you can configure for Kissflow's data backup feature, alongside Amazon S3, Google Cloud Storage, and email delivery. Once configured, Kissflow exports your account data as JSON files, packages them into ZIP files, and uploads them directly to the Google Drive folder you specify.

 
 Data backup is available on request. Contact Kissflow support to enable it for your account.
 

To configure backup preferences, you need admin access to the **Data backup** section of your account — see [User roles and permissions](/docs/admin/users/user-roles-and-permissions) for how backup access is granted.

## Start a backup configuration

1. Sign in to Kissflow.
2. Click your profile picture at the top right of the page, then click **Account administration** > **Data backup**.
3. Click **Start configuration** on the **Data backup** page.

 

Setting up a backup involves four steps: authenticating your storage destination, managing backup preferences, selecting the data categories to include, and reviewing your configuration before you start the backup.

## Authenticate your Google Drive account

1. Select **Google Drive** as the storage mode from the dropdown.

 ![Storage mode authentication step with Google Drive selected](/migration-assets/backing-up-data-to-google-drive/1.png)
 

2. Sign in with your Google account.
3. Click **Next**.

## Manage backup preferences

1. **Set the file location.** Paste or

nodes.label: Backing up data to Kissflow
nodes.snippet:
# Backing up data to Kissflow

 

 
 Data backup is available on request. Contact Kissflow support to enable it on your account.
 

This is the simplest of Kissflow's backup destinations: instead of authenticating a cloud storage account, you choose **Email** as the storage mode and receive a downloadable link once the backup completes. As a [Super Admin](/docs/admin/users/user-roles-and-permissions#super-admin), you configure preferences once and reuse them for every future backup.

 
 You can only back up your data once every six months, and a backup can't be canceled once it starts. Plan your configuration and timing accordingly.
 

## Start data backup configuration

1. Sign in to Kissflow.
2. Click your profile picture at the top right of the page, then click **Account administration** > **Data backup**.
3. Click **Start configuration** on the **Data backup** page.

Configuration involves three steps: authenticating a storage mode, managing backup settings, and selecting data categories.

## Select email as your storage mode

1. Select **Email** as the storage mode from the dropdown.

 ![Data backup storage mode dropdown with Email selected](/migration-assets/backing-up-data-to-kissflow/1.png)
 

2. Click **Next**.

 
 After the backup completes, you'll receive a downloadable link by email. Download the file within seven days of receiving that email — the link expires after that.
 

## Manage backup settings

1. **Select ZIP file size.** Choose **4 GB**, **8 GB**, **16 G

nodes.label: Compliance and backup history
nodes.snippet:
# Compliance and backup history

 

 
 Data backup is available on request. Contact Kissflow support to enable it for your account.
 

As a [Super Admin](/docs/admin/users/user-roles-and-permissions#super-admin), you manage your account's data backup — you can edit its configuration, cancel a backup that's still in the queue, and review its history.

## Cancel a queued backup

You can cancel a backup only while it's still in the queue, before processing starts. Once a backup is in progress, cancellation is no longer available.

1. Sign in to Kissflow.
2. Click your profile picture at the top right of the page, then click **Account administration** > **Data backup**.
3. While the backup shows as queued, click **Cancel** next to it.

## Edit your backup configuration

While a backup is queued or in progress, its configuration is read-only — you can view it, but not change it. Once the backup completes, the configuration becomes editable again.

1. Sign in to Kissflow.
2. Click your profile picture at the top right of the page, then click **Account administration** > **Data backup**.
3. Click the **More options** button (![More options icon](/migration-assets/compliance-and-backup-history/1.png)) beside **Backup now**, then click **Edit configuration** to change your backup settings, or **Delete configuration** to remove them entirely and stop future backups until you set up a new configuration.

![Data backup page with the More options menu open, showing Edit configuration, Bac

nodes.label: Data backup overview
nodes.snippet:
# Data backup overview

 

 
 Data backup is available on request. Contact Kissflow support to have it enabled for your account.
 

Data backup in Kissflow safeguards your organization's data against loss and security incidents, and helps meet audit and compliance requirements. [Super admins](/docs/admin/users/user-roles-and-permissions) can export account data to a cloud storage destination or a downloadable link, keeping it accessible for reading, archiving, and analysis outside of Kissflow.

 

## Storage destinations

When you configure a backup, you choose where the exported files go:

- [Google Drive](/docs/admin/data-backup/backing-up-data-to-google-drive)
- [Amazon Simple Storage Service (S3)](/docs/admin/data-backup/backing-up-data-to-amazon-s3)
- [Google Cloud Storage (GCS)](/docs/admin/data-backup/backing-up-data-to-google-cloud-service-gcs)
- [Kissflow backup](/docs/admin/data-backup/backing-up-data-to-kissflow) — no external account needed; Kissflow emails you a downloadable link once the backup completes.

## What gets backed up

Configuring a backup involves authenticating your chosen storage destination, setting backup file preferences (like the ZIP split size and optional encryption), and selecting which categories of data to include:

- **User and group data** — all account user and group records.
- **Transactional data** — flow item details such as status, comments, and assignees, across Processes, Boards, Datasets, Forms, and Projects.
- **Audit log data**

nodes.label: Understanding your backup output
nodes.snippet:
# Understanding your backup output

 

 
 Data backup is available on request. Contact the support team to have it enabled on your account.
 

Once your account backup finishes, you receive a set of JSON files and your original attachments, packaged into ZIP files (split and optionally encrypted based on your configuration). This article explains what's inside that package, how the data is organized, and how to read it.

If you haven't run a backup yet, start with [Data backup overview](/docs/admin/data-backup/data-backup-overview), which covers the available storage options.

## How the backup is packaged

Your backup mirrors the data categories you selected during configuration. After you unzip the package, you'll see a file for user and group data, a folder each for transactional data and audit logs, plus a flow index and a readme:

![Unzipped backup package showing the user and group data file, transactional data and audit log folders, flow index, and readme](/migration-assets/understanding-your-backup-output/2.png)

At a glance, here's what each part is for:

| Part | What it is | Best used for |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `UserAndGroup.json` | Your master identity file: every user, external user, service account, role, and group in the account. | Mapping permissions and your directory structure; resolving "who did what." |
| `TransactionalData-0000/` | The actual records from your flows, plus their comments and attachments. | Loading operational data into tools like Power BI

nodes.label: Duplicating and deleting a dataset
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

You can easily duplicate datasets for reuse or delete them when they’re no longer needed. Here's how to manage both actions:

## Duplicating a dataset

If your business requirements are in such a way that you cannot use an existing dataset as is but need a slightly modified version of it, then you can opt to create a replica of it. Duplicating a dataset creates a copy of it so that you can modify its structure without affecting its original version.

1. Click the **More options** button (![](/migration-assets/publishing-editing-duplicating-archiving-and-deleting-a-process/6.png)) and then click **Duplicate**
2. The default name of the new dataset is **Copy of <original dataset name>**. However, you can modify it as you want.
3. Check the box if you would like to retain the same values and views for the new dataset.
4. Click **Duplicate**.

![](/migration-assets/duplicating-and-deleting-a-dataset/1.png)

## Deleting a dataset

1. Click the **More options** button (![](/migration-assets/publishing-editing-duplicating-archiving-and-deleting-a-process/6.png)) and then click **Delete.** Kissflow checks if any flow is using the dataset you are trying to delete. Its dependencies, if any, will be shown on your screen. You can delete the dataset only after you remove the dependencies.![](/migration-assets/duplicating-and-deleting-a-dataset/2.png) 
 When deleting a dataset with granted app access, only dependencies in the production environment

nodes.label: Importing and exporting data from a dataset
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

The **CSV import** feature in your dataset will help you transfer data from a CSV file or a spreadsheet with a **.csv** extension to your Kissflow dataset. Once imported, your CSV rows will appear as rows in your dataset, and each column will contain metadata about your data. 

## Organizing the data in your CSV file

The CSV Import feature allows you to import data in bulk into Kissflow. Here are some guidelines to follow when preparing a CSV file for import:

- **Mandatory fields**: If mandatory fields exist in your Dataset form, you must map **at least one column** in your CSV file with the required fields.
- **Data**: Within the CSV file, you can map only the following field types to your dataset: **Text, Text area, Number, Rating, Date, Date-time, Currency, Yes/no, User, Single-select dropdown, Multi-select dropdown, Slider, and Checkbox**.
 - You cannot import data from a CSV file for the following field types: Attachment, checklist, signature, and image. 
 - For **Yes/No** fields, you can use any binary values: True/False, 1/0, Yes/No.
- **Delimiter**: You must only use the comma (**,**) as a delimiter inside the CSV file.
- **Rows**: The CSV file can have up to **50,000 rows**, excluding the header.
- **Columns**: Your CSV file can have up to 100 columns. The CSV column names can be alphanumeric but should contain only the following characters: space, hyphen (-), and underscore (\_).
- Encoding: The CSV file must have a .csv exte

nodes.label: Managing associated flows in a dataset
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans: ✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

The **Associated flows** section provides an overview of how your dataset interacts with other flows. It helps you understand dependencies, making managing and updating them easier.

To access associated flows, click **Manage** at the top-right corner of the dataset and select **Associated flows**.

![](/migration-assets/managing-associated-flows-in-a-dataset/2.gif)

## Looked up flows

[Lookup fields](/t/g9h4azr/lookup-field) allow a dataset to retrieve and display information from other flows. This section is divided into two parts:

### **Flows looked up to**

This section shows the flows that reference this dataset to display or fetch information. For example, if a Process references vendor names or vendor IDs from this dataset, the process' name will appear here.

> **Note:**
> 
> If a value in the master dataset is updated, any existing requests that have already used that value will remain unchanged. However, any workflows that reference the updated value will use the new data when processing new requests.

![](/migration-assets/managing-associated-flows-in-a-dataset/1.gif)

To configure the reference, click **Configure**, and you'll be redirected to the [Security settings](/t/83h4al5/security-settings-and-access-control) page.

### **Flows looked up from**

This section displays the flows this dataset pulls data from. For example, if your Vendor name dataset retrieves address data from another dataset, the name of that flow will b

nodes.label: Managing audit logs in a dataset
nodes.snippet:
**Audit log** is a chronological record of user activities within your dataset. These activities include actions performed on flows and items by users.

Audit logs help maintain transparency and accountability by tracking changes at both the **Flow-level** and **Item-level**.

## Accessing the Audit Log

To view the audit log:

- Navigate to **Manage** \> **Audit log**.
 
- Use the toggle to switch between **Flow-level** and **Item-level** views.
 

![](/migration-assets/managing-audit-logs-in-a-dataset/3.gif)

## What you can see in the audit log

Each log entry provides detailed information about a specific event:

- **Audit ID**: A unique identifier automatically generated for every recorded event.
 
- **Timestamp**: The exact date and time the action occurred.
 
- **Actor**: The user account that performed the action.
 
- **Action**: Describes the specific activity, such as Dataset published, Items updated in batch, and so on.
 
- **Object**: The resource or entity that was affected.
 
- **Event Category**: A general label for the event type, useful for filtering. For example, Dataset administration. 
 
- **Platform**: The device or operating system used during the action.
 
- **IP Address**: The network address from which the action was performed.
 
- **Batch ID**: A shared identifier for a group of related events, useful for correlating actions. Unlike the Audit ID, multiple events can share the same Batch ID.
 

> **Note**:
> 
> Audit log entries may appear in the inte

nodes.label: Managing filters
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

A filter helps you view a confined set of data that matches certain conditions. With advanced filters, you can filter contextually on a few attributes like user and time thereby giving more depth to the filter conditions.

## Creating a filter

1. Click the **Filter** button (![](/migration-assets/managing-filters/5.png)) on the dataset's page.
2. Click **Add a filter**.
3. Select a field that you want to add to the filter from the **Select field** dropdown to define your filter conditions:
 1. The operators are contextually populated based on the field you select. Choose an appropriate operator such as **Equal to**, **Not equal to**, **Contains**, **Is empty**, and so on.
 2. Next, select a **Field** or enter the **Value** against which you are validating this condition.
4. Click **Add a condition** to add as many **AND/OR** conditions as you want.![](/migration-assets/managing-filters/1.png)  
5. Similarly, click **\+ Add new filter** to add as many filters as you want.
 - After adding enough conditions and filters, choose between **AND** and **OR** conditions based on your preference. **AND** **operator** checks if all the applied filter conditions are met, and **OR operator** checks if at least one of the applied filter conditions is met.
6. Click **Apply** to filter records in your dataset.

You can use the ( - ) symbol in red beside every filter condition to remove it, and the close icon beside every filter to delete it.

## User-b

nodes.label: Managing members and permissions
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

You control who can access and manage your datasets through roles: **Admin**, **Member**, and **Viewer**. Each role comes with specific permissions to ensure the right level of access for every collaborator.

Here's a quick guide to what each role can do.

## Admin

A dataset admin has the highest privilege in a dataset and anyone who creates a dataset becomes its Administrator, that is, **Admin** by default.

A dataset admin can:

- Create, edit, and delete datasets and dataset views.
- Add or remove flow members in a dataset and assign relevant roles to them.
- Access the dataset history.
- Add or remove columns from a dataset.
- Modify the role of flow members to an **Admin**, a **Member**, or a **Viewer** as necessary without any restrictions.

> **Tip**
> 
> **Is there a way for a dataset admin to access others' datasets?** 
> 
> Yes, if the dataset admin is also a Super Admin at the account level, they will be able to access anyone's dataset, access its share settings, make themselves an admin or any role of their choice.

## Dataset member

The permissions granted to a dataset member are relatively restricted when compared to an admin and they can:

- Modify the values of a dataset.
- Add or remove rows in a dataset.
- Duplicate a dataset.
- Create, edit, and delete the dataset views they own.
- Make another flow member a dataset member or a viewer only, that is, they cannot make anyone an admin or modify or delete the already exi

### Global permission

Global permission defines the access rule for the fields in your dataset across all flows. By default, the **All fields** permission is enabled for the Lookup artifact. 

![](/migration-assets/security-settings-and-access-control/2.png)

You can disable this permission for more restrictive control and grant permission to look up only specific fields. 

1. Expand the **All fields** section and select **Lookup specific fields**.
 
2. In the popup, choose the fields you want to expose to your flows.
 
3. Click **Done**. 
 

![](/migration-assets/security-settings-and-access-control/3.png)

### Customized permission

Customized permissions provide granular control over dataset access for specific flows. Dataset Admins can set exclusive access r

nodes.label: Account governance overview
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic ✓ Enterprise

## What is account governance?

Account governance is a centralized hub, allowing [Super Admins and IAM Admins](/t/35h4ak0/user-roles-and-permissions) to audit and manage their Kissflow accounts effortlessly. This feature is pivotal for maintaining data security and protecting sensitive information within your account. A user-friendly dashboard gives you a comprehensive view of all users, groups, workflows (including processes and boards), datasets, lists, connections, and authentication keys associated with your Kissflow account.

 

## Benefits of account governance

- **Compliance and standards:** By implementing robust governance practices, you can maintain data integrity and establish a responsible and compliant operations framework.
 
- **Data protection and risk mitigation:** Account governance is crucial in safeguarding sensitive proprietary data and critical assets. By preventing risks associated with ungoverned data, you can mitigate vulnerabilities and maintain the security and integrity of your account’s data.
 
- **Activity monitoring and abnormal detection:** By tracking account activities over time, you can gain valuable insights and identify any abnormalities or suspicious behavior. This proactive approach allows for timely intervention, ensuring the security and integrity of your account.

## What can you govern?

- [Users](/t/h7hvts5/governing-users)
- [Groups](/t/60hvts2/governing-groups) 
- [Processes](/t/m1hvtsb/gove

Super admins can access AI settings for their Kissflow account using the **AI Control Center**. This feature allows administrators to enable or disable individual AI features. To access the AI Control Center, click your **Profile icon > Account governance > AI control center**.

## Managing AI access

Turn on **Account-wide AI access** switch to activate AI services for your account and then begin to enable or disable specific AI features across Process, Board, Dataset, Apps and portals, Integrations, and Account. 
 

![](/migration-assets/ai-control-center/1.gif)

> **Note:** When **Account-wide AI access** is switched back on, the previously configured settings for individual AI features will be restored.

### **Data shared with AI**

The **Data shared with AI** tag indicates that the feature uses your account or transactional data as input to provide AI-powered functionality. When a feature has this tag, relevant data from your Kissflow account may be sent to the underlying AI model (Large Language Model) to generate responses, suggestions, or insights. This tag helps administrators clearly understand which AI features rely on account data to function.

You can consider controlling  AI features listed on this page based on the following factors:

- Need of the AI feature for your account users.
- Enablement of features based on the **Data shared with AI** tag.
- Align usage with internal AI compliance policies.

> **Note:** When accou

App governance offers [Super admins and IAM admins](/t/35h4ak0/user-roles-and-permissions), a lens into how apps are used across their Kissflow account. It highlights trends in app activity, helps spot idle or high-usage apps, and surfaces potential risks like unauthorized access or external data exposure. These insights are essential for making informed decisions around app lifecycle management and data governance.

- **Active apps** - Apps that were most active during the selected time period. This helps you understand where most work is happening.
 
- **Inactive apps** - Unused apps in the selected time range. This lets you reassess whether these apps are still required or can be retired.
 
- Installed from app store
 
- **External data** - Apps connected to external systems which may involve data exchange with platforms outside Kissflow.
 

Clicking any live app in the overall view takes you to its detailed page with deeper insights. View the app’s status, creator, creation date, and last deployed details. Apart from these details, you have a lot of other sections where you can learn more about the app.

![](/migration-assets/app-governance/1.gif)

The **Users** section shows everyone who is part of the app across environments. Monitoring the app

Governing authentication keys is crucial for several reasons. First and foremost, it helps maintain the security and integrity of your Kissflow account and its data. As a [Super Admin, or an IAM Admin](/t/35h4ak0/user-roles-and-permissions), you can control who has access to your APIs and integrations by managing and governing these keys. This minimizes the risk of unauthorized access or data breaches. ### Integrations used in

This section lists the integrations that use a particular connection to transfer data between various internal and external systems. This allows you to ensure that only the intended integrations use the connection, reducing the potential for data exposure and associated risks.

nodes.snippet:

Portal admins have access to the Portal settings tab on the portal administration screen. From your portal page, click **Manage > Portal administration** > **Portal settings**. Here, you can view and customize your portal domain name, change the portal logo, apply format settings to the portal, and manage portal security.

## Portal settings

### Portal details

While setting up the portal, you would have given a name for your portal and a unique portal domain URL. You can change the portal name and domain at any time, but if another portal that's part of your Kissflow account uses a particular domain URL, you cannot use that URL. 

If you change the domain address yourself, Kissflow will automatically redirect the old domain to the new one, and all portal users will be locked out and notified. However, your former domain will become available to register by another organization. If it is claimed by someone else, your old domain will no longer redirect.

![](/migration-assets/administering-portals/4.png)

### Portal Customization

#### Portal logo and Mobile branding

Your organization’s logo is your default portal logo. It will appear on the sign in screen and certain product emails. You can also change it to a customized logo. 

We recommend you use a rectangular logo that is 240 X 36 pixels. The logo file must be less than 250 KB and in JPG, PNG, or SVG format. 
 
Also, for portal on mobile phones, provide app name and app icon under

nodes.label: Creating and managing portals
nodes.snippet:

## Creating a portal from scratch

Creating a new portal inside Kissflow is a straightforward process. As a [Super Admin](/t/35h4ak036h4gg/user-roles-permissions) of your account, you can create portals. 

1. Click the **Create** button (![](/migration-assets/creating-and-managing-portals/3.png))> **Portal** from the left navigation bar to create a new Portal.  ![](/migration-assets/creating-and-managing-portals/1.png) 
2. Enter a unique name for your portal. It should be less than 80 characters in length. Give your portal a custom domain address. External users will use the **Portal domain** URL to access your portal. You can later change this portal domain by going to portal administration. 
3. Once you have created the portal, go to **Manage** and click **Edit portal**. You will be taken to the development environment that allows you to develop the portal application, which you can share with portal users. 
  ![](/migration-assets/creating-and-managing-portals/2.png)  
 [Click here](/t/g9h9qkp/app-environments) to learn more about the different types of environments in an app builder. Once a portal is created, it will be in a draft state until you make it live by deploying the developed portal application.

To learn how to build your application from scratch, read these topics next:

1. Assigning [roles and permissions](/t/h7yzj46/application-roles).
2. Creating [data forms](/category/dataforms-in-apps), [processes](/category/processe

They can also resend verification emails to unverified users and send reset password emails when necessary. The **User Management** table offers a way to modify multiple portal users simultaneously. Click here to [learn more](/t/h7yzj46/application-roles) about defining roles within an app.

## Adding users manually

1. Navigate to **Portal administration** > **Portal users**.
 
2. Click **Add a user.**
 
3. A dialogue box will appear where:
 1. Both the First name and Email address are required.
 2. There's an option to send or skip an email invite to the new user.
4. Click **Add** to finalize the user addition.

### Inviting users

When you invite someone to join your portal, an invitation email is sent to the specified address. If the invitee accepts and there are available user licenses, they'll be automatically activated. Alternatively, you can manually activate them by selecting the user checkbox and clicking **Activate**. Activation of multiple users simultaneously is possible if adequate licenses are available. The user count is automatically updated in the **Active Users** tile. If there's a shortage of licenses, the invited user will remain inactive and unable to sign in to the portal.

nodes.label: Portal overview
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic ✓ Enterprise

## What is a portal?

[Kissflow Apps](/t/x2h9qkj/introduction-to-kissflow-apps) enable you to create complex, dynamic applications using visual tools and custom scripting. Businesses and non-professional developers can quickly create and deploy applications only Kissflow users can use.

With **Kissflow Portals**, you can create and deploy applications you can share with users outside your Kissflow account.

Portal eliminates the need to add external users to your Kissflow account. It allows you to keep business-sensitive data within your Kissflow account while creating an application for external users. It can allow external users like vendors, customers, or partners to connect and interact with applications created using Kissflow Apps. You can create applications with a wide range of possibilities by utilizing the capabilities of Kissflow Apps and integrations with third-party applications, all while maintaining the enhanced security that comes with a Kissflow account.

> Only Enterprise accounts can create portals. You can increase your Kissflow account's soft limit of **10 portals** by contacting Kissflow customer support later.

## Common use cases for portals 

You can use portals to build applications to cater to various use cases. Below are some practical ways to use it:

- A vendor portal that you can share with external vendors that are not a part of the company. 
- A helpdesk that customers of your product can use to raise and

nodes.label: Admin actions in a process
nodes.snippet:
# Admin actions in a process

 

The **Administration** section of a process gives Process admins and Data admins a single place to view every item, edit its data, and step in when an item is stuck — by reassigning, completing, or deleting it. This article covers opening the Administration section and using each of these actions.

## Open the Administration section

1. Navigate to the process.
2. Click the **More options** button ( ![More options icon](/migration-assets/admin-actions-in-a-process/2.svg) ) at the top right.
3. Select **Administration**.

This opens a list of every item in the process, showing:

- **Title** — the [item's name](/docs/admin/processes/process-settings#configure-the-form-title), as configured in the process settings.
- **Created at** — when the item was first created.
- **Created by** — the person, or guest (if submitted via a public form), who created the item.

Click the **Show/hide fields** button ( ![Show/hide fields icon](/migration-assets/admin-actions-in-a-process/4.png) ) to add other fields to the list or apply filters.

## Search for items

Use the search bar to find items quickly. Its dropdown lists every field in the process — select one, then enter a value to list only the items whose selected field matches it.

![Search bar with a field dropdown for filtering items](/migration-assets/admin-actions-in-a-process/1.png)

## Edit an item's fields

You can view and edit any field on an item, or reassign it to someone else.

1. Open the ite

nodes.label: AI-powered solution overview
nodes.snippet:
# AI-powered solution overview

 

A solution overview gives you a complete, AI-generated breakdown of a process in a readable, structured format that highlights how its different components come together to solve a business problem. It helps **process admins** and **developers** understand the overall structure and logic behind a process without having to manually explore every configuration detail.

 

## Generate a solution overview

To access the solution overview of a process, click the **More options** button at the top right and select **Solution overview**.

![More options menu with the Solution overview action](/migration-assets/ai-powered-solution-overview/1.png)

 
 **Solution overview** only appears for a published (live) process. It isn't available while you're still working on a draft version.
 

Generating the overview can take a moment since it's written fresh by AI based on your process's current design. While it's generating, you can safely navigate away or continue other work — come back later and the completed overview will be waiting for you.

![Solution overview generating for a process](/migration-assets/ai-powered-solution-overview/2.png)

## Sections of the solution overview

The generated overview is organized into five sections:

- **Overview** — a brief description of what the process is designed to do.
- **Glossary** — definitions of the terms and components used in the solution, such as connected flow, form, integration, goto, parallel branch, se

nodes.label: AI solution analyzer
nodes.snippet:
# AI solution analyzer

 

Solution analyzer is an AI-powered tool for process admins and developers who build and manage a [solution](/docs/reference/glossary/solution). It reviews your form fields, workflow steps, and permissions against the intent it infers from your process design, then flags potential issues before you take the process live.

 
 These are AI-generated findings — always review them against your process's actual requirements before acting on them.
 

 
 Solution analyzer is currently available only for no-code processes. It isn't supported inside apps or portals.
 

## When to run it

We recommend running the analyzer after you finish building your process, to check that:

- Forms have the required validations.
- Workflow steps are assigned to the right people.
- Permissions match your intent.

## Run the analyzer

1. Open your process.
2. Click **Manage** > **Edit process** to open the **Form builder**.
3. Click **Analyze** at the top right of the builder.

The analyzer runs automatically and displays the results.

![Solution analyzer running in the process builder](/migration-assets/ai-solution-analyzer/4.gif)

 
 You don't have to wait around for the analysis to finish. Close the analysis window, or click **Run analysis in background**, and you'll get a toast notification once it's complete.
 

## Review the results

If no issues are found, you'll see a success message confirming that your form, workflow, and permissions align with your process's intent

nodes.label: Associated flows for processes
nodes.snippet:
# Associated flows for processes

 

The **Associated flows** section shows how a process depends on, and is depended on by, other flows in your account — giving you a map of relationships to check before you rename fields, change permissions, or archive the process.

## View a process's associated flows

1. Open the process and click the **Manage** button at the top right.
2. Click **Associated flows**.

 
 **Associated flows** is available only to process admins and editors, and only while the process is **Live**.
 

![Process Manage panel with the Associated flows tile](/migration-assets/associated-flows-for-processes/1.png)

There are two types of associations, shown as tabs on this page: **Looked up flows** and **Connected flows**.

## Looked up flows

[Lookup fields](/docs/build/forms/field-types/lookup-field) let a process pull and display data from other flows. The **Looked up flows** tab shows both directions of that relationship.

![Looked up flows tab showing the Flows looked up to and Flows looked up from lists](/migration-assets/associated-flows-for-processes/2.png)

### Flows looked up to

Shows which flows are looking up this process to fetch and display information.

**Example**: If another process references details like **Purchase Order IDs** or **Vendor Names** from the current process, that process appears under **Flows looked up to**.

Click **Configure** next to a flow in this list to open this process's [security settings](/docs/admin/processes/security

nodes.label: Connected processes overview
nodes.snippet:
# Connected processes overview

 

In Kissflow, you can connect multiple processes so that actions in one process automatically initiate related items in another. When a work item in a source process reaches a step you've configured, a dependent item is created in the connected (destination) process — carrying over the field values you've mapped between them. This lets team managers and IT admins orchestrate and keep related workflows across departments in sync, without manual handoffs.

 

## Why connect processes

- **Automate item creation** — when a source item reaches a configured step, a dependent item is created in the connected process automatically, cutting out manual handoffs between teams.
- **Coordinate across teams** — connected processes give everyone a shared, holistic view of how work moves between departments.
- **Reduce errors** — automating the handoff removes the chance of a step being missed or details being miscommunicated when work passes from one team to another.

 
 What happens to the source item once its dependent item is completed — whether it's automatically unblocked and resumed, or handled another way — depends on how the connected step is configured. The example below describes the common setup, where the source item pauses at the connected step until its dependent item completes.
 

## Example: connecting Purchase Request and Purchase Order

Say your organization wants purchase orders to be created automatically once a purchase request is appr

nodes.label: Connecting processes
nodes.snippet:
# Connecting processes

 

Connecting processes lets a step in one process (the source) automatically create and link an item in another process (the connected process) — for example, automatically creating a Purchase Order item when a Purchase Request is approved. This article covers adding the connection, mapping fields between the two processes, and viewing connected items.

 
 For a walkthrough of why and when to connect processes, see [Connected processes - overview](/docs/admin/processes/connected-processes-overview).
 

## Connect a process

1. Click **Manage** at the top right of your process home page, then click **Edit process**.
2. In the **Workflow** tab, click the **Add** button ( ![Add step icon](/migration-assets/connecting-processes/1.png) ) at the point in the workflow where you want the connection, and select **Run a process**.

 ![Adding a Run a process step to a workflow](/migration-assets/connecting-processes/6.gif)
 

3. In the panel that opens on the right:
 - Enter a name and, optionally, a description for the step.
 - Select the process you want to connect to. Items are initiated in this process whenever the source item reaches this step.
 - **Map fields** — map fields between the connected processes so the source item's field values are inherited by the dependent item(s). You can also apply functions or expressions while mapping a field.

 ![Field mapping panel for a connected process step](/migration-assets/connecting-processes/3.png)
 

 To map fie

nodes.label: Document templates
nodes.snippet:
# Document templates

 

A document template lets you automate the generation of process-related PDFs. For example, as an HR manager managing the **Recruitment** process, you can create a document template that generates offer letters automatically once an interview is closed.

 
 You must be a [Process Admin](/docs/admin/processes/managing-process-members-and-permissions#process-admin) or a [Process Developer](/docs/admin/processes/managing-process-members-and-permissions#developer) to create a document template.
 

## Creating a document template

You can create a document template from scratch or generate a first draft with Kissflow AI.

### Create from scratch

1. Navigate to the process for which you want to create a document template.
2. Click **Manage** > **Document templates** > **Create template** > **Create from scratch**.
3. Enter a name and description for your document template.
4. Click **Create from scratch** to open the template in the editor and build it manually.

![](/migration-assets/document-templates/4.gif)

### Generate using AI

1. Navigate to the process for which you want to create a document template.
2. Click **Manage** > **Document templates** > **Create template** > **Generate using AI**.
3. Enter a document template name — for example, **Employee Offer Letter** or **Purchase Order**.
4. Select a language for the template.
5. Click **Generate**. Kissflow AI uses these inputs as a prompt to create the first draft, called **Draft 1**.

![](/migrati

nodes.label: FAQ - Connected processes
nodes.snippet:
### Why can't I see the process I want to connect to?

You can only pick from processes where you have admin access — processes you don't administer won't appear in the list.

![](/migration-assets/faq-connected-processes/1.png)

### Why can't I initiate dependent items for all the table rows in my source item at once?

Kissflow initiates up to 100 dependent items per source item, based on the number of matching table rows. If your source item's table has more than 100 rows, only 100 dependent items are created — the rest are not initiated.

### I'm getting an error while creating dependent items in the connected process. What could cause this?

A couple of things to check:

- **Unmet conditions** — the source item, or the specific table rows you expected to trigger dependent items, may not satisfy the conditions configured for creating them.
- **Assignee on the connected process' first step** — if that step's assignee is set to **Created by**, **Modified by**, or **Initiator** rather than an actual user, it can prevent items from being created correctly. Assign a real user (for example, Stephen Strange) instead.

 
 If neither of these explains the error you're seeing, contact Kissflow support with the item ID and a screenshot of the error.
 

### Can I initiate dependent items from the same source item more than once?

Not while the earlier set of dependent items is still in progress or already completed. Moving the source item back to the flow-connected step doesn't automa

nodes.label: Managing process members and permissions
nodes.snippet:
# Managing process members and permissions

 

Every member added to a process is assigned a role that determines what they can do — from initiating items to editing the workflow itself. This article covers the available roles, what each one can do, and how to add members, change their roles, and manage their permissions.

Whoever creates a process becomes its **Process Admin** by default. From there, only a Process Admin can add other members and assign them a role.

## Roles in a process

### Process Admin

A Process Admin has the highest privilege in a process. They can perform any operation on the process, including adding, removing, or modifying the roles of other members. As a Process Admin, you can:

- Edit the form and workflow.
- Assign a role to a member while adding them to the process.
- Add Process Admins, Developers, Data Admins, Report Admins, and Members at any point in the process's lifecycle.
- Remove any member, regardless of their role.
- Grant or revoke permissions for a member.
- Share reports with members or with anyone outside the process.
- [Manage process settings](/docs/admin/processes/process-settings) and [perform bulk actions and view the process administration table](/docs/admin/processes/admin-actions-in-a-process).

### Data Admin

A Data Admin can view, edit, and delete process data, and create reports.

 
 Data Admin is available on the **Enterprise** plan only.
 

As a Data Admin, you can:

- View, edit, and delete process data.
- View all

nodes.label: Process audit log
nodes.snippet:
# Process audit log

 

The audit log stores the history of all activity related to a process, and lets [Process Admins](/docs/admin/processes/managing-process-members-and-permissions#process-admin) search flow-level and item-level changes made by users in the process.

 
 The audit log is available only to Process Admins. If you don't see it, check with your Process Admin.
 

## Open the audit log

1. Open the process and click the **Manage** button.
2. Click **Audit log**.
3. Go to **Flow-level logs** to see changes to the process itself (settings, publishing, permissions), or **Item-level logs** to see changes to individual items within it.

![Process manage menu with the Audit log option](/migration-assets/process-audit-log/1.png)

The sorting, filtering, and export instructions below apply to both views. Each entry shows the following columns:

- **Audit ID** — a unique ID the system generates automatically for every recorded event.
- **Time** — when the action happened.
- **Acted by** — the user who performed the action.
- **[Event](/docs/admin/audit-log/event-categories-and-actions)** — the kind of activity that took place, for example **Process published**, **Item created**, or **Item deleted**.
- **Object** — the resource that was changed.
- **[Event category](/docs/admin/audit-log/event-categories-and-actions)** — a common name for a set of events, useful for filtering down to similar events. Examples include **Process Administration** and **Process Settings**.
- **

nodes.label: Process settings
nodes.snippet:
# Process settings

 

When you create a process, you become its Process Admin. Process settings let you control how the process behaves — from the form title and submission locking to notifications, public forms, sequence numbers, and multilingual support.

To open process settings, click the **Manage** button on the process, then **Settings**.

## General settings

The **General** tab covers the form title, submission locking, and the process's quick link.

### Configure the form title

The form title is the name given to each item as it moves through the process workflow.

1. On the **General** tab, click the **Add** button (**+**).
2. Choose the system fields or form fields you want, and arrange them in the order you want the title to read.
3. Click **Save**.

Based on this format, titles are generated automatically and shown at the top of each process form.

![](/migration-assets/process-settings/1.png)

### Lock submissions

When you're making significant changes to a process, you may want to stop members from starting new items while you work.

1. On the **General** tab, check the **Lock submissions** checkbox.
2. Click **Save**.

 
 Locking submissions restricts members from creating and submitting new items. Items already in progress continue through the workflow as normal.
 

![](/migration-assets/process-settings/2.png)

### Share the quick link

Share the process's quick link with members so they can start a new item directly. Logged-in members go straight to the

nodes.label: Publishing, editing, duplicating, archiving, and deleting a process
nodes.snippet:
# Publishing, editing, duplicating, archiving, and deleting a process

 

Process Admins and Developers manage a process through its full lifecycle — from publishing it live, to editing it later, to duplicating, archiving, or deleting it when it's no longer needed.

## Publish a process

1. Finish designing the process [form](/docs/build/forms/creating-a-form) and [workflow](/docs/build/processes/workflow-design/making-a-process-workflow).
2. Run the [AI Solution analyzer](/docs/admin/processes/ai-solution-analyzer) to check for potential errors in your design. This step is optional but recommended.
3. Click **Go live** at the bottom right of the process editor.

 ![Go live button at the bottom right of the process editor](/migration-assets/publishing-editing-duplicating-archiving-and-deleting-a-process/1.png)

 
 Going live publishes the process in one step — all of your latest changes to the form, workflow, and permissions go live together.
 

 
 The previous version of a process can't be restored once you publish. Review your changes carefully before clicking **Go live**.
 

Changes you make in the process editor are saved automatically as you work. If you've made unwanted changes before publishing, you can ask another Process Admin to open the process and [discard your changes while they take over editing](#edit-a-process).

 
 Changing a field's type isn't recommended once the field already has data in it. If the field existed before the process's last publish, Kissflow

nodes.label: Reviewing publish changes
nodes.snippet:
# Reviewing publish changes

 

The **Review changes** feature gives Process admins and Developers complete visibility into every update made to a process before it goes live. Instead of manually comparing versions or tracking edits across sessions, you get a clear, consolidated summary of what changed.

 

## Open Review changes

Inside the process editor, you can review changes in two ways:

1. **While editing** — Click **Review changes** at the top-right corner at any time during your editing session, without needing to publish.
2. **Before publishing** — Click **Go live** at the bottom-right, then select **Review changes** in the confirmation pop-up.

![Go live confirmation pop-up with the option to review changes before publishing](/migration-assets/reviewing-publish-changes/2.gif)

## What the summary shows

The **Review changes** page lists everything that's been **added**, **deleted**, or **modified** in your form, workflow, and permissions since the last published version.

![Review changes page showing a consolidated summary of form, workflow, and permission changes](/migration-assets/reviewing-publish-changes/1.png)

 
 On the Enterprise plan, complex changes are also explained in plain language using **AI-powered summaries**.
 

## Discard changes

If some updates are no longer needed, click **Discard changes** on the **Review changes** page. This removes all draft updates made after the last published version and restores the process to its previously published s

nodes.label: Security settings for flows
nodes.snippet:
# Security settings for flows

 

Flow Admins can control how their process, board, dataset, or dataform shares data with other flows in your Kissflow account. The **Security** page gives you two layers of control — an account-wide default and per-flow overrides — plus a set of access controls for printing, downloading, commenting, watching, and impersonation.

## Access security settings

1. Go to the process, board, dataset, or dataform you want to manage.
2. Click **Manage** > **Security**.

![Manage menu open with the Security option highlighted](/migration-assets/security-settings-for-flows/1.png)

## Global permissions

Global permissions are the default access rule that applies to every flow in your account. Today, **Lookup** is the only permission with a true global toggle — an **All flows** setting that's turned on by default, so any flow in your account can look up this flow's data unless you turn it off or override it for a specific flow.

 
 Integration access doesn't have a global setting. It must be granted to each flow individually — see [Customized permissions](#customized-permissions) below.
 

### Turn off global lookup access

1. On the **Security** page, find the **Global permissions** section.
2. Clear the **Lookup** checkbox for **All flows**.

Unchecking it means no flow in the platform can look up this flow's data by default — you'd need to grant access to specific flows individually instead.

 
 Turning off global Lookup access restricts it across eve

nodes.label: Configuring Azure AD directory sync
nodes.snippet:
As a [Super Admin, or an IAM Admin](/t/35h4ak0), you can synchronize user and group information from your third-party directory accounts to Kissflow using the **Identity Provider** feature under **User Provisioning** in Kissflow. We currently support user provisioning from Google Workspace and Microsoft Azure Active Directory.

An account can have only one user provisioning service enabled at a time, so if you are currently using SCIM, we recommend you contact our customer support team to help you disable it.

## Prerequisites

- An Azure AD subscription.
- Administrator access in the Azure and Kissflow accounts used for the directory sync.

> **Note**: 
> If your Administrator access is revoked in the future or your credentials do not work anymore, all the scheduled syncs will be disabled until you sign in back to your Azure AD account.

## Connecting to Azure AD

1. Access your Kissflow account on your web browser and click your profile picture > **Account Administration** > **User provisioning** > **Identity providers** > **Configure.**
2. Click **Connect Azure AD** on the **Select your Identity Provider** page.
3. Sign in to your **Microsoft Azure AD** account with your credentials.
 1. Your connection might fail if you aren’t an Administrator in your Azure AD account or if the account you tried to sign in wasn’t an Azure AD account.
4. Select the **Consent on behalf of your organization** checkbox to give your consent to let Kissflow access the information required for t

nodes.label: Configuring Google Workspace directory sync
nodes.snippet:
You can add users and synchronize user information from your third-party directory accounts to Kissflow using the **Identity Provider** feature under **User Provisioning** in Kissflow. We currently support user provisioning from Google Workspace (formerly called G Suite) and via [SCIM](/t/p8h4ak436h4gg/managing-members-with-scim-provisioning).

An account can have only one user provisioning service enabled at a time, so if you are currently using SCIM, we recommend you contact our customer support team to help you disable it.

## Prerequisites

Here are a few things you must know before you attempt to connect to Google Workspace:

1. You must be a [Super Admin, or an IAM Admin](/t/35h4ak036h4gg/user-roles-permissions) in your Kissflow account.
2. You must be an Administrator in your Google Workspace account with the following permissions:
 - Organization: Read
 - Organization unit: Read
 - Users: Read
 - Groups: Read
 - Domain Settings
 - Domain management
 - Schema Management: Schema Read

If you are unsure whether you have the required permissions in your Google Workspace account, check this [link](https://support.google.com/a/answer/2405986?hl=en) to learn more about your role and privileges. Also, if your Administrator access is revoked in the future or your credentials do not work anymore, all the scheduled syncs will be disabled until you sign in back to your Google Workspace account.

## Connecting to Google Workspace

1. Access your Kissflow account on your web browse

nodes.label: Directory sync overview
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic ✓ Enterprise

As a [Super Admin, or an IAM Admin](/t/35h4ak0), you can navigate to your **Identity provider** settings under **Account Administration** > **User provisioning** > **Directory sync** to manage and perform other actions related to your configuration. You can track the progress of ongoing sync, its current status, sync history, and perform actions like edit, delete, or disable configuration in this section. 

![](/migration-assets/directory-sync-overview/1.png)

As mentioned earlier, Kissflow supports both manual and scheduled syncs. Click **Sync now** to trigger sync manually anytime.

## Disabling configuration

- Access the configuration and click the **More options** button (**...**) > **Disable**
- Read the alert message displayed on the popup window and click **Disable** if you would like to suspend the configuration temporarily. You can always enable it later when you want to.

## Deleting configuration

- Access the configuration and click the **More options** button (**...**) > **Delete configuration**
- Read the alert message displayed on the popup window and click **Delete** if you would like to delete the configuration permanently. Please note that you have to create the configuration from scratch if you need it back in the future.

## Managing sync history

Access the configuration and click **More options** > **Sync history** or click the **View sync history** link present beside the last synced status information.
