Secondary c

nodes.label: Introduction to Kissflow Apps
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans: ✕ Basic ✓ Work Platform Enterprise

## What is a Kissflow App?

Kissflow Apps uses a low-code app development approach that requires little to no coding in order to create applications. It uses visual tools and custom scripting to develop and deliver complex applications at scale.

You can utilize the Kissflow Apps framework to create dynamic user applications and workflows on top of Kissflow. You can use them to create custom dashboard views for Kissflow Processes and Boards, build tight integrations, or display layouts from reports and data forms in Kissflow.

Based on specified user roles, users can then interact with these apps inside their Kissflow Work Platform. Your apps can be purely client-side apps or make requests to remote resources.

> **Note:** Please note that the App builder is available only for the **Kissflow Work Platform Enterprise** plan.

### How's it different from traditional application development?

Rather than using traditional coding languages such as Java, C++, Python, etc., Kissflow's app platform has drag-and-drop features and pre-configured components that allow for application development with little or no coding. You can also use various flavors of JavaScript to add custom features to your application. This not only makes application development easier and more accessible to non-professional developers but also speeds up the overall development significantly.

Apart from your primary Kissflow account, you'll also have access t

nodes.label: Launching Kissflow Apps
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans: ✕ Basic ✓ Work Platform Enterprise

## Creating Kissflow Apps

Every **Kissflow Enterprise account** will have two **environments** to build and deploy apps to live.

- **Development environment** - You'll have an app builder to create your app from scratch. All apps can only be built in this environment. You'll need a separate **Super Admin** or **User** permission to access this environment. A development environment can be accessed by adding the prefix "dev-" before your Kissflow account domain. For example, **[dev-starkcorp.kissflow.com](http://dev-starkcorp.kissflow.com)** will be the URL of your account's development environment.
- **Production environment** - This is the live environment where you and other app users can access the final app. This is always your account domain, i.e., in our above example, **[starkcorp.kissflow.com](http://starkcorp.kissflow.com)**.

If you require a testing environment to test the app before deploying it to production, you can request our support for the additional environment while setting up your Kissflow account.

All Kissflow users will have the ability to build apps provided they have _Super Admin or User_ permissions in these environments.Creating and managing an app

1. Sign in to Kissflow.
 
2. Click the **Create** button in the left navigation bar and click **App**. Alternatively, click the **Explore** button > + **Create** to open the **Create from scratch** menu.
3. Enter the app name, description and click *

nodes.label: Model viewer
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic ✓ Enterprise

A model viewer in Apps is similar to an Entity-relationship (ER) model widely used in DBMS (Database Management System). An ER model is used to create data models using the data from the database. There can be relationships established between each entity in the ER model. 

Unlike an ER model, the model viewer is a visualization of all the [processes](/t/g9yzjcd/process-form-overview), [boards](/t/g9yzjal/boards-overview), and [dataforms](/t/m1h9q1y18h4g5/data-forms-overview) created within the app and the relationship between them. Any changes to the data model of the model viewer cannot be made directly in the model viewer. Rather it’s a reflection of changes made in the forms created and looked up using the [Lookup field](/t/g9h4azr/lookup-field).

For example, below is the model viewer of an Employee Management app.

![](/migration-assets/model-viewer/2.png)

Here, forms such as Employee, Business Unit, Department, Teams etc., are the entities. Based on the relationship established between them, entities are linked to one another.

To open the model viewer in an app,

1. Open an app. Click **Manage > Edit app**.
2. In the left navigation panel, click **Data > Model viewer**.![](/migration-assets/model-viewer/1.png)

nodes.label: Multilingual settings in Apps
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic ✓ Enterprise

Multilingual settings allow admins for Kissflow apps to customize their apps to be displayed in languages supported by the platform.

## What are Multilingual settings?

As an app admin, if you have developed an app in English, all the fields and label names will be displayed in English once you deploy the app. However, during development, you can enable multiple languages for the app. The end users can view the app fields in the selected language if they match the one set up for their profile.Language precedences work as follows:

First precedence: Profile language under a user’s **profile >** **My settings**Second precedence: The language selected for all users under account-level settings by the account admin.

For example, if an app is developed in English and the admin has enabled Chinese, Japanese, and Korean under the Multilingual settings, when a user who has selected Japanese as their preferred language views the app, it will be displayed in Japanese for them. The app will be displayed in its original language if both profile and account-level languages are unselected.

## How does it work?

The multilingual settings feature offers two ways in which app admins can translate the fields and label data.

- Manual translation
- Automatic translation using AI

**Follow the below steps to configure Multilingual Settings**

1. Click **Manage > Edit app**, go to **App settings**, and click **Multilingual settings**.
2. Enable the **Conf

There are two types of notifications available within Kissflow Apps:

2. **App management notifications** - These are app-level notifications that are specifically designed for app developers and intended to provide them with important updates and notifications related to managing apps. They can only be toggled on or off.
 

> **Note**:

If you have made any changes to the notifications, you must re-deploy the app for the changes to reflect in the production environment.

✕ Basic ✓ EnterpriseResource Manager in Apps is a file management system that allows you to store, organize, and quickly access image files from within Apps. Resource manager offers a resource library where you can consolidate all the image resources required to build an enterprise application in the same space, facilitating consistency and collaboration within a team.With the resource manager, you can store organization-specific images to build highly customized landing pages for your enterprise application. In addition, the resources added to the resource library can also be used for icons and background images in the page builder.To view the resource library in the app builder:

Open an app.Click **Manage > Edit app.**Go to the left navigation panel, click **Data > Resources**.![](/migration-assets/working-with-resource-manager-in-apps/1.png)

>

- It offers search and sort functions to help you locate files quickly and efficiently.

You can archive and delete a board when you no longer need it. A board can only be deleted after it is archived. When you archive a board, all the contents will no longer be accessible, and no one can add new items to it. 

## Archiving and unarchiving a board

To archive a board, click the **More options** button (![](/migration-assets/archiving-deleting-and-duplicating-a-board/2.png)) at the top right > **Archive.**

However, if you are an admin of the board, you can access the audit log for the board and unarchive or delete it.

To find the archived boards you once managed, click **Explore** from the left panel and select the **Managed by me** tab at the top. To view the archived boards managed by others, select the **Others** tab. Select the **Boards** tab in the left navigation panel and select the status as **Archived**.

![](/migration-assets/archiving-deleting-and-duplicating-a-board/1.png)

### [Unarchiving a b](/t/y4h9qtb/publishing-editing-duplicating-archiving-and-deleting-a-process#unarchiving-a-process)oard

To unarchive a board you managed, click **Explore** > **Managed by me** > **Board** > **Status - Archived**. To unarchive other boards, click **Explore** > **Others** > **Board** > **Status - Archived**. Hover over a board and click the **Unarchive** button ( ![](/migration-assets/archiving-deleting-and-duplicating-a-board/3.png) ) to make the board available again.

nodes.label: Associated flows for boards
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

  
The **Associated flows** section helps you understand how your board interacts with other flows. They offer a visual map of dependencies, making it easier to manage changes.

Click the **Manage** button at the top right and click the **Associated flows** button. 
 

![](/migration-assets/associated-flows-for-boards/1.png)

  
There are two types of associations: **Looked up flows** and **Connected flows**.

## 1\. Looked up flows

[Lookup fields](/t/g9h4azr/lookup-field) allow a board to pull and display data from other flows. 
 

![](/migration-assets/associated-flows-for-boards/2.png)

Under **Looked up flows**, you can view these relationships:

### a. Flows looked up to

This section shows which flows are looking up this board to fetch and display information.

**Example**: If another board needs to reference details like **IT asset availability** or **Vendor Names** from the current board, it would appear under **Flows looked up to**.

### b. Flows looked up from

This section displays the flows that the current board is looking up.

**Example**: If your **IT Asset approval** board is pulling **Asset Types** or **Asset details** from a master dataset, the master list flow will appear under **Flows looked up from**.

You can [configure lookup](/t/83ymdnx/security-settings-for-flows) settings directly from this page if you need to update or remove associations.

nodes.label: Board audit log
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

The [audit log](/t/h7h4akc/admin-history) section stores the history of all the activities related to your board. It allows the [board admins](/t/m1h9qls/managing-members-and-roles-in-a-board#admin) to search and find the flow-level and item-level changes made by users in your board. To view your audit log, click the **Manage** button > **Audit log**. 
 

![](/migration-assets/board-audit-log/2.png)

You can get the following details of [events](/t/60ytnn6/event-categories-and-actions) recorded as part of your board from the audit log:

- **Audit ID**: This is a unique ID generated automatically by the system for every event that's recorded as part of the board history. 
  
- **Timestamp**: The exact time when a specific action happened or was performed. 
  
- **Actor**: The user who performed the action. 
  
- **Action**: The kind of activity or action that had taken place. Examples include **Board published**_,_ **Item created**_,_ **Item deleted**_,_ etc. 
  
- **Object**: The object or the underlying resource that is being changed. 
  
- **Event category**: Common name for an event that you can use to filter down to similar events. Examples: Board administration, Item actions, etc. 
  
- **Platform**: The device or OS from which the event was recorded. 
  
- **IP**: The IP address from which the event was recorded. 
  
- **Batch ID**: This is a unique ID generated automatically by the system for a group of events that are related to

Board admins and members can monitor overall team performance, upcoming deadlines, and workload trends using the following default widgets:

- **AI insights**: AI analyzes your workflow data to uncover patterns and trends that aren’t easily visible in standard reports. These insights highlight opportunities for improvement, helping you make informed decisions and address bottlenecks.
 
- **Workload distribution**: Visualizes how tasks are distributed across team members, helping identify over- or under-assigned workloads.
 
- Due within 7 days
 
- **Overdue in last 30 days**: 
 
 - Tracks how often items exceed their due dates. 
 
 - Shows the number of overdue items compared to all items with defined due dates.
 
- Priority distribution
 

## My dashboard

As a board user (all board roles), you can track your personal progress and tasks with the widgets below.

- **Notes awaiting my action**: Displays mentioned notes that require your response (reply or reaction).
 
- Overdue tasks in last 30 days
 
- Due within 48 hours
 
- Items I'm watching

Apart from the predefined default widgets, the dashboard sugge

The **Files** panel acts as a centralized hub for all attachments within a board. Instead of scrolling through long board forms to find receipts, invoices, or images, you can access them all from a single sidebar. The panel is available within the board workflow, regardless of an item's status.

To open the panel, 

1. Click **Files**, on the right-hand sidebar inside any board item.
 
2. The panel will slide out, displaying all attachments grouped by their respective sections.
 

![](/migration-assets/board-files-panel/3.gif)

### Previewing attachments

You can review attachments without leaving the current page or downloading files.

1. Click directly on any file name (for example, _Screenshot_) within the list.
 
2. This opens the built-in viewer overlay.
 
3. From here, you can download, print, open the attachment in a new tab, zoom in or out, rotate, and cycle through multiple attachments using the navigation arrows.
 

### Locating a file in the form

If you need to see the context of an attachment (for example, checking which specific expense row a receipt belongs to):

1. Hover over the file in the **Files** panel.
 
2. Click the **View field in form** (![](/migration-assets/board-files-panel/1.png) ) button next to the file name.
 
3. The main form on the left will automatically scroll and highlight the exact field where that file is attached.
 

![](/migration-assets/board-files-panel/4.gif)

✕ Basic ✓ EnterpriseYou can modify how your board functions by changing specific options. To configure or change settings for a board form, click the **Settings** tab of your board form.

## General settings

On the **General** tab, you can configure some of the main parameters of your board form. You can change the name and item type, as well as configure how titles appear on each board form:

- **Item type** - the type of items that are managed in the Board. You can rename the item type here.
- **ID prefix** - The prefix for Item IDs generated in this system. For example, if you use IT, items will be numbered IT-001, IT-002, and so on.

![](/migration-assets/board-settings/1.png)

To configure form titles, click the **Add button (+)** and arrange default system fields or form fields in the order you want, and then click **Save**. Based on this format, titles are auto-generated and are displayed on top of each board form.![](/migration-assets/board-settings/2.png)

## Optional settings

### Priority

Priority indicates the urgency to resolve an item. Setting a priority allows you to manage and track your items efficiently.For example, if there are items that are of a higher priority, you can set their priority to **High** or **Critical**. Critical items are easily identifiable and therefore easier to track.On the **Optional** **settings** tab, you can turn on or off priority for the entire board form.![](/migration-assets/board-s

nodes.label: Boards overview
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

One of the main factors contributing to an organization's growth and success is efficient work management. Every department or part of an organization relies on completing short-term and long-term tasks, solving problems, managing leads, following up on transactions, fulfilling requests, and more. While all these operations happen in separate modules, localized or online, tracking these operations from their respective modules might not seem ideal for stakeholders. If one could track different projects, cases, and operations from a single platform, that would result in KPIs achieved and tasks completed on time, which in turn increases productivity. This is what Kissflow Board aims to achieve. 

With **Kissflow Boards**, you can create highly adaptable workflows that let you keep track of information and manage work efficiently. You can create work items in Boards and monitor their progress. You can configure a form with various types of fields to collect information regarding these items and create custom steps or statuses according to the nature of your work. An example of a board is a helpdesk. Tickets raised in the help desk become cases or items. As a Board admin, you can create tickets, set custom statuses, assign tickets to the right users, and collaborate to resolve them. Similarly, a case can be a bug, task, service request, or incident, depending on how you set up your board.

## Features

Here’s an overview of all that you can

nodes.label: Boards overview
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic ✓ Enterprise

 Boards help you manage highly adaptable workflows that involve interactions between people and information. Similar to other flows, a form stores the data for an item and all steps.

An example of a board is a helpdesk. Tickets raised on a help desk become items. As a Board admin, you can create tickets, set custom statuses, assign tickets to the right users, and collaborate to resolve them. Similarly, an item can also be a bug, task, service request, or incident, depending on how you set up your board.

#### Features

Here’s an overview of all that you can do using boards in Kissflow:

- [Build custom workflows](/t/35h9qlyx1h4gb/building-boards) to create, collaborate, and resolve items.
- Create [custom forms](/t/h7h9qlxx1h4gb/configuring-a-board-form) with advanced fields that support conditional visibility, validations, and expressions.
- Configure and manage [custom statuses](/t/60h9ql7x1h4gb/configuring-board-settings#configuring-statuses) for workflows.
- [Navigate](/t/60h9qlmx1h4gb/using-board-layouts) between items with ease using list and board views.
- Collaborate with other members on items using [notes](/t/35h9qllx1h4gb/note-transitions-and-activity-boards#notes).
- View [item transitions](/t/35h9qllx1h4gb/note-transitions-and-activity-boards#transitions) and [activity logs](/t/35h9qllx1h4gb/note-transitions-and-activity-boards#activity) for better management of priority items.
- Use default and custom filters that support

nodes.label: Building a board
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

Boards help you manage work that covers a wide spectrum, including project tasks, issue tracking, status tracking, help desks, service desks, service request management, employee onboarding, incident management, bug tracking, and many others, all on a single platform.Getting started with a board is super easy. There are just a few steps that should be completed, and you will be all set to manage your board:

- Create a new board.
- Add new members to your board.
- Customize the board form.
- Set up custom statuses that your workflow requires.
- Determine whether a priority field is required.

## Creating a new board

Boards can be built from scratch so that you can customize them the way you want. Before starting, you should check if you have the right permissions to create boards. If you are an admin newly starting on an account, you should have these permissions already; else, you should contact an [Account Admin](/t/m1h9qls/managing-members-and-roles-boards).

1. On the left navigation, click **Explore.** 
  
2. On the **Explorer** page, click **Create.** Alternatively, you can go to the **Template gallery** and install one of the predefined templates. 
  
3. Alternatively, you can create a board by selecting Create > Board from the left navigation.
4. On the **Create a board** page, select from one of the two options:
 
 1. Build with AI
 
 2. Create from scratch
 

![](/migration-assets/building-a-board/1.png)

### Create from scrat

nodes.label: Charts in boards
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

You can generate reports as charts. There are ten types of charts you can choose from: auto, area stacked, area, horizontal bars, vertical bars, combo, doughnut, line, pie, and bubble.

## Creating a chart

1. To create a chart, click the **Reports** button at the top right. 
 
2. Click **\+ Create report.**
 
3. Provide a name for the chart, select **Chart** as the report type**,** and click **Create**.
 
 ![](/migration-assets/charts-in-boards/2.png)
 
4. Choose the type of chart you want to create. Select **Auto** when you’re unsure what type of chart to choose, and Kissflow will create the best one for you.

## Types of charts

- **Area charts** function similar to a line chart, but with the areas below the lines filled in. You can use this chart to show how values develop over time.
- **Area stacked chart** is an area chart that includes a stack of multiple areas. It can be used instead of a line graph with multiple graphs to compare development or trend over time.![](/migration-assets/charts-in-boards/1.png) 
- **Horizontal bar charts** avoid clutter when one data point is longer than the others or if you have more than ten items to compare. You can use bar charts to display negative numbers.![](/migration-assets/charts-in-boards/5.png) 
- **Vertical bar charts** are used to show a comparison among different items, or they can show a comparison of items over time.
- **Line charts** use lines to represent changes to data over a peri

nodes.label: Configuring a board form
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

Every item on a board begins with a form. A [form](/category/forms-and-expressions) is where you can record all the data for an item. You can customize a board form to include fields to capture the essential information for your workflow. You can also set the visibility conditions for sections, fields, and tables across different statuses. Overall, these features offer you more control over the item and how users interact with various fields of the item across statuses.To customize the board form, click the **Manage** button at the top right corner > **Edit form.**The following fields are system fields and cannot be modified or deleted.

**Field**

**Description**

Assignee

The item is assigned to this person. You can only assign items to users who are members of the board.

Priority

This field indicates the level of urgency to close an item. You can prioritize either **Low**, **Medium**, **High**, or **Critical**. When creating a new item, the default priority is set to **Low**. 

> **Note:**
> 
> You can control the visibility of this field and its function by turning on/off priority on the [Settings](/t/60h9ql7/configuring-board-settings#priority) page.

Start date

An item will start on this date and time

Due date

An item must be closed by this date and time.

Requester

The person who has requested an item. This is not necessarily the person who has created the item on the board. For instance, an item can be requested by a user

nodes.label: Configuring statuses
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

Statuses indicate the progress of an item in a board. To configure or change statuses for a board, click the **Manage** button > **Edit workflow.** On the **Workflow settings** tab, you can view all the default statuses in use. Statuses are grouped into categories that correspond to typical phases in a workflow.There are five default categories for statuses. You can create custom statuses under each of these categories except Reopened.

**Category**

**Description**

**Not started**

The statuses under this category are assigned to all new items that have not yet progressed. 

> **Note:**When you create a new item, it defaults to the first status under this category.

**In progress**

The statuses under this category are typically assigned to all items that are in progress.

**Done**

This is an optional category. You can use this category for use cases where you need to temporarily park items that are resolved but need to be validated before closure.

**Closed**

The statuses under this category are assigned to items that don’t need any further action.

**Reopened**

This is an optional category. This is used to categorize items that are reopened after closure. You cannot create new custom statuses under this category.

### [Creating a custom status](/t/60h9ql7/configuring-your-board-settings#creating-a-custom-status)

In addition to the default statuses under a category, you can create custom statuses according to your workflow. Custom

nodes.label: Configuring your board settings
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

If you are an admin of a board, you can configure how your board behaves by making changes to certain settings.To configure or change settings for a board, click the **Manage** button at the top right corner > **Settings.**

## General settings

On the **General** tab, you can configure some of the main parameters of your board. You can change the item type and ID prefix, as well as configure how titles appear on items:

- **Item type** - The type of items that are managed in the board. You can rename the item type here.
- **ID prefix** - The prefix that gets attached to the item IDs.
- **Configure form title** - Specify a format that is used to auto-generate titles for each item.

### Configuring form titles

To configure form titles, click the **Add** button (+), arrange default system fields or form fields in the order you want, and then click **Save**. Based on this format, titles are auto-generated and are displayed on top of each item.![](/migration-assets/configuring-your-board-settings/2.png)

## Notification settings

You can create and customize notifications to stay informed about important updates or ensure timely communication within your team. Learn more about [board notifications](/t/60y68hm/board-notifications).

## Optional settings

### Priority

Priority indicates the urgency to resolve an item. Setting a priority allows you to manage and track your items efficiently.For example, if there are items that are of a higher

nodes.label: Connecting boards with other boards and processes - Overview
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic ✓ Enterprise

 

In Kissflow, you can connect a parent board to another board or process to automate workflows. A status change in a parent board's item can trigger actions in the linked board or process. 
 
This enhances overall efficiency and simplifies workflow management for team managers and IT admins who need to connect and orchestrate multiple workflows and gain complete visibility into them.

## Benefits

1. **Automate item creation**: When a work item in a parent/source board reaches a certain status, new items are automatically created in the connected boards/processes. This reduces manual effort and ensures timely task creation.
 
2. **Improve coordination**: By creating related/dependent items across different flows, teams can better coordinate their efforts and maintain a holistic view of the project’s progress.
 
3. **Reduce errors**: Automated item creation minimizes the risk of overlooking important tasks or miscommunication between teams.
 

## Use case: Connecting Employee onboarding and IT asset approval boards

Let’s say you want to create an IT asset request when a new hire accepts their job offer letter. Offer letter approvals are tracked in the **Employee onboarding** board, which has a status named **Offer accepted**. You can specifically connect the **Offer accepted** status to the **IT asset approva**l board, where IT asset requests are made. So, whenever you move an item to the **Offer accepted** status, one or more IT asset

By connecting a parent board with another board/process, you can interconnect workflows. 
 
A change in status in a parent board's item can act as a trigger to initiate an action in another board or process. This allows you to automate item creation and orchestrate the item’s end-to-end lifecycle across those flows. Here’s how you can do it:

1. Click the **Manage** button at the top right corner, and click **Edit workflow**. The **Workflow settings** modal will open up.

 - Select which board/process you want to connect to the status and create dependent items in it.
 
 - **Map fields**: You can map fields between the connected flows so that the source item’s field values are inherited by the dependent item(s). You can map them either manually or using AI. Additionally, you can add functions while mapping the fields.
 
 ![](/migration-assets/connecting-boards-with-other-boards-and-processes/1.png)
 
 To do table-to-table field mapping between the current board and connected flow, click **Add a table**, select the table, and add the fields you want to map. 
 
 ![](/migration-assets/connecting-boards-with-other-boards-and-processes/3.gif)

nodes.label: Creating and using board views in apps
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic ✓ Enterprise

## Overview

Board views ensure alignment within your team by allowing you to focus on specific sets of data and share them effectively with your team members. 

Let’s say you have a Product release board where one team is focused on feature development while another handles feature testing. With board views, each team can create a filtered view that displays only the relevant items. 

As an app admin or a developer, you can create and save board views in [kanban](/t/60h9qlm/using-board-layouts#kanban), [list](/t/60h9qlm/using-board-layouts#list), [matrix](/t/60h9qlm/using-board-layouts#matrix), and [timeline](/t/60h9qlm/using-board-layouts#timeline) layouts and share them with the desired app roles. This makes it easier to work collaboratively with better context.

## Creating views

To create a new view,

- Click **Views** > **\+ New view.**
 
- Provide a view name and select the desired layout for your view.
 
 ![](/migration-assets/creating-and-using-board-views-in-apps/3.gif)
 
- Sort based on fields.
- Select the fields you want to display on the items under the **LAYOUT FIELDS** section. Certain system fields cannot be removed as they are required to remain visible.  
- Add simple and advanced filter conditions under the **FILTER** section to track specific items on your unique views. 

> **Note:**
> 
> You cannot access the items while configuring the view.

Click the **Save** button at the top after you’re done with the configur

nodes.label: FAQ - Connected boards
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic ✓ Enterprise

1. ### Why am I not able to unlock a source item?
 
 Only the board admins can unlock a source item. 
  
2. ### Why am I not able to see the board I want to connect to?
 
 ![](/migration-assets/faq-connected-boards/1.png) 
 You can see only the boards where you have admin access. 
  
3. ### Why can't I create dependent items for all the table entries in my source item at once?
 
 Based on the number of table entries/rows in the source item, you can create up to 100 dependent items in the connected board.  
 
 If the number of table entries in the source table is more than 100, you can still create only 100 dependent items.
4. ### I am getting an error while creating items in the connected board. What could cause this?
 
 It can be due to one of the below reasons:
 - The source item did not meet the set conditions.
 
 - **Simultaneous bulk updates**: In the list layout, if you try to bulk-update items to a flow-connected status, while another bulk-update to the same status is already in progress.
 
5. ### What happens if the table entries do not meet the conditions for creating dependent items?
 
 - If no table entries meet the set condition, the source item will still move to the flow-connected status and remain unlocked even if the status has a lock configuration.  
  
 - If at least one table entry meets the condition, the source item will move to the flow-connected status and get locked if the status has a lock configuration. In this

nodes.label: Managing board roles and permissions
nodes.snippet:

As an app owner, you will have default access to the board form and the ability to change many settings.

As an admin of your board, you can manage the roles and permissions of the board form. This way, you can specify which roles can access the board form, whether accessed internally (in-app) or from other external applications, such as other Kissflow apps or [portal apps](/t/m1h4azh/portal-overview). 

To manage roles and permissions,

1. Access the **board** form > click **Share**.
 
2. On the Share page, click **\+ Assign roles** to select and add members. The hierarchy of roles includes external app roles listed below and in-app roles, along with their respective app names.
 
3. Add roles and assign **Read-only**, **Initiate**, **Edit**, or **Manage** permissions, and click confirm.
 
 ![](/migration-assets/managing-board-roles-and-permissions/2.gif)
 
 > **Note**:
 > 
 > External app roles cannot be provisioned with the **Manage** permission.
 
4. Click the **Delete** button beside the role names in the **Share** page to remove roles from a board form. The role cannot access or view the contents of the board form after that.
 
5. You can also change the permissions for roles after they are added. For example, if you want to change the role of a member from **Initiate** to **Manage**, use the radio button.
 
 ![](/migration-assets/managing-board-roles-and-permissions/1.png)
 

#### Roles and permissions

You can assign roles to memb

nodes.label: Managing members and roles in a board
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

## Roles and permissions

You can manage the members in your board by assigning different roles and permissions to their profiles based on the responsibilities mapped with them in a specific board.Kissflow offers the following roles for members in a board:

### Admin

A Board Admin has the highest set of privileges in a board. They can,

- Create, modify, and delete items.
- Create and manage views.
- Add and modify notes.
- Modify, and manage the settings of a board.
- Add or remove members.
- Perform bulk actions.
- Access the audit log.
- View transitions and activity.
- Generate custom reports.

> **Tip:**
> 
> **Admins can modify the role of any member in a board.** 
> 
> An admin can make anyone in the board, an admin, member, initiator, viewer, or restricted member. They can also downgrade someone's role from an **Admin** to a **Restricted member** based on their requirements in the board.

### Member

A board member falls next in the hierarchy following the admin**,** and they can,

- Create, modify, and delete items.
- Create and manage views.
- Add and modify notes.
- View transitions and activity.

There are a few things that a member cannot do in a board:

- They cannot duplicate or delete a board.
- They can access only the reports that are shared with them.

### Initiator

An initiator is someone who is a part of the board but has a limited set of privileges and responsibilities. They can only,

- Create new items.
- Update

nodes.label: Overview of custom reports in boards
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

Reports help you derive insights into how your board is functioning. You can generate customized reports to analyze trends and various metrics about your workflow.

You can access custom reports by clicking the **Reports** button in the upper-right corner of the board and then clicking the **Custom reports** tab. Only [admins](/t/m1h9qls/managing-members-and-roles-boards) can create reports. Members and initiators can only access reports admins have shared with them.

## Creating a new report

To create a new report, click **\+ Create report**. Choose the type of report, provide a name for the report, and click **Create**.

### Types of reports

- [Tabular reports](/t/h7h9qld/tabular-reports-boards) display information in columns and rows, like a spreadsheet. 
- [Charts](/t/p8h9qlw/charts-boards) display data graphically in one of ten chart types, including area, bar, doughnut, line, pie, and bubble chart.
- [Pivot tables](/t/35h9ql2/pivot-tables-boards) can be used to summarize large quantities of data quickly. You can convert columns into rows and rows into columns.![](/migration-assets/overview-of-custom-reports-in-boards/1.png) 

### The All Items report

The first time you open the **Reports** page, you’ll see an auto-generated report called _<board\_name> All Items_. This is a tabular report that shows every item in your board. It cannot be deleted or renamed, but you can configure it to change how data is displayed.

### Findin

nodes.label: Pivot tables in boards
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

You can generate pivot tables to summarize, arrange, and compare a large amount of data related to your board. Pivot tables allow you to easily interchange fields, rows, and columns. The representation of data in a pivot table reorients according to changes you make.

## Creating a pivot table

1. To create a pivot table, click the **Reports** tab on your board page.
 
2. Click **\+ Create report**.
 
3. Provide a name, select **Pivot** as the report type**,** and click **Create**.
 

![](/migration-assets/pivot-tables-in-boards/1.png)

## Configuring a pivot table

To configure the pivot table, start by choosing the fields you want to display from the right panel. In the field selection click the **\+ Add fields** button to add the required fields.

Choosing fields

There are two types of fields to choose from.

1. **Custom fields** - These are fields you created on your board form and are unique to your board.
 
2. **System fields** - These are system-generated fields that are created by default for a board such as Title, Created by, Modified by, Created at, Modified at, Flow name, DocVersion, Item ID, Assignee, Status, Priority, Start date, Due date, Requester, Estimated time, Watchers, and Resolution time.
 

> **Note:**
> 
> The resolution time of boards will be shown in seconds.

#### Columns

Add fields as columns from your board form. The order of your selection creates the column hierarchy.

You can include up to 10 fields as co

nodes.snippet:

## Introduction

Board admins can manage the sharing of their flow data with other flows within the Kissflow platform through the security settings page. This provides a more robust, flexible, and granular approach to controlling access to boards.

The security settings page allows you to manage permissions for lookup fields:

## Accessing security settings

To manage the security settings for a specific flow, follow these steps:

1. Go to the board you want to manage.
 
2. Click the **Manage** button > **Security.**
 
 ![](/migration-assets/security-settings-for-boards/1.png)

### Global permissions

Global permissions are the overarching access rules that apply to all flows within your account. They serve as the default access settings for all the flows in your Kissflow account. 

### Setting up global permissions

On the **Security** page, locate the **Global permissions** section. Enable or disable the checkbox to set global permissions for that artifact. To adjust these permissions, simply check or uncheck the desired artifacts. For example, unchecking the **Lookup** artifact for **All flows** ensures that no flow in the platform can perform lookups on this flow.

> **Note:**
> 
> Disabling an artifact here will restrict its use across all flows unless overridden by customized permissions.

### Customized permissions

Customized permissions allow for fine-grained control over access at the individual flow level, overriding global pe

nodes.label: Status performance report
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

The status performance report displays all descriptive statistics about the distribution of time ranges in the data. This report provides the minimum, maximum, mean, and median time spent by items for each step and assignee combination.To access this report, click **Reports** on your board. This will take you to the **Metrics** tab by default, where you can find the status performance report.

![](/migration-assets/status-performance-report/1.gif)

You can use this report to isolate steps where items take up more time than others and identify performance ranges in your board. You can further break down each step by users of your team.**Minimum time:** The least time spent by items in a specific step or with a user.  **Maximum time:** The maximum time spent by items in a specific step or with a user.**Mean time:** The average time spent by items in a particular step or with a user. It is calculated by adding up the individual time values and dividing the result by the number of items in these steps or with these users. It is also known as the arithmetic mean.**Median time:** This is preferred over mean time when you have outliers in your data ranges. Just like the mean time, the median also represents the location of a set of numerical data points by means of a single number. Roughly speaking, the median is the value that splits the data points into two halves: the (approximately) 50% largest and 50% lowest data in the collective when arr

nodes.label: Breadcrumb
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic   ✓ Enterprise

The **Breadcrumb** component provides a secondary navigation trail on any page in your app. It allows users to track their current location within a multi-layered folder or the page hierarchy and navigate to any page with a single click.

## What to use it for?

- **Hierarchical navigation**: Simplifies browsing through enterprise structures, such as moving from a **Department view** to **Team-specific pages** and individual **Employee Profiles**.
 
- **User orientation**: Helps users understand their relative position within a complex application map.
 
- **Quick access**: Provides immediate links to other pages and enables quick navigation.
 

## How to use it?

### Adding a breadcrumb item

1. Drag and drop the **Breadcrumb** component onto your page.
 
2. By default, the component includes two items. To add more, go to **Settings** and click **\+ Add item**.
 

![](/migration-assets/breadcrumb/3.gif)

### Configuring breadcrumb items

For each item added, configure the following:

- **Item name**: The label that appears on the page.
 
- **Redirection page**: The destination page when a user clicks the item.
 
- **Icon (Optional)**: Attach an icon to appear next to the item name.
 

> **Tip:** You can drag and drop items within the Breadcrumb items list in the settings panel to rearrange their order on the page.

![](/migration-assets/breadcrumb/4.gif)

### Layout and separators

- **Separators**: By default, a Slash (/) is used. Yo

nodes.label: Button
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic   ✓ Enterprise

#### Introduction

A button is a key component of any application as it lets users submit their preferences. Buttons can be added to point you to a different page or launch a popup based on specific on-click events. You can also use a JavaScript (JS) code on your button component to execute specific actions within the page.

![](/migration-assets/button/1.png)

#### How to use it?

Unlike other components, a button is used only based on its necessity as it prompts the user to take an action. Once you drag and drop the button component, you will be able to perform the following actions:

- Rename the button caption. Some of the commonly used button captions are Submit, Share, Accept, Reject, Approve, Cancel, etc.
- Modify the button style including the size, background color, and text color.
- Under **Style properties**, you will be able to align the button, adjust the sizing, modify the padding and margin, and set the border size, color, and radius.
- In **Event properties**, you will be able to add the event “**On click**” to direct the button to perform an action. 

##### Actions to perform

1. **Redirection** - This action redirects you to the user-defined pages, the system-defined home page, or lets you go back to the same page.
2. **Open popup**\- The open popup action lets you open a popup that you had created using the [Popup](/t/35yzj4w/popup) component.
3. **JavaScript action**\- The JavaScript action lets you define the actio

nodes.label: Card
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic   ✓ Enterprise

#### Introduction

A card can be configured to display important data like, the available assets available, cost of available assets, or pending requests with the admin staff. It can be used to keep track of tasks or can serve a different use case such as listing out the details of a particular domain.

![](/migration-assets/card/1.gif)

#### How to use it?

Once you drag and drop the card component, you will be able to perform the following actions:

- Enter any caption such as Tasks assigned, Tasks overdue, etc in the caption.
- Choose a card style from the different card styles that are available.
- You can enter the number of tasks as well as set the icon for the card.
- Modify the color, caption color, background color, and icon background.
- Under [Style properties,](/t/y4yzj9m/managing-component-style-properties) you will be able to align the card, adjust the sizing, modify the padding and margin, and set the border size, color, and radius.
- In Event properties, you will be able to add the event “**On click**” to direct the card to perform an action. 

**There are three actions that can be performed:**

1. **Redirection** - This action redirects you to the user-defined pages, the system-defined home page, or lets you go back to the same page.
2. **Open popup** - The open popup action lets you open a popup that you had created using the [Popup](/t/35yzj4w/popup) component.
3. **JavaScript action** - The JavaScript action lets yo

nodes.label: Columns (deprecated)
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

> As of July 7, 2025, the **Column component for layouts** has been removed from Kissflow Apps as mentioned in this [pre-release note](/t/g9ykzy6/coming-soon-grid-layout-style-in-kissflow-page-builder#whats-changing). You can use the [grid layout](/t/m1ykbax/working-with-page-layouts#what-is-the-grid-layout) for your page or grid containers to achieve a layout design similar to columns.

Columns are a unique type of container component. You can use columns to place and organize content side-by-side horizontally. In Kissflow, you can add columns, change their layout, and create responsive layouts. Columns help you place and organize content side by side. Each column element consists of a single row of columns.

#### **Adding columns**

Drag a **Columns** element from the **elements panel** and drop it onto your page builder.

#### **Selecting a layout**

From the **columns settings**, you can select a layout option, and you'll see the layout change right on the canvas. Here, you can select the number of columns you want.

These are six types of columns that you can create:

- Two columns
- Three columns
- Four columns
- Small left
- Small right
- Small left & right

![](/migration-assets/columns-deprecated/1.png)

#### **Make changes to your columns layout**

You can always go to **element settings** and make any changes to resize your columns, or even add or remove columns. You can also access the columns settings by selecting the elemen

nodes.label: Container
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic   ✓ Enterprise

#### Container

A container is a tool used to build a set of components within a defined space. Within a container, you will be able to add different components that are available within the page builder. You can configure two types of page layouts for container: [Flex and Grid](/t/m1ykbax/working-with-page-layouts).

![](/migration-assets/container/1.png)

#### How to use it?

Containers can be used as the body of that defined space. It allows you to add multiple components, such as [buttons](/t/x2yzj42/button), [labels](/t/g9yzj40/label), etc., within that container.

Each container has a unique container id specific to that container. 

**The following actions can be performed under** [**Style properties**](/t/y4yzj9m/managing-component-style-properties) **in the container:** 

1. Modify the alignment.
2. Set the sizing.
3. Adjust the padding and margin.
4. Set border size, color, and radius.
5. Apply shadow and background color.

> **Note:** Events cannot be triggered in a container.

nodes.label: Creating custom components using AI
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic   ✓ Enterprise

Custom page components let you build fully tailored UI experiences using AI. Describe exactly what you need to the AI chatbot and it generates a ready-to-use page component for your app.

## Building a custom component using AI

1. Open **App Builder.** In the left nav, go to **Interface → Components**.
2. Click **New Component**.
3. You will find two options: [Create and upload](/t/y4yzj4d) & Build with AI. Click **Build with AI**.
4. Choose **Page** as the component type and enter a name.
5. Enter detailed instructions describing what you want the AI to build.
6. Click **Create.** The AI will generate the component and display a live preview in the center panel.

![](/migration-assets/creating-custom-components-using-ai/6.gif)

> **Tip:** You can continue refining the component at any time by typing further instructions into the AI chatbot.

## Writing effective instructions

The more detail you provide about the look and feel of your component, the better the result. Your instructions should cover:

1. **Data source:** Reference a form by its full name or ID (e.g., `Hiring requests form` or `Hiring_requests_A00`). Type `/` to browse and attach forms directly.
2. **Features:** Search, filters, sorting, collapsible panels, etc.
3. **Layout:** Cards, tables, lists, and how items should be arranged.
4. **Visual style:** Color coding, spacing, card size, and other design preferences.

### Example:

Create an inventory table component wit

nodes.label: Divider
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic   ✓ Enterprise

## What is a divider?

The **Divider** component used to create a visual separation between sections of a page. It can be used in your app pages to break up content into clearly defined areas.

Dividers improve visual hierarchy, making complex layouts easier to scan and understand. They're especially useful when grouping related elements or separating distinct sections like headers, footers, sidebars, or different content blocks.

## How to use it?

Drag and drop the **Divider** component onto your page and place it between containers or components in your page. You can rearrange the components how you like using the Component tree.

### General properties

**Orientation**: Choose between **Horizontal** or **Vertical** from the **General** tab. The divider will adjust automatically based on your selection. 
**Label**: Optionally, add a meaningful label to provide context. You can type text directly into the field or insert a global or local variable by clicking the + icon.

### Visibility

In the **Visibility** tab, you can set a default visibility and add visibility rules for the divider, if you wish to show or hide it under certain conditions.

![](/migration-assets/divider/1.png)

### Style properties

Under [Style properties,](/t/y4yzj9m/managing-component-style-properties) you can  choose from a variety of formatting options for both the divider and its label. You will be able to adjust the sizing, modify the padding and margin, a

nodes.label: Hyperlink
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic   ✓ Enterprise

#### Introduction

A hyperlink component lets you add links to your page. On clicking the hyperlink, the users can access additional information related to the given content.

![](/migration-assets/hyperlink/1.gif)

#### How to use it?

Once you drag and drop the hyperlink component, you will be able to perform the following actions:

Under **General Properties**, you will be able to

- Edit the caption.
- Enter the URL to which you want the user to be redirected to.
- Choose if the URL should open in a new tab using the toggle button.
- Set the font size, color, background color, text style such as Bold, Italics, Underline and Strikethrough, and tooltip.

In [Style properties](/t/y4yzj9m/managing-component-style-properties), you will be able to adjust the sizing, modify the padding and margin, and set the border size, color, and radius.

nodes.label: Icon
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic   ✓ Enterprise

#### Introduction

Icons are interactive elements that are used in applications for UI purposes. An icon component allows you to add icons to your page.

#### How to use it?

- After you drag and drop the icon component, you will find a static icon in place. This can be modified by providing a different icon URL to set an icon of your choice.![](/migration-assets/icon/1.gif) 
- You can choose a background color for the icon from the color palette.
- To add a badge to the icon, turn on the badge toggle button. Badges can be set as a count or as a dot.
- If a badge is set as a count, the count value can be manually set or called using global/local variables and input parameters.
- You can set the badge color from the color palette.
- You can also add a tooltip for your icon and position it.
- In [Style properties](/t/y4yzj9m/managing-component-style-properties), you will be able to adjust the sizing, modify the padding and margin, and set the border size, color, and radius. [Learn](/t/m1ykbax/working-with-page-layouts#setting-up-icon-sizing) how icon sizing works in the grid layout.
- In **Event properties**, you will be able to add the event “**On click**” to direct the icon to perform an action.

##### There are three actions that can be performed:

1. **Redirection** - This action redirects you to the user-defined pages, the system-defined home page, or lets you go back to the same page.
2. **Open popup** - The open popup action lets

nodes.label: IFrame
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic   ✓ Enterprise

#### Introduction

An inline frame, commonly referred to as the iframe, is an HTML element used to embed another webpage within the current page. The iframe element gets the code from the referenced source and is rendered within that page.

![](/migration-assets/iframe/1.png)

#### How to use it?

The iframe component is dragged and dropped into the page builder. The source URL consists of a pre-existing URL embedded within the page. It can be modified to embed the source URL of the webpage that you wish to embed.

In [Style properties](/t/y4yzj9m/managing-component-style-properties), you will be able to adjust the sizing, modify the padding and margin, and set the border size, color, and radius.

nodes.label: Image
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic   ✓ Enterprise

#### Introduction

Using an image component, you will be able to add images to your application.

![](/migration-assets/image/1.png)

#### How to use it?

Images can be sourced to the page in the form of URLs.

You can add the URL of the image in the image source and choose the image style from the following options:

1. **Contain**: In contain, the image is resized to fit the given dimension but the image retains its aspect ratio.
2. **Cover**: In cover, the image keeps its aspect ratio and fills the given dimension.
3. **Fill**: When you select fill, the image is resized to fill the given dimension. 
4. **None**: Here, the image is not resized.  

In [Style properties](/t/y4yzj9m/managing-component-style-properties), you will be able to adjust the sizing, modify the padding and margin, and set the border size, color, and radius.

In **Event properties**, you will be able to add the event “**On click**” to direct the image to perform an action. 

**There are three actions that can be performed:**

1. **Redirection** - This action redirects you to the user-defined pages, the system-defined home page, or lets you go back to the same page.
2. **Open popup** - The open popup action lets you open a popup that you had created using the [Popup](/t/35yzj4w/popup) component.
3. **JavaScript action** - The JavaScript action lets you define the action that you want to perform using JS code.

nodes.label: Label
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic   ✓ Enterprise

#### Introduction

A label can be used to add a specific text label for your page. For instance, you may have different sections in your page and you can use different text labels to identify these sections.

![](/migration-assets/label/1.png)

#### How to use it?

Once you drag and drop the Label component, you will be able to perform the following actions:

1. Edit the preexisting label text to a text of your wish.
2. Set the font size, color, background color, and text style such as Bold, Italics, Underline, and Strikethrough.

In [Style properties,](/t/y4yzj9m/managing-component-style-properties) you will be able to adjust the sizing, modify the padding and margin, and set the border size, color, and radius.

nodes.label: Managing component style properties
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic   ✓ Enterprise

### Alignment

#### Align and Justify

The page builder provides various alignment options to align the components placed in the body of the page. The alignment can also be done within a [container](/t/q6yzj4n/container), a [master details](/t/83yzj44/master-details) container, a [tab](/t/p8yzj4s/tab) container, and a [popup](/t/35yzj4w/popup) container.![](/migration-assets/managing-component-style-properties/2.png)

Here you can see how different alignment options affect the placement of the selected component in a page. With more components, you can try out various combination of alignments to make the page more optimized.![](/migration-assets/managing-component-style-properties/5.gif)

#### [Overflow](/t/y4yzj9m/managing-component-style-properties#overflow)

Overflow can be set as auto, hidden, or visible. On setting the overflow as auto, the scroll is automatically formed at the end of the page. You can scroll down to see the other components in the page. On setting the overflow as hidden, the page breaks at the end and you will not be able to scroll down to view other components.

### Sizing

Sizing offers you the flexibility to set the width and height of the components. This can be set as a percentage (%), pixel (px), and auto. In “auto”, the size is adjusted automatically to the original size of the component.You can also set the minimum and maximum width and height. Again, this can be set as a percentage (%), pixel (px), and

nodes.label: Master details
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic   ✓ Enterprise

#### Introduction

A master detail is a repository of data. Using a master detail component in your page lets you add a dataset or a dataform that holds the master record. The master-detail shares a parent-child relationship, where the detail derives its existence from the master.

#### How to use it?

After you drag and drop the master-detail component into the page, you will find two columns. The left column is the master detail and the right column is the master detail container. 

In the master detail column, you will be able to:

- Change the caption name.
- Configure the data source. The data source can be a dataset or dataform that you have created.
- Once you select your data source, you can select a title field and subtitle field for your master.
- Add filters to it based on the fields available.
- Sort data based on the fields as well as order such as ascending or descending.
- In [Style properties](/t/y4yzj9m/managing-component-style-properties), you will be able to adjust the sizing, modify the padding and margin, and set the border size, color, and radius.
- In Event properties, you will be able to add the events **On load** and **On change** to direct the master detail to perform an action.

![](/migration-assets/master-details/1.gif)

There are three actions that can be performed:

- **Redirection** - This action redirects you to the user-defined home page, the system-defined home page, or lets you go back to the same pa

nodes.label: Popup
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic   ✓ Enterprise

#### Introduction

The popup component lets you add popups to your page. A popup is a small window that appears over the existing page on an action. Each popup component contains a popup header and a popup container.

![](/migration-assets/popup/1.png)

#### How to use it?

Once you drag and drop the popup component, the popup window opens up and you will be able to perform the following actions:

- Modify the popup name, popup header title, and caption color.
- Background type can be set as a color or as an image.
- Popup parameters can be added and the parameter type can be set as a number or text.
- In [Style properties](/t/y4yzj9m/managing-component-style-properties), you will be able to adjust the sizing, modify the padding and margin, and set the border size, color, and radius.
- In Event properties, you will be able to add the event “**On open**” and “**On close**” to direct the popup to perform an action. 

There are two actions that can be performed:

1. **Redirection** - This action redirects you to the user-defined pages, the system-defined home page, or lets you go back to the same page.
2. **JavaScript action** - The JavaScript action lets you define the action that you want to perform using JS code.

Popups can be linked as an event to other components such as [buttons](/t/x2yzj42/button), [cards](/t/35yzj4v/card), and [tabs](/t/p8yzj4s/tab).

nodes.label: Progress bar
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic   ✓ Enterprise

The progress bar component shows the progress status for a value range in different forms. There are three types of progress bars: Linear, Circular, Semicircular.

![](/migration-assets/progress-bar/1.gif)

#### How to use it?

Drag and drop the progress bar component and choose a type from the dropdown under **Settings**. You can choose the progress bar style as well.

You can set the value range of the maximum and the current value. The progress status will adjust based on the input values. There are options to add a caption, help text, and a tooltip for the progress bar based on your requirement.

In [Style properties](/t/y4yzj9m/managing-component-style-properties), you will be able to align the progress bar, adjust the sizing and color. You can choose the bar linecap to be rounded or flat. There are further options to modify the padding and margin, and set the border size, color, and radius.

### [On-click event capabilities](/t/83yljht/introducing-enhanced-progress-bars#on-click-event-capabilities)

The progress bar component now supports configurable on-click events with the following capabilities:

- **Redirection**: Enables navigation to a specific page in the app or return to the current page.
 
- **Open popup**: Opens a popup created using the Popup component, providing quick access to additional context or information.
 
- **JavaScript action**: Provides the flexibility to define custom actions using JavaScript, allowing yo

nodes.label: Repeater
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic   ✓ Enterprise

## What is the repeater?

The **Repeater** is a powerful, configurable data display component that lets you design a layout template once and automatically repeat it for every record in your dataset. Connect it to any data source — forms, processes, or boards — and it renders each item using the same template, keeping your UI consistent without manual effort.

![](/migration-assets/repeater/6.gif)

## Key features

- **Virtualized rendering:** handles large datasets without performance impact
- **Infinite scroll:** automatically loads more records as users scroll
- **Drag-and-drop integration:** seamlessly works with the page builder interface
- **Template-based design:** create once, applies to all data records
- **Performance optimization:** maintains smooth scrolling regardless of dataset size

### Use case

The **Repeater** offers exceptional flexibility for displaying dynamic datasets while maintaining consistent design patterns. Virtualized rendering ensures optimal performance regardless of data volume, and the event properties ensure rich interactivity. These capabilities make the component ideal for dashboards, data management interfaces, and workflow applications where you need to present structured information efficiently.

#### Example

In an e-commerce product catalog, a repeater can be used to display product inventory. Each product is designed as a card containing category icons, product names with color variants, and **

nodes.label: Rich text
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic   ✓ Enterprise

## What is rich text?

The **Rich text** component allows you to add styled text content directly to your page. It supports headings, paragraphs, links, lists, images, and basic formatting (like bold, italic, and underline), making it ideal for adding informational or descriptive content without writing any code.

You can use the rich text component to insert instructions, descriptions, labels, or any other static content that enhances your page layout. It’s fully customizable and can be styled to match the look and feel of your app.

## How to use it?

1. Drag and drop the rich text component onto your page.
 
2. Click inside the component to type in your text directly into the built-in text editor.
 
3. Type a forward slash / to get the commands drop-down. Here, you can find formatting and styling options.
 
4. Use the **Fields** option in the commands drop-down to insert variables and parameters.
 

### Using commands for rich text

The commands dropdown has the following options:

- **Inline formatting**: Bold, italic, underline, strikethrough, subscript, superscript.
 
- **Text structure**: Headings (H1, H2), paragraph, block quote.
 
- **Lists**: Bulleted and numbered list.
 
- **Links and media**: Add hyperlinks, images, or embedded content.
 
- **Code snippet**: Provides you with a code block to add a code snippet to your page.
 
- **Variable support**: The **Fields** option helps you insert dynamic values using local or global

nodes.label: Tab
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic   ✓ Enterprise

#### Introduction

A tab component lets you categorize content into different tabs. Using a tab container, you can create a horizontal navigation bar with individual tabs having a container(link) of its own. It basically lets you create a secondary navigation for your application.

![](/migration-assets/tab/1.gif)

#### How to use it?

Once you drag and drop the Tab component, you will be able to perform the following action:

1. Choose a tab style for the tab.
2. Under Tab settings, you can:

- Edit the tab name.
- Make a particular tab as the default tab.
- Add additional tabs.

1. Set the color, active color, and hover color for the tab.
2. In Style properties, you will be able to adjust the sizing, modify the padding and margin, and set the border size, color, and radius.
3. In Event properties, you will be able to add the event “On Tab change” to direct the tab to perform an action. 

**There are three actions that can be performed:**

1. **Redirection** - This action redirects you to the user-defined home page, the system-defined home page, or lets you go back to the same page.
2. **Open popup** - The open popup action lets you open a popup that you had created using the [Popup](/t/35yzj4w/popup) component.
3. **JavaScript action** - The JavaScript action lets you define the action that you want to perform using JS code.

nodes.label: Working with custom components in Kissflow Apps
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic ✓ Enterprise

## What are custom components?  

Custom components play a crucial role in low-code development by enabling code reuse and accelerating app creation. Kissflow provides a variety of default [page components](/category/components) and several default [form fields](/t/y4h4azv/basic-and-advanced-fields), which are categorized into Basic Fields, File & Media, Data Lookup, Widget, and Advanced Fields—with the Advanced category featuring options like Signature, Geolocation, and Scanner.

In addition to these default components and fields, you can create custom components to use within your pages and forms. These reusable components allow developers to build high-quality app elements that can be shared across an app, streamlining development and improving efficiency.

Types of custom components in Kissflow Apps:  

1. **[Page components](/t/y4yzj4d/creating-custom-components)** – Used to build reusable UI elements within a page. You can [create page components using AI](/t/p8yg3zm/creating-custom-components-using-ai) or build from scratch using the Kissflow SDK.
2. **[Field components](/t/35yfjp0/custom-form-field-components)** – Custom form fields that can be used across multiple forms. 

Follow the above links for specific steps on how to create and use custom page and form field components.

## Managing custom components

The **Manage components** page lets you view and manage all your custom components. This is where you upload your fully bu

nodes.label: Working with custom form field components
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic ✓ Enterprise

## Building a custom form field component 

You can build your own custom form fields as custom components to use across all forms in your app. To do this, follow the installation and building instructions provided [in this developer documentation](https://developers.kissflow.com/components/formfield/).

> **Note:** The custom form fields must be built using the React framework and it is recommended to use Node.js version 16.

### Guidelines for building a custom form field

The installation steps will provide a scaffolding for your custom form field. However, you will need to write the UI configuration for your custom form field. You can use [these APIs](https://developers.kissflow.com/components/formfield/#building-a-custom-form-field) to build your custom field.

Your custom form field must include the web layout configuration for the form view and three views: **Data table, Sheet,** and **Card**. Learn more about views [here](/t/35yfjp0/custom-form-field-components#previewing-the-custom-component).

Optionally, you can also include mobile layout configuration for the form view and **Data table** view.

> **Note:** The form view refers to how the form appears on a page in its default state, without being converted into one of the three views—**[Data table](/t/g9yzja2/data-table-view)**, **[Sheet](/t/x2yzjaw/sheet-view-in-data-forms)**, or **Card**.

#### What happens if the custom form field does not have UI configuration?

Ideally, y

nodes.label: Working with custom page components
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic ✓ Enterprise

## Creating custom page components

There are two ways in which you can create custom page components:

1. [Building with AI](/t/p8yg3zm/creating-custom-components-using-ai)
2. Building from scratch using Kissflow SDK

## Building custom components using Kissflow SDK

The custom page component feature lets you build an entire page from scratch using HTML elements, bypassing the default components entirely.

Use [Kissflow's JavaScript SDK bundle](https://developers.kissflow.com/components/page/) to develop custom components over the low-code platform. The SDK methods can help you configure different client and server functions in your custom page component.Three basic steps to create and use a custom component

Design and build the required component. Use Kissflow's SDK methods to make it interactive and usable alongside other system components.

Upload the component, create input parameters, and publish it.

Add the custom component to a page and set up values for your input parameters. These values can be static or you can add a global/local variable.

## Creating custom components

You can import custom components into the app builder by:

- Uploading a .zip file containing the component entities.
- Entering the URL of the component's hosting location.

### Importing a ZIP file

Open app builder, click **Interface** in the left navigation panel, then select **Components**.2. Click the **New component** button.
3. Choose **Page** as the

nodes.label: Creating a dataset
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

You can create a dataset in two ways: from scratch or by installing a dataset template according to your business requirements.

1. Click **+Create** from the left navigation and select **Dataset**.
2. Enter the dataset name. You can also add a description to find your dataset later.
3. If you already have some data in a CSV, XLSX, or XLS format, you can click **Import data from other sources** and upload the file. You can only import a maximum of 10,000 rows.  
 
 The standard configuration supports up to 100 columns and 50,000 rows. This is a soft limit; if a business requirement necessitates a larger data volume, please reach out to the customer support team. 
  
4. Click **Create**.

![](/migration-assets/creating-a-dataset/1.png)

> **Tip:**
> 
> You can choose a relevant dataset template by navigating to **Explore > Template gallery**, or create a new dataset from scratch by going to **Explore > + Create > Dataset** if none of the templates meet your needs.

## The Key field

The first field in the dataset is called Key. This is the only field you cannot rename, delete, or change the field type. Each row must contain a unique value such as an employee number, SKU, vendor name, or serial number.

![](/migration-assets/creating-a-dataset/2.png)

## Renaming a dataset

To rename your dataset, click the current name, make changes, click **Enter**, or click off the name. You can change the description in the same way.

All the changes a

nodes.label: Creating and managing a dataset view
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

Views help you share a filtered version of your dataset with others, allowing access to only the necessary data without exposing the full dataset. In this article, you’ll learn how to create, configure, share, and manage dataset views effectively.

## Who can create views? 

You can share a confined version of your dataset with others using views. For example, you may have an employee dataset with the employees' details and payroll information across all the verticals in your organization. Say a marketing team manager requests access to the dataset to access the details of all the marketing analysts in the company. You can create a view from the main dataset that contains only the marketing department's data instead of granting access to the main dataset. This way, you do not expose the details of the other departments unless there is a need.

> **Note**
> 
> Only [Dataset admins](/t/m1h4algk9h4ga/sharing-a-datasett) can create views from the main dataset.

## Creating a view

To create a new view, click the plus icon (![](/migration-assets/creating-dataset-views/2.png)) next to the **Main dataset**. Enter the name of the view and click **Create**.

![](/migration-assets/creating-dataset-views/1.png)

Next, update the view configuration, where you can change the view's default visualization. You can customize the data that has to be shown in a view by configuring it.

- Modify the view's name
- Either display **All fields** or a restrict

nodes.label: Dataset and views overview
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

## What is a dataset?

A dataset is a collection of tabular data you can use in your flows.

Forms in your flows can retrieve data from datasets or use it to drive logic, such as field values and task assignments.

### Common datasets examples

- Product catalogues
- Employee databases
- Vendor lists
- Customer databases
- Authentication databases
- Team assignments

## How to use datasets in Kissflow?

You can use datasets in several ways:

**Control field options** 
You can control the options available for a field selection. For example, if your Purchase order form is linked to your Approved vendors dataset, then users can only select a vendor from that list.

**Auto-fill related fields** 
Use datasets to auto-populate data. In the earlier example, after you select the vendor, other fields, such as Vendor address, can automatically be filled in based on the values you have in your dataset. 

**Assign tasks dynamically** 
Another option is to assign steps in a process based on dataset values. For example, create a dataset mapping roles to locations if each location has a different team manager.  It can be used to identify the correct approver for each submission.

## What is a view?

A view is a subset of your dataset. You can create views to restrict access to certain parts of a dataset.

## How to use views?

Views help control access to sensitive data. For example, if your **Employee master** dataset contains confidential informatio

This article covers the basics of maintaining your dataset: updating values, validating inputs, and exporting data to keep everything accurate and organized.

## Adding a field

Click the plus icon on the header to add a new field.

![](/migration-assets/modifying-fields-in-a-dataset/6.gif)

Choose a [field type](/category/field-types) and name the field. Under settings, you can mark fields required, set a default value, and help text. You can also validate a field against certain conditions so that the dataset can only be submitted if the conditions are met.

![](/migration-assets/modifying-fields-in-a-dataset/3.png)

> **Note**
> 
> To protect data integrity, you cannot change the field type after you insert values. If absolutely required, create a new field and copy and paste the values. 

## Deleting a field

To delete a field, click the down arrow (▼) next to the field name and click the **Delete** **field**.

![](/migration-assets/modifying-fields-in-a-dataset/2.png)

If that field is used in a formula inside a flow, you will be prompted to remove the dependencies first. After removing the dependencies, you can safely delete the field.

## Adding or Deleting a row

To add rows, enter the number of rows you

nodes.label: Auto-generating condition values
