4. Enter a name and an optional description for the document template.
5. Click **Create**.![](/migration-assets/generating-document-templates/17.gif)

### [Generate using AI](/t/x2h9qtr#generate-using-ai)

Use Kissflow AI to help you craft your document template instantly. Navigate to the process for which you would like to create a document template. Click **Manage** > **Document templates** > **Create template** > **Generate using AI**. 
 
Enter the document template name, for example, **Employee Offer Letter** or **Purchase Order**, select a language for the template and click **Generate** to create the first draft called **Draft 1**. 
 
The AI uses these inputs as a prompt to generate draft 1 of your d

nodes.label: Automating barcode scanning in a process form
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

If you are developing a process to streamline asset procurement requirements of your organization, you can customize your process form to allow end-users to scan an asset's barcode/QR code and automatically fetch the asset details. You can achieve it with the help of a [scanner field](/t/83h4azd/scanner-field), [lookup field](/t/g9h4azr/lookup-field)  linked to a [dataset](/t/h7h4alb/creating-a-dataset)'s key, and other required fields. When a particular asset’s code is scanned by the end-user, it will auto-populate the asset’s details in the form.Follow the steps below to configure a scanner field in your asset procurement process:

1. Create a [dataset](/t/h7h4alb/creating-a-dataset) named **Asset database** and fill it with relevant asset details.![](/migration-assets/how-to-automate-barcode-scanning-in-a-process-form/3.png)
2. Set unique barcode values as keys in the dataset. This will be the value fetched on scanning the code.
3. Create a [process form](/t/h7h4a18/process-overview), then add [scanner](/t/83h4azd/scanner-field) (Asset ID), and [lookup](/t/g9h4azr/lookup-field) (Asset details) fields.![](/migration-assets/how-to-automate-barcode-scanning-in-a-process-form/2.png)
4. From the **Asset details** lookup field in your form, select the **Asset database** dataset so you can lookup dataset fields like **Key**, **Asset type**, **Asset name**, and **Asset price**.

You can assign roles to members to control what act

You can generate pivot tables to summarize, arrange, and compare a large amount of data related to your process. Pivot tables allow you to easily interchange fields, rows, and columns. The representation of data in a pivot table reorients according to changes you make.

## Creating a pivot table

1. To create a pivot table, click the **Reports** tab on your process page.
 
2. Click **\+ Create report**.
 
3. Provide a name, select **Pivot** as the report type**,** and click **Create**.
 

![](/migration-assets/pivot-report-in-processes/1.gif)

## Configuring a pivot table

To configure the pivot table, start by choosing the fields you want to display from the right panel. Click **\+ Add fields** to add the required fields.

Choosing fields

There are two types of fields to choose from.

1. **Custom fields** - These are fields you created on your process form and are unique to your process.
2. **System fields** - These are system-generated fields that are created by default for a process, such as Name, Created by, Modified by, Created at, Modified at, Flow name, DocVersion, Current step, and Status.

#### Columns

Add fields as columns from your process form. The order of your selection creates the column hierarchy.

You can include up to 10 fields as columns, and subtotal columns are automatically generated for consecutive levels, with **Grand Total** appended to the column name.

#### Values

Under **Values**, select a field to apply ag

You can also reply to people in a thread format so that you can keep the conversation in one place and won’t miss out on other important information. Mentioning users in a comment will automatically notify them. However, you won't receive notifications if you mention yourself.

nodes.label: Process overview
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

## What is a process?

A process is a type of workflow that ensures a strict sequential set of steps performed on form data. Process admins can set up a form to carry data, and then make a predefined path for it to follow. The system automatically routes the requests through various steps until the item is complete. Processes are a great fit in places where you would want strict control and efficiency.

![](/migration-assets/process-overview-2/1.png)

### Common process examples

- Leave request
- Purchase request
- Employee onboarding
- Budget approval request
- Visitor pass request
- Vendor enrolment

In the leave request process, users can fill out a form to apply for personal and sick leave. Once the form is submitted, it is sent for approval to a manager, sent to HR and/or payroll for processing.  

## When a process is the best flow

A process is ideal when you have to streamline a sequence of steps performed by people to achieve an objective, and it can be modified at any time. It best suits when you want to automate repeatable, structured tasks in your organization. The [form](/t/h7h4azm/creating-a-form), its [fields](/category/field), and the [workflow](/t/m1h9qmv/making-a-process-workflow) need to be well-defined. Every time the form is initiated, it needs to go through the predefined steps to the associated person for approval.

nodes.label: Process overview
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic ✓ Enterprise

#### What is a process?

A process is a type of workflow that follows a strict, sequential set of steps performed on form data.

- An admin or member of your application can set up a form to capture data.
 
- A predefined path is then created for the form to follow.
 
- The system automatically routes requests through each step until completion.
 

Processes are especially useful when you need strict control and efficiency.

![](/migration-assets/dataforms-overview/2.png)

Common process examples

- Vacation request
- Purchase request
- Employee onboarding
- Budget approval request
- Visitor pass request
- Vendor enrolment

**Example: Leave Request process** 
In a leave request process, users fill out a form to apply for personal or sick leave. Once submitted:

1. The request goes to the manager for approval.
 
2. After approval, it is sent to HR and/or payroll for processing.
 

### When to use a process?

Use a process when you need to:

- Streamline a sequence of steps performed by people to achieve an objective.
 
- Automate repeatable, structured tasks in your organization.
 
- Ensure that the form, its fields, and the workflow are clearly defined.
 

Each time the form is initiated, it must follow the predefined path and move to the assigned person for approval.

As an application developer, you will have default access to various data that you create. To configure or change settings for the process, click **Settings** on the process editor.

## **General settings**

On the **General** **settings** tab, you can configure form titles and lock submission.

![](/migration-assets/process-settings/7.png)

### Configuring form titles

The form title is the unique name given to each item as it goes through the process workflow. To configure form titles, click the **Add button (+)** and arrange default system fields or form fields in the order that you want, and then click **Save**. Based on this format, titles are auto-generated and are displayed on top of each process form.

### Lock submission

When you are working on the process or making significant changes, you may want to stop members of the process from starting new items. To lock submissions, select the **Do not allow new submission**s checkbox, and click **Save**.

After enabling this, members will not be able to initiate new items and nothing will show in the feed. However, items in progress will continue to follow the workflow. Items in draft cannot be submitted until this option is removed.

## [Access controls](/t/83ymdnx/security-settings-for-flows#access-controls) for process in apps

You can allow or restrict permissions to users impacting their ability to print forms, and download attachments in processes.

### [Print and download permi

nodes.snippet:

✕ Basic ✓ Enterprise

Anyone without a Kissflow account can initiate a workflow in Kissflow with the help of a public form. By activating this setting, you let the users outside your organization submit their input to Kissflow.

> **Use case**
> 
> Let us assume your organization uses process workflows as part of your Hiring process. The candidate information will be manually fed to the process form to trigger the Hiring process workflow. Now with the help of public forms, you can host a job application form online (public form) and use it to start the candidate screening process. 

Public forms are only available for Enterprise accounts. Contact our [support](https://kissflow.com/support/) team to enable public forms for your account.

## Enabling a public form

Only the Process admins can enable public forms.

1. Navigate to the process for which you would like to enable the public form.
2. Click the **Manage** button > **Settings**.
3. Click **Public form settings**
4. Turn on the Public form URL field and copy the link to share it with anyone outside your organization. You can also [embed the public form in any website](/t/35h9qtk/public-forms#embedding-iframe-code) so that an external user can access it from elsewhere and submit their details to Kissflow.![](/migration-assets/public-forms/2.png)

## Disabling a public form

1. Navigate to the process for which you would like to disable the public form.
2. Click the **Manage** button > **Settings** > **Pu

Process reports help you track and analyze how your process is functioning. Access your reports by clicking the **Reports** button in the upper-right corner of the process page. Reports are available by default to all [Process Admins](/t/p8h9qtp/managing-process-members-and-permissions). You can also share individual reports with other members of the process.Viewing reportsUse **Search** to find any report by name quickly. You can use the **Sort** button to rearrange your reports based on their created date, last modified time, and alphabetical order.

## Creating a new report

To create a new report, click **\+ Create report**. Choose the type of report, provide a name for the report, and click **Create**. 

### Types of reports

- **Tabular:** Displays information in columns and rows. 
- **Charts**: Displays data graphically in one of ten chart types, including area, bar, doughnut, line, pie, and bubble chart. 
- **Pivot tables**: Quickly summarize large quantities of data. You can convert columns into rows and rows into columns.

### All Items report

The first time you open the **Reports** page, you’ll see an auto-generated report called **<Process\_name> All Items**. This tabular report shows every item in your process. It cannot be deleted or renamed, but you can configure it.![](/migration-assets/reports-overview/2.png)

View the status summary, aging, step p

You can determine what action the assignee and the initiator of an item can take at each step.Go to the **Permissions** tab in the process editor. Select any step and scroll down to the bottom of the form. Click **Configure** **actions** and hover over the actions.

![](/migration-assets/configuring-actions-for-process-steps/5.png)

## Actions for new items

When a member of a process initiates a new item, they have two actions at the bottom. By default, these say **Submit** and **Discard**. 

![](/migration-assets/configuring-actions-for-process-steps/1.png)To configure this, click the step called **Start** and scroll to the bottom. You can choose to **Hide** or **Show the Discard action** to the initiator by default. The **Submit** button cannot be hidden because it’s the action that moves the item to the next step and must be shown.If you want to change the button captions, hover over either **Discard** or **Submit** and click **Advanced**. You can change the name to another option or click **Custom** to enter a new name.![](/migration-assets/configuring-actions-for-process-steps/2.png)In the **Advanced settings**, you can also add a validation based on the data in any field. The [field validations](/t/m1h4az4/field-validations) you configured in the form will also be in force, but this is a good option in case those values are dynamic throughout the workflow or if you want to use a [system field](/t/83h4alt/system-fields).

## Assign

nodes.label: Configuring field permissions for process steps
nodes.snippet:

You can customize the form to show different sections and fields depending on the item's current step. 

## Viewing the form by steps

You can configure the form viewing permissions in the third tab of the editor.

![](/migration-assets/configuring-field-permissions-for-process-steps/1.png)

On the left, you will see a list of all the steps you created as tabs. The form in the **Start** step is the initial form that’s displayed when someone creates a new item. Below that, you’ll see the sequential list of all the steps in the workflow. 

![](/migration-assets/configuring-field-permissions-for-process-steps/6.png)

## Configuring permissions for fields and sections

When you click on a tab, the right side of the screen shows what the form will look like at that step. You can set the permission levels for individual fields or for the entire section. 

Section permissions are configured at the top right corner of the section. Field permissions are configured by hovering over the field.  

![](/migration-assets/configuring-field-permissions-for-process-steps/2.png)

There are three permission settings:

- **Editable** - the user can add or edit values in a field
- **Read**\-**only** - field values are visible, but cannot be changed
- **Hidden** - fields are not displayed

If a table is set as read-only, you cannot add rows or values to it.

### Start and completed steps

By default, the initiator can edit all the fields in the start step of

nodes.label: Refreshing lookup and aggregation field values
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

The source data in the lookup and aggregation fields can change sometimes while a user is working on a form. 

By default, those values are retrieved when the form is first submitted, and they don’t update as the item moves through the workflow. 

However, as a Process Admin, you can choose to refresh [lookup](/t/g9h4azr/lookup-field) and [aggregation](/t/83h4azk/aggregation-field) fields in the form at certain steps in the workflow. When the user clicks an action button, the values in the lookup and aggregation fields are refreshed and updated.  

## When should I refresh my form’s lookup and aggregation fields?

- When your source data is constantly changing or updating.
- When new data may be added throughout the workflow, which can change the values.

Consider a scenario where you're submitting a **Capex (Capital expenditure) Request** that references the current available budget from a **Budget Master** dataset. While you're filling out the form, another request might get approved simultaneously, reducing the available budget. To ensure you're working with the most up-to-date data, you can use the **Refresh** action before submitting the form. This action updates the lookup field, pulling the latest budget information and preventing potential overspending.

## How do I enable refreshing fields?

Go to the **Permissions** tab in the process editor. Select any step and scroll down to the bottom of the form. Click **Configure** **actio

nodes.label: Tabular reports

Tabular reports can be used to display your data in rows and columns, forming a grid similar to a spreadsheet.

## Creating a tabular report

1. To create a tabular report, click the **Reports** button at the top right. 
 
2. Click **\+ Create report.**
 
3. Provide a name, select **Table** as the report type**,** and click **Create**.
 

![](/migration-assets/tabular-reports/6.png)

You can configure your report by choosing the fields to be displayed, applying filters, and sorting the field values accordingly. 

![](/migration-assets/tabular-reports/11.gif)

Under **Columns**, you can choose the fields to be displayed in the report. There are three types of fields you get to choose from this dropdown:

- Process fields - The new fields created exclusively for your process using the form builder
- System fields - The predefined fields that are common and available across all the processes in Kissflow
 - Name - The item’s name
 - Created by - The user who created the item
 - Modified by - The user who modified the item recently
 - Created at - The time at which the item was created
 - Modified at - The time at which the form was last modified
 - Flow name - The name of the flow
 - Doc version
 - Current step - The step the item is currently in
 - Currently assigned to - The user currently assigned to the step; this field will be empty if the workflow is completed
 - Status - The status of the item
- Table fields

nodes.label: Watching a process item inside apps
nodes.snippet:

Watchers can stay informed about a process item without participating in its workflow. As an item initiator, if you need someone to have visibility into an item's progress but don't need to approve, reject, or take action on it, you can add them as watchers.

### Use case

An employee submits a purchase request and wants their manager to stay informed about its progress and final outcome, but the manager doesn't need to participate in the approval workflow.

By adding the manager as a watcher, they can track the item's progress, receive outcome notifications, and participate in comments without being assigned any workflow tasks.

## Adding and removing watchers

Before users can add watchers to an item, admins must [enable initiators to watch items](/t/35yzjca#watcher-permissions) under **Settings**.

Only users who are already part of the process in any role can be added as watchers.

The following users can add or remove watchers for an item:

- Item initiator (add and remove)
 
- Watcher (only remove themselves)
 

### Adding a watcher

As an item initiator,

1. Open the process item.
 
2. Click the **Watchers**  (![](/migration-assets/watching-a-process-item-inside-apps/1.png)) button on the item form.
 
3. Search and select a process user.
 
4. Click **Add**.
 

![](/migration-assets/watching-a-process-item-inside-apps/5.gif)

> **Note:**
> 
> A maximum of 10 watchers can be added to an item.

### Removing a watcher

As an item init

nodes.label: Assigning steps
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

Assigning a step lets the assignee take ownership of the action. The workflow comes to a halt if they do not perform their action as expected. You can assign steps directly to a specific user, group, and user field or let the system choose the assignee automatically based on a few conditions or other sources as necessary.Let’s learn how to do these assignments one by one.

## Assigning a step to a user, group, or user field directly

Direct or static assignment lets you be very specific about who you want to assign the item to. The assignee could be a user, user group, or a value derived from a user field. Click **Add users, groups, and user fields** under **Assignee(s)** to add a specific user, group, or user field directly.![](/migration-assets/assigning-steps/7.gif)

### Assigning a step to one or more users

Type the name of the specific user, or group, or user field that you would like to add and select them from this dropdown. You can also select multiple users as assignees to perform this step, however, you must ensure how this approval is managed by them under the [Assignment preference](/t/p8h9qt8/step-assignment-preference) section.

### Assigning a step to a group

Type the name of the group in the **Assignee(s)** field. The groups created by your Account Admin show up here, and you can choose the desired group right away.

### Assigning a step to a user field

The privilege of having a user field over the user as an assignmen

nodes.label: Assignment preference
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

The assignment preference options help you determine how many people must approve an item when it is assigned to multiple users. 

![](/migration-assets/assignment-preference/1.png)

## Assign to all

This method assigns the item to all the selected assignees.

- **Only one assignee** - after one assignee submits the item, it will move to the next step.
- **All assignees** - all assignees must submit the item before it moves to the next step.
- **At least -** choose the number or percentage of assignees who must submit the item before it moves on.

Learn [when to use assignment preferences vs parallel branches](/t/x2h9qtq/best-practices-for-simultaneous-tasks).

### Picking items

With the **Allow assignees to pick items** toggle turned on, assignees can pick items for themselves and move them out of other assignees’ queues. After an assignee picks an item, no other assignee will be able to see the item or act on it. This reduces redundant efforts by assignees and improves efficiency.

### At least functionality

For example, if you have 10 assignees, but only need 7 to act on an item. Select **At least** and enter 7.

- The task will go to all 10 assignees at the same time.
- The item will move to the next step as soon as 7 assignees approve it.
- The item will be rejected as soon as 4 assignees reject it.

If you have many or a variable number of assignees, you may want to use a percentage. Let's assume that you want 60% of assignees t

nodes.label: Best practices for simultaneous tasks
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

There are times in a workflow when you want multiple assignees to act on a step at the same time. There are two ways to do this in Kissflow.

## Parallel branches

Parallel branches create separate workflows and steps that can happen simultaneously.

In the workflow section, click the **Add** button (![](/migration-assets/making-a-process-workflow/12.png)), and then **add a parallel branch**. Two branches will appear. If you want more branches, click **Add a branch**. Click **Change** to rename each branch or [write a condition](/t/83h9qth/conditional-steps) for when that branch should happen.

You must add a step under each branch. Click the **Add** button (![](/migration-assets/making-a-process-workflow/12.png)), and click **Add a new step**. Name the step and then choose the assignees.

Here’s how parallel tasks appear when reviewing the status of a workflow:

![](/migration-assets/best-practices-for-simultaneous-tasks/1.png)

## Single step with multiple assignees

The second way you can create simultaneous tasks is to create a single task that you assign to multiple people. In the step, select the users you want to assign the task. Under **Assignment preference**, click **Change**. Then choose **Assign to all**, and **All assignees**.

Here’s how a single task with multiple approvers appears in the workflow progress:

![](/migration-assets/best-practices-for-simultaneous-tasks/2.png)

## Which assignment preference mode is the best?

nodes.label: Conditional steps
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

If you want a workflow step to happen only in a certain situation, you can create a condition for the step based on a [formula](/t/q6h4alh/introduction-to-expressions-and-formulas). The step will happen only when the conditions are met. To set up a condition, click on a step. Under **When does this step happen?**, select **Based on condition.** Then, you can enter the formula. You must use [logical expressions](/t/h7h4aly/logical-expressions) for conditional steps. 

### Example 1

Let’s say you only want a step to happen if the total purchase order value of a currency field is over $1 million. The formula will look like this:Total\_estimated\_cost.value()>1000000![](/migration-assets/conditional-steps/3.gif)

In the above expression, you must convert a currency field to a number field using method value () to evaluate it against another number.

## Logical operators in workflow conditions

You can configure multiple conditions for a workflow step to create more specific and dynamic scenarios using logical operators.

- **\\|\\| (OR)**: This operator checks whether any one of the conditions is satisfied. For example: Request\_Status = "Done" \\|\\| Name = "Martin". This condition will evaluate as true if either the request status is "Done" or the Name is "Martin." 
  
- **&& (AND)**: This operator checks whether all conditions are satisfied. For example: Request\_Status = "Done" && Name = "Martin". This condition will evaluate as true on

nodes.label: Configuring deadline
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

In Kissflow, you can set default deadlines on how long a user can take to complete a task.

## Deadlines

Deadlines can be created for each step in a workflow. By default, a step does not have a deadline. To set up a default deadline, click on the step and click the **Add** button (**+**) in the **Deadline** section. Under **Allotted time**, enter the default deadline duration and then select either hours, minutes, or days. 

> **Notes:**
> 
> - [Holidays](/t/q6h4aka/weekend-holidays) will not be considered in the allotted time. For example, if you have allotted 10 days to complete a task, and there are 2 holidays during that period, the holidays would not be included in those 10 days. Instead, the deadline would be extended by the number of holidays, giving you additional time to account for the non-working days. 
>  
> - Deadlines cannot be changed for an already submitted item.

You can also set the default deadline based on a formula by clicking **fx**. You can use data from the form to create the deadline, such as _If(Destination=”US”,24,48)_. In this example, _Destination_ is a field ID. If the user selects US, then the deadline is 24 minutes. If a different selection is made, it is 48 minutes. In formulas, the units are always in minutes.You can also use a Date or Date-time as a part of the formula. To go back to setting the regular deadline, click the **Calendar** button (![](/migration-assets/configuring-deadline/3.png)).

## Al

nodes.snippet:

✕ Basic ✓ Enterprise

You can create an entire process workflow with the help of AI by clicking the **Suggest** button in the top panel. 

![](/migration-assets/creating-ai-suggested-workflow-and-steps/1.png)

A workflow suggestion based on the title of the process will be generated and displayed in a popup window. If you want to create the workflow based on a different keyword, click the text box in the top panel, enter the keyword, and click **Suggest**.In the popup window, you can choose to create the AI-suggested workflow or click **Refresh workflow** for another workflow suggestion.

![](/migration-assets/creating-ai-suggested-workflow-and-steps/4.gif)

For individual steps, click the **Add** button (![](/migration-assets/creating-ai-suggested-workflow-and-steps/2.png)) and click **AI-suggested steps**. Pick a suggestion or click **Refresh steps** for different suggestions.

![](/migration-assets/creating-ai-suggested-workflow-and-steps/3.gif)

nodes.label: Email actions
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

 

## Overview

When an item is assigned to a [process](/t/h7h4a18/process-overview) member or admin, they receive an email informing them about the item, after which they log into Kissflow and act on it, that is, approve or reject it. This requires the user to leave their inbox and manually log into Kissflow, which, in turn, can be time-consuming for busy professionals who spend more time in their email client than in Kissflow.

With **Email actions** enabled, users can act on items directly from their inboxes. They won’t have to leave their inbox or log into Kissflow.

![](/migration-assets/email-actions/1.png)

1. When an item is assigned to a user, the user receives an email with details about the item, along with two buttons to approve or reject the item. The buttons' names and the reject option's availability will depend on what’s configured in the [process permissions](/t/60h9qtm/configuring-field-permissions-for-process-steps). 
2. After making a decision, the user can choose to approve or reject. 
3. Upon clicking either of the buttons, an email is composed with auto-generated content that instructs Kissflow to approve or reject the item. 
4. The user has to click **Send**, after which the email will be sent to Kissflow, and the action will be completed. 
5. No user input is required if the user chooses to approve. For rejection, the user has to type in their rejection note in the space provided in the draft email. ![](/migratio

nodes.snippet:

Your workflow is the set of steps that must be completed to finish an item. The workflow in a process shows the path for the data in the most ideal situation and also allows for alternate conditions and flows.

## Starting a workflow

As a Process Admin or a Developer, you can create the workflow in the second step of the editor.

![](/migration-assets/making-a-process-workflow/1.png)

In the first step, you can configure who can start the process. By default, all users part of the process can initiate items. Click **Change** to add individual users or groups who can initiate items. You can also give them additional access, like editing the process and creating reports, from [share settings](/t/p8h9qtp80h4g2/managing-process-members-and-permissions).

![](/migration-assets/making-a-process-workflow/2.png)

Now, you can add other steps for the workflow. You can add as many steps as you want. To add a workflow step, click the **Add** button (+), and then [add a new step](/t/m1h9qmv80h4g2/making-a-process-workflow#adding-a-new-step), [add a parallel branch](/t/m1h9qmv80h4g2/making-a-process-workflow#adding-a-parallel-branch), [add a Goto](/t/m1h9qmv80h4g2/making-a-process-workflow#adding-a-goto), [run a process](/t/g9ytv27/connected-processes-overview), or [use AI to suggest and create steps](/t/60hv9mr/creating-ai-suggested-workflow-and-steps).

![](/migration-assets/making-a-process-workflow/3.png)

## Adding a new step

nodes.label: Send back actions
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

You can select who should be assigned when an item is sent back to a step in a process workflow. 
 

![](/migration-assets/send-back-actions/1.png)

## Assign to approvers

When an item is sent back to a step, it will be assigned only to the user who previously approved it at that step. This ensures that the same person who earlier approved the item is responsible for re-evaluating it.

## Assign to original assignees

When an item is sent back to a step, it will be assigned to all users and groups originally set as assignees for the step. 

![](/migration-assets/send-back-actions/2.png)

- Let’s say **User A**, **User B**, and **Group C** are assignees of a step. When an item is sent back to this step, all three will be assigned. This ensures that the task is reintroduced to the broader pool of assignees for reconsideration. 
  
- Let’s say a new user (User D) is added to a [group](/t/y4h4akj/group-management) (Group C) that is set as assignee for a step. When an item is sent back to this step, **User D** will also be assigned along with the other group members in **Group C**. 
  
- Let’s say **User A** reassigned an item to User E (who is not an original assignee in the step), and **User E** approves the item. Later when the item is sent back to this step, **User A**, **User B**, and **Group C** will be assigned (not User E). 
 
 Regardless of how many times the item is sent back to the step, only the original assignees (User A, User B

Simulating a workflow lets you do a mock initiation of your process workflow and check whether its path works well as expected before publishing it live. You can also reset the simulated data and execute the simulation again until you achieve the desired results.

![](/migration-assets/simulating-a-workflow/1.png)

## Simulating a workflow

1. Click the **Simulate** button at the bottom of the process editor to preview how your workflow renders in real-time.
 1. You can simulate a workflow from the **Form, Workflow,** or **Permissions** tab as you wish. The workflow steps are listed on the left side of the screen when you are in the **Simulation** mode.
 2. The **Start** label denotes the beginning of the workflow and the **Completed** label denotes the completion of the workflow respectively.![](/migration-assets/simulating-a-workflow/3.png)      
 3. Navigate through every step and enter values in the form fields to see how the process performs. You can view the form's web or mobile version by toggling the button at the top.
 
2. Click the **Submit** button at the bottom to advance to the next step. As you complete the steps, they will be tagged as **Checked** in green. The steps you skip will not have any tag associated with it.
3. Review the integrations listed, if any, for every step you navigate under the **Triggered integrations** section to your right.

> **Note:**
> 
> To view the integrations triggered at each step during the w

nodes.label: Application roles
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic ✓ Enterprise

## [Creating functional roles for accessing an application](/t/83h9q19/application-roles#creating-functional-roles-for-accessing-an-application)

Individuals or app-level user groups, be it in-app (within the same app) or external (portal apps), representing business units, departments, or divisions that will interact with the live app, are known as roles. Each role can be assigned access [permissions](/t/83yzj9j/role-permissions) to process, board, and dataform that apply to all its users.

## [Adding a role to an application](/t/83h9q19/application-roles#adding-a-role-to-an-application)

Inside the app builder, click the **Roles** option on the left navigation bar. You can create an in-app role or link a role from an external app. 
In the **Roles** page, you will find both In-app and Linked roles to the left and the workflows in your app on the right. From this space, you can share workflows with roles and review the permissions that have been granted. 
You can manage permissions for Dataforms and their corresponding views and reports , Boards and corresponding views and reports, Processes and their reports, Analytics reports, Decision tables, and External data objects.

![](/migration-assets/application-roles/1.gif)

### Creating an in-app role

In a new app, you will have two in-app roles by default **Admin** and **User**. However, you can create any number of new roles based on your needs.

1. Click **\+ New role**.
 
2. Provide a r

nodes.label: Role permissions
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic ✓ Enterprise

Kissflow Apps allows you to share your process form, board form, dataform, dataset, and list with different roles in your application and to external apps and assign them unique access permission.

> **Note**: **Manage** permission cannot be assigned to externally linked roles. However, it is possible to assign the Initiate permission.

#### [Process form](/t/x2h9q14/permissions#process-form)

A role with access to any process form will have permission to initiate or manage the process form.

**Initiate**

**Manage**

Initiate new items in the process form

Create and manage items in the process form

Work on all of the items they have created and assigned to them

Have default access to all reports as part of the process form

![](/migration-assets/role-permissions/1.png)

### How permission inheritance works

When a process is shared with a role, its permissions are automatically inherited by the associated reports.

This means that when you assign a role to a process, all users in that role will automatically get access to the related reports without needing to share them separately. 
You can still adjust or override the default permissions at any time.

The table below shows how permissions are inherited for processes:

**Permission level**

**Access to process reports**

Initiate

❌

Manage

✅

#### [Dataform](/t/x2h9q14/permissions#dataform)

A role with access to any dataform will have permission to initiate or manage the dataform

nodes.label: Sharing data across apps using component referencing
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic ✓ Enterprise

Component referencing across apps in Kissflow enables seamless and dynamic data sharing across applications within your account or [external portal apps](/t/m1h4azh/portal-overview). This guide provides a comprehensive overview of utilizing component referencing to access views, reports, and workflow steps from different data sources like your application's [processes](/t/g9yzjcd/process-form-overview), [boards](/t/g9yzjal/boards-overview), and [dataforms](/t/m1h9q1y/dataforms-overview).

## Capabilities of component referencing across apps

Component referencing enables several advanced interactions across applications:

- **Accessing views and reports**: This feature allows the integration and display of data layouts and reports from one application within another, ensuring consistent data utilization across your organization.
 
- **Acting on workflow steps**: Facilitates the execution of actions related to workflow steps from one application to another, streamlining processes that span multiple applications.
 
- **Initiating workflow items**: Enables users to start processes and initiate items in one application from the interface of another, enhancing workflow flexibility and responsiveness.
 

## Setting up component referencing across apps

Here are the three prerequisites for referring flows of a source app from your current app:

1. [Adding a role](/t/h7yzj46/application-roles#adding-a-role-to-an-application) - Go to the source a

nodes.label: Switching roles at runtime in Kissflow Apps
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✓ Basic ✓ Enterprise

Kissflow Apps offers the ability for developers to switch between different user roles during the runtime of an app, without interrupting the flow of development. This feature is designed to enhance the testing process by enabling developers to experience the app as different user personas without manually enrolling themselves in other roles.

## Key benefits of role switching

- As a developer, you can switch between app roles at runtime without needing to exit the app.
 
- When you switch to a role using the **Viewing as** dropdown, the system will automatically remove you from the current role and enroll you in the selected one. Due to this, the last role used by a developer is saved in user preferences and applied automatically the next time the developer runs the app.
 

## How to switch roles at runtime

**Scenario:**

You have set up custom navigation for the app with multiple menus that are intended for different user roles. Testing out how the navigation changes for each role by assigning users to roles manually can be a tedious process.

Follow the below steps to switch roles with a single click:

1. Click **Run** to open the runtime view with the navigation menu.
 
2. Click the **Viewing as** dropdown menu. It will list all the available roles in the app.
 
3. Click the role whose view you want to see.
 

You will be automatically enrolled in the selected role, overriding the previous role that you were assigned to.

![](/migr

nodes.label: Using variables in application development
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow plans:

✕ Basic ✓ Enterprise

## Overview

In application development, variables are used to store dynamic values that can be used by any user anywhere within the application or on a specific page. A variable lets you define constant key-value pairs. These can be used to:

- Call out values within page components of a page.
- Filter data inside your workflows like board or process.

There are two types of variables that you can use: Global variables and local variables.

**Global variables:** Global variables are declared as a common value that can be used throughout the application any number of times by any user. Once declared, a global variable stays in the app throughout the duration the app is running.

**Local variables:** Local variables are created within specific pages and can be used only within the page where they are created, limiting their scope. Other than the scope, a local variable can be used any number of times within the page where it is defined.

**Global Variable**

**Local Variable**

Global variables are declared as a common value in the app.

Local variables are declared within a specific page in the app.

The variable’s scope is global, i.e., all forms in the app can access it.

The local variable’s scope is limited to the page where it is created. No other page inside the app can use a local variable created within another page.

Any change in the global variable affects wherever it is used inside the app.

Any change in the local variable a

### Understanding variables in app development

Click [here](/t/83yzj4y/using-variables-in-application-development) to learn about the types of variables available and how to use them in app development.

### Input parameters

The input parameters are used to provide data to a field. They can be created within a page and passed from one page to another. Input parameters can be used within a page as well as passed to other pages. For example, you can pass an **instance ID** and **activity instance ID** to a process form from another process item using input parameters.

In the **app builder** > **pages**, the input parameters are found on the right side under the **General properties** tab (![](/migration-assets/variables-and-parameters-in-app-development/1.png)).

To create an input parameter:

1. Click the **Add** button (![](/migration-assets/variables-and-parameters-in-app-development/2.png)).
2. Enter the parameter name.
3. Add a description to the parameter if needed.
4. Click Add to add the new parameter. You can also edit or delete a parameter later. 
  ![](/migration-assets/variables-and-parameters-in-app-development/3.png)

### Event parameters

These are parameters passed from an event within a component. An event parameter can be created by selecting a component, going to the **Event properties** tab, adding an event, and selecting the action and page for redirection.

For example, an **instance ID** and **activity ID** can be

# Developer Guide

Extend Kissflow with APIs, SDK, and custom code.

nodes.label: API and SDK documentation
nodes.snippet:
# API and SDK documentation

 

Kissflow's API reference and SDK documentation are maintained on the Kissflow developer portal, separate from this site. Use them to build integrations, automate actions, and extend Kissflow with custom code.

## API reference

The [API documentation](https://api.kissflow.com/) covers the REST APIs available for Kissflow apps, boards, processes, and other entities — including authentication, request and response formats, and available endpoints.

## SDK documentation

The [SDK documentation](https://developers.kissflow.com/gettingstarted/) covers the Kissflow SDK, used to build custom components and extend the product's UI and behavior in JavaScript.

nodes.label: Contextual global search
nodes.snippet:
# Contextual global search

 

Global search puts every workflow, item, and person in your account within reach from one place. Type a keyword, or describe what you're looking for in your own words, and Kissflow's AI surfaces the workflows that match — even if you don't remember its exact name. You can also use global search to find people in your account.

![Global search bar open with search results listed below it](/migration-assets/contextual-global-search/1.png)

## Search with AI

Contextual search uses AI to understand what you describe and match it to the right process, board, or dataset — no exact workflow name needed. Type a few words about what you're trying to do, and the most relevant workflows rise to the top.

![Typing a natural-language description into search and seeing matching workflows appear](/migration-assets/contextual-global-search/2.gif)

 
 AI matching only works for processes, boards, and datasets — it's what surfaces a workflow when you describe it in your own words instead of typing its exact name. It doesn't cover apps, portals, or individual items. You can still find those with a plain keyword search and the filters below; they just won't come up from a natural-language description.
 

## Filter your results

The global search bar also supports plain keyword search across everything in your account, including apps and individual items — not just the AI-matched workflows above. When a search returns results across many workflows, narrow them down

nodes.label: Homepage overview
nodes.snippet:
# Homepage overview

 

Your homepage is your starting point after [signing in](/docs/get-started/signing-in) to Kissflow. It gives you an at-a-glance summary of your items, your workflows, and other key information, so you can pick up where you left off without digging through menus.

![Kissflow homepage with the top navigation bar and default widget dashboard](/migration-assets/homepage-overview/1.png)

## The top bar

Across the top of every page, you have quick access to:

- **[Global search](/docs/get-started/contextual-global-search)** — search across items, workflows, and apps by name or keyword.
- **Help** — click the help icon to open the help documentation or reach the Kissflow community.
- **[Notifications](/docs/use/personal-settings/viewing-notifications)** — the bell icon shows updates on items assigned to you and other account activity.
- **Your profile** — click your profile picture to get to **My settings**, **Themes**, or to sign out.

## Ask: Kissflow's AI agent

Ask lets you query your workflow data using natural language and get reports and insights back instantly, without building a report by hand. Click **Ask** on your homepage to start a conversation.

Before you can ask a question, add context: click **+ Add context** and select the processes, boards, apps, or datasets you want the AI to draw on. You can select up to **3 workflows** at a time.

 
 Only workflows you have sufficient access to are available to add as context. If a workflow you expect to

nodes.label: Kissflow extension for Gmail
nodes.snippet:
# Kissflow extension for Gmail

The **Kissflow for Gmail** extension lets you view and approve items assigned to you in Kissflow directly from your Gmail inbox, without switching tabs.

## Install the extension

1. Go to the [Kissflow for Gmail](https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/kissflow-for-gmail/cieofhhbpplmgpdlebmoocmoecneaolc) listing on the Chrome Web Store.
2. Click **Add to Chrome**.
3. Click **Add extension** to confirm.

## Sign in to Kissflow from Gmail

1. Open **Gmail**. A Kissflow icon appears on the right navigation panel.
2. Click the **Kissflow** icon.
3. Enter the domain name of your Kissflow account and click **Submit**.

 
 Already have another Kissflow account signed in? See [switch between accounts](#manage-connected-accounts) below.
 

![](/migration-assets/kissflow-extension-for-gmail/3.gif)

## Approve or act on items

Once signed in, the extension shows two sections:

- **My tasks** — items you can approve instantly.
- **Tasks awaiting your action in Kissflow** — items that need your input before they can be approved.

**To approve an item instantly:**

1. Under **My tasks**, find the item.
2. Click **Approve**.

If you want to review the item before approving it, click **View task** to open it in your Kissflow account.

**To act on an item that needs your input:**

1. Under **Tasks awaiting your action in Kissflow**, find the item.
2. Click **Submit** to open it in your Kissflow account, where you can provide the required input.

# Managing boards in the PWA

Manage your [boards](/docs/build/boards/boards-overview) from anywhere using the Kissflow Progressive Web App (PWA). The **Home** dashboard has two widgets for working with board items:

- **My tasks** — lists items assigned to you.
- **My items** — lists items you created.

![Home dashboard with the My tasks and My items widgets](/migration-assets/managing-boards-in-pwa/1.png)

## Create an item

1. On the dashboard, tap the **New item** (![New item icon](/migration-assets/managing-boards-in-pwa/2.png)) button next to a board.
2. In the form that opens, fill in the details — assignee, due date, status, priority, and any other fields your board captures.
3. Tap **Submit**.

 
 You can also get to a board's new-item form by tapping **Explore**, opening the board, and tapping the **New item** button there.
 

![Creating a new item from the dashboard](/migration-assets/managing-boards-in-pwa/5.gif)

## Browse items in a board

1. Tap **Explore**.
2. Tap a board to see its items.
3. Switch between tabs to filter the view:
 - **All** — every item in the board. This is the default tab.
 - **Assigned to me** — items assigned to you for action.

 
 If you're an [initiator](/docs/build/boards/managing-board-roles-and-permissions) in the board, you also get an **Initiated by me** tab alongside **All**, pinned by default.
 

4. Tap the **Sort** button (![Sort icon](/migration-assets/managing-boards-in-pwa/3.png)) to sort the items shown in a tab.

![Sorting

nodes.label: My items
nodes.snippet:
# My items

**My items** brings together every item you've created or that's waiting on you for approval, across all your processes, boards, and apps — so you don't have to open each flow individually to check on your work.

Items are grouped under two tabs:

- **Assigned to me** — items from any process, board, or app that need your attention.
- **Created by me** — items you've submitted or created, wherever they live.

If you're an [Account Admin](/docs/admin/users/user-roles-and-permissions) or a flow approver, you'll also see an **Admin actions** tab with pending **Item reassignment**, **Flow approvals**, **Admin assignment**, and **Join request** actions that need your attention.

## Finding items assigned to me

The **Assigned to me** tab lists items from every process, board, and app you have pending work on. Use the navigation panel to move between flows, or apply the **Due by** filter to narrow the list.

![My items page showing items assigned to the user](/migration-assets/my-items/1.png)

Click any item to open its process or board form. Closing the item returns you to the **Assigned to me** list.

Filter the list using the **Due by** field:

- **All** — every item assigned to you, regardless of due date.
- **Today** — items due today.
- **Tomorrow** — items due the following day.
- **This week** — items due sometime this week.
- **Overdue** — items whose due date has passed.

## Finding items created by me

The **Created by me** tab lists every item you've created

nodes.label: Signing in to Kissflow
nodes.snippet:
# Signing in to Kissflow

 

## Prerequisites

- A Kissflow account (your admin sends an invitation email)
- A supported browser (see [Supported browsers](/docs/reference/supported-browsers))

## Sign in with email

1. Go to your Kissflow account URL (e.g., `yourcompany.kissflow.com`)
2. Enter your **email address**
3. Enter your **password**
4. Click **Sign In**

 
 If your organization uses SSO, you may be redirected to your identity provider (Okta, Azure AD, Google) instead of seeing a password field.
 

## Sign in on mobile

1. Download the Kissflow app from the App Store or Google Play
2. Enter your account URL or scan the QR code from your desktop
3. Sign in with your credentials

## Troubleshooting

| Problem | Fix |
|---|---|
| "Account not found" | Check with your admin that your email was added to the account |
| Password reset | Click **Forgot password** on the sign-in page |
| SSO redirect loop | Clear browser cookies, try incognito mode, contact admin |

## What's next

- [End user quickstart](/docs/get-started/for-users)
- [Account security settings](/docs/admin/security) (admins)

nodes.label: What is Kissflow
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow is a **work platform** that lets you build apps, automate workflows, and manage work — without writing code.

## Key capabilities

- **Apps** — Build custom business applications with drag-and-drop pages, forms, and data models
- **Processes** — Design multi-step approval workflows with conditional routing and SLA tracking
- **Boards** — Track work items across statuses with Kanban, list, timeline, and gallery views
- **Integrations** — Connect to Google Workspace, Salesforce, Slack, SAP, and 40+ services
- **AI Builder** — Generate workflows, form fields, formulas, and custom components with AI
- **Analytics** — Create reports, dashboards, and data views across all your apps

nodes.label: Apps FAQs
nodes.snippet:
### What programming languages can I use to create Kissflow Apps?

Kissflow Apps isn't tied to a single programming language. Our [Software Development Kit (SDK)](https://developers.kissflow.com/) is designed for client-side apps and is written in JavaScript. To interact with server-side apps, use the **Run script** connector in Integration.

### Can I get a Kissflow Apps account for testing purposes?

Yes. If you don't already have an account, sign up for a free trial with your work email address at [Kissflow](https://kissflow.com/). Trial availability and duration depend on your plan, so check the current pricing page for details before you sign up.

### Is there a central platform to access different low-code apps?

Yes — the [Kissflow App Store](/docs/app-store). It hosts ready-made apps you can install with little to no coding, and you can also build and share your own apps with other Kissflow users. See [Introduction to Kissflow App Store](/docs/app-store/introduction-to-kissflow-app-store) to get started.

### How do I provide public access to an app?

Kissflow Apps themselves don't support public links, so there's no direct way to make an entire app publicly accessible. Specific components within an app, such as processes, offer limited public access through [public forms](/docs/build/processes/public-forms). These let external users interact with a process form without accessing the app itself.

### How do I share private access to an app?

Since apps can't have publ

nodes.label: Boards FAQs
nodes.snippet:
![](/migration-assets/boards-faqs/1.svg)

### Can I handle multiple use cases, such as issue tracking, help desk, and service requests, using Kissflow Board?

Yes. Kissflow Board is built to support a wide range of workflows on a single platform — project management, issue tracking, bug tracking, help desks, service requests, ticketing systems, and more.

### There are a few boards I use frequently. How can I access them quickly without going to the Explore page?

Pin them to the left navigation. On the board, click the icon next to the board's name to pin it. Pinned boards then appear in the left navigation for quick access.

### I'd like to add custom statuses to my board. Is that possible?

Yes. You can add any number of [custom statuses](/docs/build/boards/configuring-statuses) relevant to your workflow from **Settings**.

### What happens when I rename a custom status?

The new name is applied across the board immediately. Items currently assigned to that status show the new name — there's nothing to update on the items themselves.

### I no longer need one of the statuses in my workflow. Can I delete it?

Yes. Delete custom statuses you created from inside the **Edit workflow** menu. If items are still assigned to a status you delete, you're prompted to confirm deleting those items along with the status — there's no option to move them to another status first, so reassign any items you want to keep before deleting the status.

nodes.label: Analytics FAQs
nodes.snippet:
## General

### Can I bring in data from external sources into Kissflow Analytics?

No. Kissflow Analytics is built to report on data that already exists in your Kissflow account — processes, boards, datasets, and dataforms. It isn't a general-purpose BI tool for importing outside data.

### Can I connect BI tools like Power BI or Tableau to Kissflow Analytics?

Not directly. Use the [Kissflow connector for BI](/docs/build/analytics/kissflow-connector-for-bi) to connect Kissflow data to external BI tools. See [Enabling Kissflow connector for BI](/docs/build/analytics/enabling-kissflow-connector-for-bi) to set it up, or [contact Kissflow support](/docs/whats-new/important-notices/slas-and-support-models#how-to-reach-support) for help.

### How often does my data sync into Analytics?

Kissflow syncs your processes, boards, datasets, and dataforms into Analytics once every hour. If a report looks out of date, open the data source in **Data explorer** and check its **Last synced** time.

### How does data sync work with third-party middleware like Snowflake?

There's no direct database access to your Kissflow data. Instead, integrations like the [Kissflow connector for BI](/docs/build/analytics/kissflow-connector-for-bi) go through middleware such as Snowflake, which reads from the same hourly-synced Analytics data. See [Configuring Snowflake for Kissflow connector for BI](/docs/build/analytics/configuring-snowflake-for-kissflow-connector-for-bi) for setup steps.
