nodes.label: Product FAQs
nodes.snippet:
## General

### What is Kissflow Work Platform?

Kissflow is a single platform for managing the full spectrum of work across your organization — apps, processes, and boards — without requiring code. Alongside these building blocks, it includes collaboration, integration, and analytics tools for day-to-day operations.

### Who is Kissflow for?

Kissflow is built for the entire organization, not just one department or role. Individual users see exactly what's assigned to them and track it as it moves through a flow. Team leaders create and control codeless workflows and forms. Across the organization, Kissflow acts as an operational system of record for what work is in progress and what's been completed.

### How is Kissflow different from other products?

Most tools focus on one slice of work — automated processes, collaboration, or project and case management. Kissflow brings all of these together on one platform, so it can be used across departments rather than for a single use case. It's designed for the common business user and builder, with a layout that doesn't require specialized training in process, project, or case management concepts.

### Do you have templates to help me get started?

Yes. Kissflow ships with a library of pre-built templates for processes, projects, cases, and datasets, installable from the Kissflow marketplace. Once installed, you have full control to configure a template to fit your needs.

Example templates include:

- Sales Enquiry
- Purchase Re

nodes.label: Account governance
nodes.snippet: [Account governance](/t/x2hvt75/auditing-and-governance-in-kissflow) under account administration is a centralized hub, allowing [super admins](/t/35h4ak0/user-roles-and-permissions#super-admin) and [IAM admins](/t/35h4ak0/user-roles-and-permissions#iam-admin) to audit and manage their Kissflow accounts. This feature is pivotal for maintaining data security and protecting sensitive account information.

nodes.label: Action
nodes.snippet: An event a connector performs after it is triggered. Multiple [actions](/t/g9h4alw/integrations-overview#action) can be configured for a single integration flow.

nodes.label: Activity instance table
nodes.snippet: In Kissflow Analytics, the activity instance table is a [system-generated table](/t/h7yq95d/system-table-in-analytics) that displays the complete step history of each item across all processes in the Kissflow account.

nodes.label: Administration
nodes.snippet: The [Administration](/t/60h9qtj) section of a process lets Process admins and Data admins access and modify all items within the process.

nodes.label: Aggregation
nodes.snippet: [Aggregation field](https://community.kissflow.com/t/83h4azk/aggregation-field) pulls together data from various fields to show relationships and perform calculations. You can use it to calculate sums, averages, or other aggregations of data within a single form or across different processes. You can apply filters to restrict the values used in the aggregation, giving you more control over the results.

nodes.label: AI-suggested fields
nodes.snippet: [AI-suggested fields](/t/y4yqkrg/ai-suggested-fields) are a list of form fields automatically generated by AI. Unlike standard fields that require manual selection and configuration, these fields are suggested based on the provided form name and come pre-populated with a relevant Field name and Field type.

nodes.label: AI-suggested steps
nodes.snippet: [AI-suggested steps](/t/60hv9mr/creating-ai-suggested-workflow-and-steps) in process use artificial intelligence to automatically generate workflow steps tailored to your process. For individual steps, clicking AI-suggested steps provides intelligent suggestions, which can be refreshed for more options.

nodes.label: AI-suggested workflow
nodes.snippet: [AI-suggested workflow](/t/60hv9mr/creating-ai-suggested-workflow-and-steps) is a feature that automatically generates an entire process workflow based on the process title or a keyword you provide. When you click Suggest, our AI analyzes your input and creates a complete set of workflow steps, saving you time and effort in process design. This builds upon our AI-suggested fields feature, offering a more comprehensive solution for workflow creation.

nodes.label: Analytics report
nodes.snippet: [Analytics reports](/t/m1h9qzd/overview-of-kissflow-analytics) allow you to create cross-form reports from various data sources, including process tables, child tables, system tables, datasets, and data views. These reports can be visualized in tabular or graphical formats. There are four types: Tabular reports, charts, card, and pivot reports.

nodes.label: App builder
nodes.snippet:
**[Kissflow App Builder](/t/m1yzj9p/app-builder-overview)** is a workspace within the development environment where you can assemble the pages and components of your app to create a complete, functional application. It is accessible only from the development environment. That is, when you click **Edit app** on a published or unpublished app, the app builder automatically opens.

The app builder makes it easy to develop multiple features simultaneously, allowing you to work on a page while also modifying a process in a separate tab, accessible from the left panel. You can switch between tabs without losing any progress. It also provides several default components that you can simply drag and drop onto your canvas, along with a variety of layout and styling options to customize the look and feel of your app.

nodes.label: Application
nodes.snippet: An application is a software solution that combines various elements such as forms, workflows, and integrations to carry out one or more tasks of a complex use case. Users can create enterprise applications by assembling these components visually, often with minimal or no coding. You can build fully functional and complex applications using [Kissflow Apps](/t/x2h9qkj/introduction-to-kissflow-apps).

nodes.label: Assignee
nodes.snippet: An assignee is the account user to whom a [process](/t/h7h4a18/process-overview) or [board](/t/q6h9ql3/board-overview) item is assigned for their action.

nodes.label: Audit log
nodes.snippet: [Audit logs](/t/h7h4akc/audit-log) provide valuable insights, enhance security, and help track potential exposure of sensitive data, allowing for better governance and management of your Kissflow account. At the account level, super admins, IAM admins, and user admins can access logs for account settings, user activities, and flow-level actions. For individual flows, process admins can view detailed logs of all activities related to their processes, including user actions and item-level changes.

nodes.label: Board
nodes.snippet: A [board](/t/q6h9ql3/board-overview) is a highly adaptable workflow that lets you keep track of information and manage work efficiently. You can create work items in a board and track their progress across different statuses.

nodes.label: Checkbox
nodes.snippet: A [checkbox field](https://community.kissflow.com/t/35h4azp/dropdown-multi-select-dropdown-checkbox-checklists-and-radio-buttons#checkbox-field) allows users to select multiple options from a list, dataset, or dataform in a form. It's a versatile field type that can be used in various scenarios.

nodes.label: Child table
nodes.snippet: In the [analytics](/t/35h9qzj/analytics-reports-an-overview) module, child tables are automatically created for process and board. They are named in the ProcessName-ChildTableName or BoardName-ChildTableName format. For example, the Purchase Request process generates a child table named Purchase Request Catalog Items.

nodes.label: Collaborator
nodes.snippet: [Users](/t/83h4ak6/collaborating-with-other-members) with whom a connector has been shared. These users can use the connectors in different integrations.

nodes.label: Components
nodes.snippet: Components are the building blocks used to create applications in the Kissflow app builder. They include elements like containers, forms, tables, and reports which can be visually configured to define the app's functionality and user experience. Components allow users to design tailored solutions that address specific business needs through customization.

nodes.label: Connection
nodes.snippet: An [Authentication](/t/g9h4alw/integrations-overview#connection) that enables Kissflow to interact with internal or external connectors using Access key, secret token, or OAuth authentication.

nodes.label: Connector
nodes.snippet: [Connectors](/t/g9h4alw/integrations-overview#connector) can be services or applications that communicate with each other, comprising triggers, actions, and search actions based on their use cases.

nodes.label: Custom components
nodes.snippet: [Custom components](/t/y4yzj4d/creating-custom-components) can be created in the app builder’s pages in addition to the the system components provided by Kissflow out of the box. Custom components allow developers to create high-quality app components and share them with others, shortening application development time.

nodes.label: Data Source
nodes.snippet: In Analytics, a [data source](/t/x2h9qzw/understanding-data-sources) refers to any form of data from which you can create dataviews and reports. This can be a flow table, child table, or a system table.

nodes.label: Dataset
nodes.snippet: A [dataset](/t/p8h4alk/dataset-and-views-overview#what-is-a-dataset) is a collection of tabular data that can be used in various flows.

nodes.label: Dataview
nodes.snippet: A [dataview](/t/83h9qzn/understanding-dataviews) is a tabular representation of data created from one or more tables or other dataviews in Kissflow Analytics. It is formed by performing joins or unions via Snowflake SQL queries in the query editor. You can create reports based on the dataview once it's generated.

nodes.label: Deadline
nodes.snippet: The time allocated for a step assignee to act on a process item.

nodes.label: Dimension
nodes.snippet: A [dimension](/t/y4h9qzg/using-reports) is an attribute used in analytics to categorize and provide context to the data shown on a chart. Dimensions are usually represented on the X-axis of a graphical chart. For example, in a sales chart, dimensions could include product categories, regions, or time periods.

nodes.label: Document template
nodes.snippet: A [document template](/t/x2h9qtr/generating-document-templates) allows you to automate the generation of process-related PDFs. It's particularly useful for creating standardized documents like offer letters or reports.

nodes.label: Editor
nodes.snippet: [Editor](/t/g9h4alw/integrations-overview#editor) is a configuration interface where users select and configure trigger connectors, trigger events, action connectors, and action events.

nodes.label: Evaluate
nodes.snippet: [Evaluate](/t/83ylatd/ai-formula-builder#evaluating-formulas-using-ai) is an AI-powered validation tool in the [AI Formula builder](/t/83ylatd/ai-formula-builder) that allows users to test formulas, whether manually entered or AI-generated, before saving them. It simulates the formula's execution to ensure the logic produces the expected output.

nodes.label: External data object
nodes.snippet: An [external data object](/t/60y3mhj/introduction-to-external-data-objects) is a flow in Kissflow Apps that allows app developers to securely access the data stored in external platforms, such as MySQL and MSSQL, without storing the data within Kissflow.

nodes.label: Failure analysis
nodes.snippet: Report generated by the [Troubleshoot](/t/x2h4ak8/managing-integrations#ai-troubleshooting-for-failed-integrations) feature after analyzing an error. Failure analysis is divided into three sections: Impact, Root cause, and Suggested solutions.

nodes.label: Field ID
nodes.snippet: A [field ID](/t/x2h4az9/field-settings#name-and-field-id) is a unique string automatically assigned to each field in the settings panel of the form builder.

nodes.label: Field
nodes.snippet: [Fields](/t/h7h4azm/creating-a-form#fields) are the building blocks of a [form](/t/h7h4azm/creating-a-form), used to capture user input within a workflow. You can access the available fields from the fields panel on the left side of the from builder.

nodes.label: Form
nodes.snippet: A [form](/t/h7h4azm/creating-a-form) is an entity used to collect input from participants in your workflow. It is used in [processes](/t/h7h4a18/process-overview), [boards](/t/q6h9ql3/board-overview), and [dataforms](/t/35yzjag/creating-and-managing-dataforms) in Kissflow. A form consists of three main components: [section](/t/h7h4azm/creating-a-form#sections), [field](/t/h7h4azm/creating-a-form#fields), and [table](/t/h7h4azm/creating-a-form#child-tablesnbsp).

nodes.label: Formula builder
nodes.snippet:
**[Formula builder](/t/35h4alz/computing-fields-using-formula-builder)** is a built-in tool in Kissflow forms that helps you create and manage expressions more efficiently. It offers an intuitive editor with smart auto-suggestions when using the dot operator (.) or calling functions, and a user-friendly drag-and-drop interface for adding fields and functions.

The builder also includes a search function to locate specific fields or functions quickly, and lets you evaluate expressions before saving them. The formula builder simplifies the entire process of writing and editing expressions.

For Enterprise plans, an [AI formula builder](/t/83ylatd/ai-formula-builder) is available as an add-on.

nodes.label: Geolocation
nodes.snippet: A [geolocation field](/t/q6h4az7/geolocation-field) in a form allows you to record users' location data with their consent. This enhances data collection capabilities, providing valuable location-based information for various use cases.

nodes.label: Goto
nodes.snippet: A [Goto](/t/m1h9qmv/making-a-process-workflow) is a feature in process workflow that allows you to jump between process steps based on specific conditions. It provides flexibility by enabling forward or backward movement within the workflow. For example, in a travel request process, if an employee doesn't need hotel accommodations, plane tickets, or a travel advance, you can use a Goto to skip those steps and move directly to final approval.

nodes.label: Grid
nodes.snippet: [Grid](/t/q6yywc7/grid-widget) is a layout widget that groups related fields into a single container to optimize screen space in a form. It allows you to apply visibility conditions to the entire grid, enabling users to show or hide a collection of fields.

nodes.label: Guided autofill
nodes.snippet:
While smart attachments autofills process form fields, [guided autofill](/t/m1yzcgv/guided-autofill-for-smart-attachments) allows you to further instruct **how to autofill**. 
This can be done by adding autofill instructions to form fields. You can instruct AI to follow a format or template or even translate the autofill content to a desired language.

nodes.label: Impact
nodes.snippet: Impact is a section within the [Troubleshoot](/t/x2h4ak8/managing-integrations#ai-troubleshooting-for-failed-integrations) feature’s [Failure analysis](/t/35ypys2/failure-analysis) report that provides a non-technical summary or gist of the error that occurred during the integration.

nodes.label: Impersonation
nodes.snippet: [Impersonation](/t/p8h4akb/impersonation-in-service-accounts) allows [service accounts](/t/g9h4akd/service-account-overview) to act on behalf of users or items within a Kissflow account. For example, it can make changes to user details, access all processes and integrations, or perform actions within specific flows.

nodes.label: Initiate
nodes.snippet: Initiate is a type of [permission](/t/83yzj9j/role-permissions) that allows users to initiate new items in process and boards and work on the items they create.

nodes.label: Integration
nodes.snippet: An [integration](/category/integration) consists of a series of sequential steps that transfer and transform data between various Kissflow applications or third-party applications.

nodes.label: Item bar
nodes.snippet: An item bar is a colored horizontal bar that represents an item inside the [timeline layout](/t/60h9qlm/using-board-layouts#timeline) of a [board](/t/q6h9ql3/board-overview).

nodes.label: Item
nodes.snippet: An item is a single instance/task in a [process](/t/h7h4a18/process-overview) and [board](/t/q6h9ql3/board-overview).

nodes.label: Kanban
nodes.snippet: [Kanban](/t/60h9qlm#kanban) is a layout type in a [board's](/t/q6h9ql3/board-overview) task management that visually organizes items or workflows into columns, representing different stages of progress, like **To do**, **In progress**, and **Done**.

nodes.label: Key
nodes.snippet: The first field, found in the first column, is the [key](/t/h7h4alb/creating-a-dataset#the-key-field). This field is unique and you cannot rename, delete, or change its field type.

nodes.label: Kissflow App Store
nodes.snippet: [Kissflow App Store](/t/60h9qts/introduction-to-kissflow-app-store) is a centralized repository that hosts a wide array of applications that cater to different business use cases. Unlike the process and board templates in Kissflow’s template gallery, the Kissflow App Store hosts pre-built applications that are designed and developed by Kissflow specialists with little to no coding effort. After installation, you can quickly deploy these applications for your end users. You can also inquire for new apps that address use cases that are not already available.

nodes.label: Kissflow Apps
nodes.snippet: [Kissflow Apps](/t/x2h9qkj/introduction-to-kissflow-apps) empowers you to build fully functional applications using a low-code development approach that requires minimal to no coding. With visual tools and optional custom scripting, it enables the creation and deployment of complex, scalable applications with ease.

nodes.label: Kissflow Bot
nodes.snippet: Kissflow Bot is a chat-based assistant that connects your Kissflow workflows to chat apps like Slack, Google Chat, and Microsoft Teams. It helps you receive notifications and take quick actions like approving or viewing tasks directly from your chat app.

nodes.label: Least loaded assignment
nodes.snippet: [Least loaded](/t/p8h9qt8/step-assignment-preference#least-loaded) is an assignment method that you can choose under assignment preference for distributing tasks in a process. It assigns the task to the user with the fewest current assignments. This method aims to balance the workload among team members in real-time, preventing any one person from becoming overwhelmed.

nodes.label: Legend
nodes.snippet: In the chart interface of Kissflow reports, the Legend appears as a key within the chart area. It provides labels that explain the data displayed in the chart. Users can hover over the chart area to view the data corresponding to each label in the Legend.

nodes.label: List
nodes.snippet: [List](/t/60h9qlm#list) is a layout type in a [board's](/t/q6h9ql3/board-overview) task management that presents items in a simple, linear format, typically organized in rows, where each item is listed one after another.

nodes.label: Lookup layout
nodes.snippet: [Lookup layout](/t/g9h4azr/lookup-field#configuring-lookup-layout) is a configuration setting that determines how lookup results are visually presented to the user within a [lookup field](/t/g9h4azr/lookup-field) in Forms. It controls the display format both during the selection and after an item has been chosen.

nodes.label: Manage
nodes.snippet: Manage is a type of [permission](/t/83yzj9j/role-permissions) that allows users to initiate new items and manage existing ones. They will have access to all reports by default.

nodes.label: Matrix
nodes.snippet: [Matrix](/t/60h9qlm#matrix) is a layout type in a [board's](/t/q6h9ql3/board-overview) task management that organizes items in a grid, based on multiple criteria helping teams prioritize and categorize items.

nodes.label: Measure
nodes.snippet: In Analytics, a measure is an attribute used to compare data on a [chart](/t/q6h9qz5/pivot-reports). It is displayed on the Y-axis of a graphical chart. You can choose to display multiple measure values in a chart/pivot report. Measures are aggregated (Sum, Average, Count, Min., Max., Std Dev) to describe the data.

nodes.label: Model viewer
nodes.snippet: [Model viewer](/t/60yzjcv/model-viewer) in Apps is similar to an entity-relationship (ER) model widely used in DBMS (Database Management System). Model viewer can help you create a real-time visualisation of all the process, board, data forms that are present in the app and the relationship between them.

nodes.label: Multi-select dropdown
nodes.snippet: A [multi-select dropdown](https://community.kissflow.com/t/35h4azp/dropdown-multi-select-dropdown-checkbox-checklists-and-radio-buttons#multi-select-dropdown) field is a form element that allows users to choose multiple options from a list, dataset, or dataform of values. It's similar to a regular dropdown, but with the added functionality of selecting more than one item. This field type is particularly useful when you need to gather multiple selections from a predefined set of choices.

nodes.label: Navigation
nodes.snippet: The [navigation](/t/35yzja6/creating-and-managing-navigation) bar and its associated menu options allow you to define your application's structure for different user [role types](/t/83h9q19/application-roles). You can design your own navigation and assign it to different user roles.

nodes.label: Page
nodes.snippet: Pages help you create interactive interfaces within the app builder that allow you to collect, display, and manage data visually. You can create multiple pages, tailor them to specific [user roles](/t/83h9q19/application-roles), and control access through permissions. Pages enable you to aggregate and present data in an organized, aesthetic format, offering a comprehensive overview on a single screen.

nodes.label: Parallel branch
nodes.snippet: [Parallel branches](/t/m1h9qmv/making-a-process-workflow) in workflows enable concurrent tasks or conditional routing based on form data, ensuring flexibility and efficiency in process design.The workflow won't move forward until all parallel branches are completed. This feature is particularly useful for processes where multiple teams need to act simultaneously without depending on each other's tasks.

nodes.label: Portal Admin
nodes.snippet: A [Portal Admin](/t/p8h4atv/administering-portals) has access to the [Portal](/t/m1h4azh/portal-overview) settings option under Portal administration. Portal admins can control the portal's default settings that impact how data is formatted and displayed. They will also have access to the user management screen, audit log, and deployment history.

nodes.label: Process instance table
nodes.snippet: The [process instance table](/t/83yq9jv/process-table-in-analytics) shows the current status of each item across all processes. This table provides insights into the progress and performance of processes within the account in Analytics.

nodes.label: Process
nodes.snippet: A [process](/t/h7h4a18/process-overview) is a structured workflow for getting tasks done in a specific order. It involves initiating items within the process and moving them forward to subsequent steps in the workflow involving approve, reject, withdraw, and reassign functionalities.

nodes.label: Remote lookup
nodes.snippet: The [remote lookup](https://community.kissflow.com/t/p8yx2qt/remote-lookup-field-overview) is a field in our form builder that allows you to retrieve data from external systems and integrate it into your forms.

nodes.label: Rerun
nodes.snippet: Option to [rerun](/t/x2h4ak8/managing-integrations#using-bulk-rerun) a previously completed or failed integration with the same configuration and data.

nodes.label: Root cause
nodes.snippet: A section within the [Failure analysis](/t/35ypys2/failure-analysis) report that identifies the specific technical reason for an integration's failure. It explains the actual origin of the error and why it occurred.

nodes.label: Round robin assignment
nodes.snippet: [Round robin](/t/p8h9qt8/step-assignment-preference#round-robin) is an assignment method that you can choose under assignment preference for distributing tasks in a process. It assigns tasks to users in a rotating sequence. For example, if you have three users, the first task goes to User A, the second to User B, the third to User C, then back to User A, and so on. This ensures an even distribution of work.

nodes.label: Run
nodes.snippet: The [Run](/t/x2yzjva/running-an-application) option allows you to preview the app before deploying it to the test or production environment. To successfully run the app in preview mode, the app must contain at least one page and a navigation.

nodes.label: Run history
nodes.snippet: [Record](/t/x2h4ak8/managing-integrations#run-history) of all data pieces that have passed through your workflow, showing automated tasks and their execution status.

nodes.label: Run
nodes.snippet: Each piece of data that passes through the workflow system. Multiple [runs](/t/g9h4alw/integrations-overview#integration-run) can occur when an integration processes multiple items.

nodes.label: Scanner
nodes.snippet: [Scanner](/t/83h4azd/scanner-field) is an advanced form field that allows users to capture data by scanning barcodes and Quick Response (QR) codes directly within a form. It is compatible with process forms, board forms, and dataforms.

nodes.label: Search action
nodes.snippet: [Functionality](/t/g9h4alw/integrations-overview#search-action) that finds and fetches specific data from applications based on defined requirements.

nodes.label: Section
nodes.snippet: A [section](/t/h7h4azm/creating-a-form#sections) is a group of related [fields](/t/h7h4azm/creating-a-form#fields). Flow admins can create sections to organize fields that are logically connected when grouped together.

nodes.label: Smart attachment
nodes.snippet: A [smart attachment](https://community.kissflow.com/t/y4h4az1/image-attachment-smart-attachment-and-signature-fields#smart-attachment-field) is an AI-powered field type in our forms that automatically extracts data from uploaded documents like receipts or invoices. It uses intelligent document processing to map the extracted information to corresponding fields in the form, saving users time on manual data entry.

nodes.label: Solution analyzer
nodes.snippet: The **[Analyze](/t/q6y1jx0/ai-solution-analyzer)** button in the process builder is an AI feature to assess whether the workflow aligns with the intended solution. It identifies critical issues and recommends improvements to enhance overall workflow quality.

nodes.label: Solution overview
nodes.snippet: A [solution overview](/t/p8y1ntk/ai-powered-solution-overview) gives you a complete, AI-generated breakdown of your process in a readable, structured format that highlights how different components come together to solve a business problem.

nodes.label: Solution
nodes.snippet:
A solution in Kissflow refers to any business use case or an operational need solved using the platform’s no-code tools, such as a **[Process](/t/h7h4a18/process-overview)**, **[Board](/t/q6h9ql3/board-overview)**, **[App](/t/x2h9qkj/introduction-to-kissflow-apps)**, or **[Portal](/t/m1h4azh/portal-overview)**. 

Solutions can function as standalone entities (for example, a single process or board) or as integrated systems, where multiple solutions and even external tools work together to deliver a unified outcome.

nodes.label: Step
nodes.snippet: A single configuration unit within an integration that consists of either a trigger or an action. Steps are executed sequentially to complete the workflow.

nodes.label: Style
nodes.snippet: Style is a configuration that defines the visual appearance of a form and its various building blocks. This includes color schemes for [sections](/t/m1y3m5r/section-and-table-style#section-style), [tables](/t/m1y3m5r/section-and-table-style#table-style), and [fields](/t/60h4azc/field-appearance), as well as layout preferences for help text.

nodes.label: Subitem
nodes.snippet: Subitems in Kissflow Boards are smaller, related tasks that are used to break down a parent item into manageable parts.

nodes.label: System tables
nodes.snippet: In Analytics, [system tables](/t/h7yq95d/system-table-in-analytics) are automatically generated by the system to monitor status and activities across datasources like processes, boards, and datasets. Examples include Activity Instance, Process Instance, Board Workflow, User and Group tables.

nodes.label: Table
nodes.snippet: A [table](/t/h7h4azm/creating-a-form#child-tablesnbsp) acts as a separate section, allowing flow admins to collect and manage data in a structured, tabular format.

nodes.label: Timeline
nodes.snippet: [Timeline](/t/60h9qlm#timeline) is a layout type in task management that visually represents items along a horizontal time axis, showing when each item begins and ends in a [board](/t/q6h9ql3/board-overview).

nodes.label: Trigger
nodes.snippet: A [trigger](/t/g9h4alw/integrations-overview#trigger) is the primary event that starts other actions in your workflow. It initiates the automated sequence of steps.

nodes.label: Troubleshoot
nodes.snippet: [Troubleshoot](/t/x2h4ak8/managing-integrations#ai-troubleshooting-for-failed-integrations) is an AI-powered feature that analyzes failed integrations to identify the error. It generates a comprehensive report including the impact, root cause, and prioritized solutions to help users resolve issues quickly.

nodes.label: Validation
nodes.snippet: [Validation](/t/m1h4az4/field-validations) is a field setting available on the settings panel of the form builder. It allows flow admins to add specific conditions to a field so that the [form](/t/h7h4azm/creating-a-form) can be submitted only if those conditions are met.

nodes.label: View
nodes.snippet: A [view](/t/p8h4alk/dataset-and-views-overview#what-is-a-view) is a filtered subset of your dataset. You can create views to control access to certain parts of a dataset.

nodes.label: Ensuring smooth access to Kissflow
nodes.snippet:
# Ensuring smooth access to Kissflow

If you can't reach Kissflow, or parts of the product fail to load, the most common causes are a corporate firewall blocking required domains, or an ISP blocking the underlying Amazon or Google infrastructure Kissflow runs on.

## Symptom: Kissflow won't load, or loads partially

Pages fail to load, styling or icons are missing, or features like chat support, file picker, or maps don't work — while other websites work fine.

**Cause:** Your organization's firewall is blocking one or more domains that Kissflow depends on.

**Fix:** Ask your IT team to whitelist the following domains.

### Core application

- `https://*.kissflow.com` — the asterisk (`*`) whitelists any subdomain of `kissflow.com`, which covers your account's own subdomain (for example, `https://yourcompany.kissflow.com`). If your firewall doesn't support wildcard entries, whitelist your exact account subdomain instead.
- `https://static-cdn1.kissflow.com` — static assets.
- `https://www.gstatic.com` — subresources for Google-hosted embeds like reCAPTCHA and Google Maps.

 
 Some accounts are served additional static-asset domains depending on your data region (for example, a second CDN host for US accounts, or a `kissflow.eu` variant for EU accounts). If assets still fail to load after whitelisting the domains above, check with your Kissflow contact for the exact CDN domain for your account's region.
 

### Supporting services

| Domain | Used for |
| --- | --- |
| `https://

nodes.label: Troubleshooting guide - common issues and solutions
nodes.snippet:
# Troubleshooting guide - common issues and solutions

This guide covers the issues Kissflow users run into most often, grouped by area, with the likely cause and how to fix each one. If a fix here doesn't resolve your issue, contact support via chat.

## General

### "Something went wrong. Please contact customer support."

This generic error can appear for several reasons — most often a recently added formula or a workflow misconfiguration. Start with the basics:

1. Clear your browser cache and reload the page.
2. Try the same action in an incognito/private window to rule out a stale cache or extension conflict.
3. If a specific form or workflow triggers it consistently, check any formulas or automations you recently added there.

If the error persists, contact our support team via chat with the steps that reproduce it.

### Account approval is pending after sign-up

New sign-ups are added to the account as pending users, not active ones. Every Kissflow account has a set of user licenses, and an Account Admin controls who gets activated with one. If your account is stuck in the pending stage, you can't sign in until an Account Admin manually activates it.

Contact one of your Account Admins and ask them to approve your access request.

![Pending user approval notice shown to a new sign-up](/migration-assets/troubleshooting-guide-common-issues-and-solutions/1.png)

### Validation error when submitting a workflow or project form

If a form won't submit and shows a validation

nodes.label: Using board layouts
nodes.snippet:
# Using board layouts

 

A board's layout makes it easy to locate and navigate between items. Three default tabs let you quickly switch between the most commonly used groups of items:

- **All** — the default tab that displays all the items in the board.
- **Assigned to me** — all the items assigned to you for action.
- **Created by me** — all the items you have created.

More system views — such as **Critical**, **Due this week**, **Overdue**, **Unassigned**, and **Archived items** — are available from the **Views** menu, and you can save your own with **Save as a new view**.

How your items are displayed depends on the layout you choose. There are four:

| Layout | Best for |
|---|---|
| List | Spreadsheet-style review and bulk updates |
| Kanban | Visualizing progress across statuses |
| Matrix | Slicing data across two dimensions |
| Timeline | Planning items against dates |

## List

The list layout shows your items in a tabular format: rows are items, and columns are fields from the board form. Click a row to open the item's form. This layout is ideal if you're used to managing tasks in spreadsheets — you can review a lot of information in one go.

Click the **Show fields** button to select the fields displayed as columns, and drag and drop columns to arrange them. The list layout also supports [bulk actions](/docs/use/items/creating-items): select multiple items to delete them or update most custom field values.

In the list layout you can:

- Search for items using k

nodes.label: Notes, status logs, and activity logs in boards
nodes.snippet:
# Notes, status logs, and activity logs in boards

 

 
 This content now lives in [Collaborating on items](/docs/use/items/collaborating), which covers notes, the status and activity logs, and watching items in one place. Head there for the full walkthrough — a quick summary follows below.
 

## Notes

Notes offer a way to collaborate with stakeholders on an item. Add comments, log updates or action items, and react to notes added by others — visible to anyone collaborating on the item.

To add or update a note, open an item and click the **Notes** tab in the right navigation strip. Type **/** in the text area to open the formatting menu — attach a link, add a bulleted or numbered list, or apply Bold, Italic, Underline, Strikethrough, Code snippet, and Blockquote formatting.

You can also add emojis, attach files, images, and audio (played directly in Kissflow without downloading), annotate uploaded images, and tag people with **@mentions** — the tagged user gets a notification. Click the thumbs-up icon to acknowledge someone else's note, or copy a note's link to share it with others.

 
 Once an item has at least 10 notes, a **Summarize** button appears at the top of the **Notes** tab to generate a quick AI summary of the recent notes.
 

## Status log

The status log captures every status change made to an item, along with the time spent in each status. Open an item, click the **Item log** tab in the right navigation strip, and select the **Status log** subtab.

Use the ti

nodes.label: Use cases for timeline layout
nodes.snippet:
# Use cases for timeline layout

 

The timeline layout lays your board items out against dates, giving you a bird's-eye view of what's coming up and helping you plan ahead and meet deadlines. This article walks through one example — a content production workflow — to show the layout in action.

 
 For the full reference on timeline intervals, actions, and how this layout compares to list, kanban, and matrix, see [Using board layouts](/docs/use/apps/using-board-layouts).
 

## Content production and marketing campaigns

The timeline layout works well for content and marketing teams tracking a piece of content through multiple handoffs. Consider a board for managing blog production, where a post moves through four steps: the content writer drafts it, the copyeditor edits it, the manager approves it, and the marketing team promotes it on social platforms.

Converting these tasks to board items and laying them out on a timeline produces a view like this:

![Timeline layout showing blog production tasks scheduled across two weeks with different assignees](/migration-assets/use-case-for-timeline-layout/1.png)

**Week 1**

1. Research and outline the blog post (Assignee: Content writer)
2. Write the blog post (Assignee: Content writer)

**Week 2**

1. Edit the blog post (Assignee: Copyeditor)
2. Review and approve the blog post (Assignee: Manager)
3. Promote the blog post (Assignee: Marketing team)

 
 Statuses such as **Yet to start**, **In progress**, **On hold**, and **Completed

nodes.label: Collaborating on items
nodes.snippet:
# Collaborating on items

 

Every item in a board carries its own conversation and history. Use notes to discuss work with stakeholders, the status and activity logs to trace what changed and when, and watching to stay updated on items that matter to you.

## Notes

Notes offer an easy way to collaborate with different stakeholders on an item. Use them to add comments, log updates or action items, and react to notes added by others.

To add a note, open an item on your board, click the **Notes** tab in the right navigation strip, and type your comment in the text area.

![Notes tab of an item showing the conversation thread and the text area for adding a note](/migration-assets/notes-status-logs-and-activity-logs-in-boards/1.png)

Type **/** in a note to open the formatting menu:

- **Attach** — add a link, image, or video clip.
- **List** — add a bulleted or numbered list.
- **Formatting** — Bold, Italic, Underline, Strikethrough, Code snippet, and Blockquote.

![Slash menu in a note showing the attach, list, and formatting options](/migration-assets/notes-status-logs-and-activity-logs-in-boards/2.png)

In addition to formatting, you can add emojis, attach files or images, and annotate uploaded images.

Tag someone by typing **@** and their name — the tagged user gets a notification about the note. You can acknowledge others' notes by clicking the thumbs-up icon, and copy a note's link to share with people so they land right on that note.

![A note with an @mention and the

nodes.label: Creating and managing items
nodes.snippet:
# Creating and managing items

 

Once a board and its form are set up for you, you can start creating items to track your work. Every item moves through the statuses configured in the board's workflow, starting in the first status of the **Not started** category.

## Add a new item

1. Click the **New item** button at the top right of the board.

 
 The button name changes based on the item type configured for the board — for example, **New ticket** on a ticketing board.
 

2. In the form that opens, fill in the details:
 - **Assignee** — the member responsible for the item.
 - **Priority** — choose **Low**, **Medium**, **High**, or **Critical**.
 - **Start date** — when work on the item should begin.
 - **Due date** — when the item should be closed.
 - **Requester** — the user who requested the item.
 - Any other fields your board's form captures.
3. Click **Submit**.

 
 The **Priority** field is hidden if priority has been turned off in the board's settings.
 

![Board with the New item button at the top right and the list of items below](/migration-assets/creating-and-managing-items/1.png)

New items appear in your board's current layout. Click the **All** tab to see every item in the board, or switch between [layouts](/docs/use/apps/using-board-layouts) to view items in different ways.

## Create multiple items by pasting

You can paste a list of text to create multiple items at once. Copy a list (for example, from a notepad), go to any board layout, and paste it (Ctrl/

nodes.label: Creating and managing subitems
nodes.snippet:
# Creating and managing subitems

 

Subitems help you break down complex tasks into smaller pieces and track them efficiently. Like an item, a subitem has an assignee, a state, a start date, and a due date. Breaking down items into subitems helps you estimate the scope and duration of a task, which can significantly improve turnaround time.

## Create a subitem

1. Open the item from the board and click **Subitems** in the right navigation strip.
2. Click **Add subitem**.
3. Type a title for the subitem.

You can also create subitems in other ways:

- **Paste text** — copy a list of text and click the **Paste subitems** button inside the item. You can paste up to 20 subitems at a time.
- **Generate with AI** — let AI suggest subitems related to your main item, then click **Generate** to create them.

![Subitems tab of an item showing the AI option to generate subitems](/migration-assets/creating-and-managing-subitems/2.png)

While creating a subitem, you can set its state, assignee, and start and due dates right from the item form — or configure them later. Click a subitem to open its form and add a description, which gives other users more context.

![Animation of creating a subitem and setting its state, assignee, and dates](/migration-assets/creating-and-managing-subitems/7.gif)

 
 An item can have up to **100 subitems**.
 

To rename a subitem, hover over it and click the **Edit title** button, or open the subitem and rename it in its form.

## Manage a subitem

- **Set

nodes.label: Exporting items from a board
nodes.snippet:
# Exporting items from a board

 

You can export items from a board to a CSV file. The file is emailed to the email address registered with your Kissflow account.

Exporting helps you share information about items with people who may not have access to Kissflow, or move data into other applications.

## Export items

1. In any layout — list, kanban, matrix, or timeline — click the **More options** button in the upper-right corner of the layout and select **Export**.

 
 The **Export** option is only available to board admins and members with export permission — if you don't see it, ask your board admin.
 

2. In the **Export data** dialog, confirm that only the fields shown in the current layout will be exported, and click **Export**.
3. Download the exported CSV file from your email.

![Animation of exporting items from a board through the More options menu and the Export data dialog](/migration-assets/exporting-items-from-a-board/1.gif)

## What gets exported

The export includes only the fields visible in your current layout — hidden fields are not exported. If you have applied a filter or sorted the items, the same conditions apply to the exported data. For example, if a filter is active, only the filtered items are exported.

These field types are never included, even if visible:

- Lookup
- Image
- Signature
- Attachment
- Checklist

 
 Remote lookup fields are exported as text values — unlike standard Lookup fields, they aren't excluded.
 

Fields inside tables (child

nodes.label: Adding advanced filters using AI
nodes.snippet:
# Adding advanced filters using AI

 

 

Instead of manually building filter conditions, you can describe what you want to see in plain language and let AI build the filter for you.

## Generate a filter from a prompt

1. Click the **Filter** button on your board.
2. In the filter pane that opens, click **Advanced filter**.
3. In the text box under **Generate advanced filter using AI**, type your filtering criteria in simple, plain language. For example: _My high priority assigned items._
4. Click **Generate**. AI creates the corresponding filter conditions in the section below. You can also add or adjust conditions manually.
5. Once you're satisfied with the conditions, click **Apply** to see your filtered results.

 
 To include subitems in your filtered results, enable **Apply filter to subitems** in the filter pane.
 

## Summarize and regenerate filters

Click **Filter summary** to get an AI-generated summary describing what your filter conditions represent. You can use this summary as a base for new prompts:

1. Click **Edit** next to the filter summary.
2. Update the summary text to reflect your new requirement.
3. Click **Regenerate** to make AI create a new set of filter conditions.

For example, if the summary says _This filter shows items with priority High that are assigned to the current user_, you can edit it to say _This filter shows items with priority Critical that are assigned to the current user_ and regenerate the filter.

## Example prompts

| To see...

nodes.label: Importing items from a CSV file
nodes.snippet:
# Importing items from a CSV file

 

CSV import lets you transfer items from a CSV file or a spreadsheet saved with a `.csv` extension into a Kissflow board. You can also use it to bring in data from other work management tools.

Once imported, each row in your CSV file becomes an item in your board, and each mapped column fills in a field on those items.

## Prepare your CSV file

Follow these guidelines when preparing a CSV file for import:

- **Mandatory fields** — if your board form has mandatory fields, you must map at least one CSV column to each of them.
- **Field types** — you can map CSV columns to these field types: **Text**, **Text area**, **Number**, **Rating**, **Date**, **Date & time**, **Email**, **Currency**, **Yes/No**, **User**, **Multiple users**, **Single-select dropdown**, **Multi-select dropdown**, **Slider**, and **Checkbox**.
- **Delimiter** — use only the comma (`,`) as the delimiter.
- **Rows** — the file can have up to **500 rows**, excluding the header.
- **Encoding** — the file must have a `.csv` extension and use UTF-8 encoding.

## Import a CSV file

1. On the top-right section of the board, click the down arrow next to the **New item** button and select **Import CSV**.

 ![Dropdown next to the New item button showing the Import CSV option](/migration-assets/importing-items-from-a-csv-file/1.png)
 

 
 The **New item** button is named differently depending on the item type configured for the board.
 

2. Choose your `.csv` file from the file pi

nodes.label: Android devices
nodes.snippet:
# Android devices

On Android, you can access your Kissflow account through your mobile browser, or install Kissflow as a **Progressive Web App (PWA)** for a more app-like experience.

## Using a mobile browser

Open your preferred mobile browser and go to your account's URL: ` .kissflow.com`.

## Using a Progressive Web App (PWA)

A Progressive Web App gives you a native app-like experience directly from your browser, without installing anything from the Play Store.

 
 Recommended minimum: 2 GB of RAM on your device.
 

New features are added to the PWA on an ongoing basis. At any given time, it may support a smaller feature set than the full desktop experience.

### Advantages of the PWA

- **Faster updates** — the PWA loads the latest version from the browser each time you use it, so you're not waiting on a staged rollout like you would with a native app update.
- **Nothing to install** — it runs in your mobile browser instead of installing as a separate app from the Play Store.

## Installing the PWA

1. On your Android device, open **Chrome**, **Firefox**, or another browser and go to your Kissflow account URL: `https:// .kissflow.com`.

 ![Kissflow login page opened in a mobile browser](/migration-assets/android-devices/1.jpg)
 

2. Tap the browser's menu button (![Browser menu icon](/migration-assets/android-devices/3.png)) and select **Add to Home screen**.
 
3. Tap **Add**.

The Kissflow icon appears on your device's home screen, and you can open it just like any ot

nodes.label: Form child tables in the PWA and native mobile app
nodes.snippet:
# Form child tables in the PWA and native mobile app

 

Kissflow's PWA and native [Android](/docs/use/mobile/android-devices) and [iOS](/docs/use/mobile/ios-devices) apps let you view and edit child tables inside process forms, board forms, and data forms. You can switch between two display modes depending on how you want to work with the data:

- **Tabular view** — the default view. Shows multiple rows at once as a table, making it easy to compare entries and edit several rows without expanding each one individually.
- **Accordion view** — keeps the child table compact by collapsing each row into its own expandable section, so you can focus on one row at a time.

 

## Switch between display modes

1. Sign in to your Kissflow account using a PWA or native mobile app.
2. Open any form that contains a child table.
3. Tap the **More options** button at the top of the child table.
4. Tap **Change display mode**.
5. Select **Tabular** or **Accordion**.

![More options button at the top of a child table](/migration-assets/form-child-tables-in-the-pwa-and-native-mobile-app/1.png)

## Edit data in a child table

1. Tap any cell in the child table to open the editing modal.
2. Update the field values as needed. Use the previous and next buttons in the modal to move between rows without closing it.
3. Changes are saved automatically as you edit.

## View in fullscreen (native apps only)

 
 Fullscreen view is only available on the native Android and iOS apps. It isn't available on th

nodes.label: iOS devices
nodes.snippet:
# iOS devices

On an iOS device, you can access your Kissflow account either through a mobile browser or by installing it as a Progressive Web App (PWA).

## Use a mobile browser

Open your preferred mobile browser and go to your account URL: ` .kissflow.com`.

## Use a Progressive Web App (PWA)

A Progressive Web App gives you a native app-like experience — it looks and feels like a mobile app, consumes less data than the browser experience, and updates itself in the background without requiring any action from you.

![Kissflow Progressive Web App running on an iOS device](/migration-assets/ios-devices/1.jpg)

 
 For the best experience, use a device with at least 2 GB of RAM.
 

## Install the PWA on iOS

1. Open **Safari**.
2. Type your account URL in the address bar — `https:// .kissflow.com`.
3. Tap the **Actions** (share) icon.
4. Tap **Add to Home Screen**.
5. Tap **Add**.

 
 iOS prevents Chrome from adding a PWA to the home screen. Use Safari to install the PWA.
 

## What's next

- [Android devices](/docs/use/mobile/android-devices)
- [Managing processes in the PWA](/docs/use/mobile/managing-processes-in-pwa)
- [Form child tables in the PWA and native mobile app](/docs/use/mobile/form-child-tables-in-the-pwa-and-native-mobile-app)
- [Signing in to Kissflow](/docs/get-started/signing-in)

nodes.label: Managing processes in the PWA
nodes.snippet:
# Managing processes in the PWA

The dashboard in the Kissflow Progressive Web App (PWA) has two widgets for working with process items: **My tasks** and **My items**.

## My tasks

A task is an item that's awaiting your approval or some other action. The **My tasks** widget on the **Home** page shows your ongoing and overdue tasks, and you can add filters based on due date. Tap **Show all** in the **My tasks** section to open a detailed list of all your tasks.

### Managing approvals

1. Tap the **My tasks** widget on the dashboard.
2. Tap a task from the list.
3. Tap **Approve** to approve the item.
4. Tap **More actions** to perform another action instead. From here you can reject, send back, or reassign the item.

 
 Withdraw isn't available from **More actions** on a task. Withdrawing an item is only available to the person who initiated it, from the **My items** widget.
 

 ![Task action sheet showing Approve and More actions options](/migration-assets/managing-processes-in-pwa/1.gif)
 

### Other actions

1. Tap the **My tasks** widget on the dashboard.
2. Tap a task card from the list.
3. Tap the **More options** menu in the upper-right corner of the task.
4. Tap **Progress** to view the workflow and the task's current status.
5. Tap the **Comments** button (![Comments icon](/migration-assets/managing-processes-in-pwa/2.png)) to leave a comment about the task.

 ![Progress and Comments views opened from a task's More options menu](/migration-assets/managing-processes-

nodes.label: Managing active sessions
nodes.snippet:
# Managing active sessions

 

The Active sessions page lists every device your account is currently signed in from, along with the browser, operating system, and IP address used for each session. Use it to spot sessions you don't recognize and sign them out.

## Access active sessions

1. Sign in to Kissflow and click your profile picture in the upper-right corner of the home page.
2. Click **My settings** > **Security settings** > **Active sessions**.

![Active sessions page listing signed-in devices with their browser, OS, and IP details](/migration-assets/active-sessions/1.png)

## What each session shows

Every entry in the list is a single device or browser where your account is signed in.

| Detail | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Device information | The web browser and, for computer sessions, the operating system version. |
| IP address | The IP address the session is connecting from. |
| First signed in on | The date and time the session first signed in. Hover over it to see the exact timestamp. |

 
 Operating system version is only shown for sessions from a computer. For other device types, this field is left blank.
 

## Sign out of a session

- **Sign out of one session** — click the **More options** button on that session and select **Sign out**. Signing out your current session prompts you to confirm before it signs you out.
- **Sign out of every session** — click **Sign out from all active sessions** to sign out of your current session and every other active s

nodes.label: API Authentication
nodes.snippet:
# API Authentication

 

The API authentication page in your personal settings brings together everything you need to authenticate API calls to Kissflow — your account identifiers, your debug session key, and the API keys or access keys used to authorize requests.

## Access API authentication settings

1. Click your profile picture in the upper-right corner, then click **My settings**.
2. Click **API authentication**.

## Account identifiers

Account identifiers help you uniquely identify your Kissflow account when making API calls or establishing sessions.

| Identifier | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Account ID | A unique alphanumeric key that identifies your Kissflow account. Copy it and use it to identify your account while making API calls. |
| Account domain | The domain address you use to access your Kissflow account directly in a web browser. |

![Account identifiers section showing the Account ID and Account domain fields with copy controls](/migration-assets/api-authentication/1.png)

## Debug session key

If there's an issue with your account, the Kissflow team may need your debug session key to troubleshoot it.

1. Under **Debug session key**, click **Show** to reveal your unique key.
2. Click **Copy** and share the key with the Kissflow team to help them troubleshoot.

 
 Share your debug session key only with the Kissflow team. Avoid sharing it anywhere else.
 

![Debug session key section with the Show and Copy controls](/migration-assets/api-authentication/2.

nodes.label: Changing the background theme
nodes.snippet:
# Changing the background theme

 

You can switch the background theme of your Kissflow account between **System default**, **Light**, **Dark**, and **Dim** modes from the **Themes** section.

## Change your theme

1. Click your profile picture in the upper-right corner of the page.
2. Select **Themes**.

 ![Profile menu open with the Themes option highlighted](/migration-assets/changing-the-background-theme/1.png)
 

3. Choose a theme. Your selection is applied immediately.
4. Click **Done** to close the dialog.

The available themes are:

- **System default** — matches the theme set on your local device or browser.
- **Light** — a white background with dark text.
- **Dark** — a near-black background with light text.
- **Dim** — a dark grey background with light text.

 
 The theme is applied as soon as you select it — **Done** only closes the dialog, so you can preview a theme before dismissing it.
 

 
 The theme you select applies to all Kissflow accounts you access from the same browser.
 

## What's next

- [Personal info, format settings, and task delegation](/docs/use/personal-settings/personal-info-format-settings-and-task-delegation)
- [Customizing themes for Kissflow Apps](/docs/build/apps/customizing-themes-for-kissflow-apps)
- [Viewing notifications](/docs/use/personal-settings/viewing-notifications)

nodes.label: Personal info, format settings, and task delegation
nodes.snippet:
# Personal info, format settings, and task delegation

 

The **General settings** section of **My settings** is where you manage your own profile, password, display preferences, and task delegation — separate from account-wide settings, which admins manage.

## Access your settings

1. Click your profile picture in the upper-right corner of the Home screen.
2. Click **My settings**.

The **My settings** page has three categories: **General**, **Security**, and **Notification settings**. **General settings** contains **Personal info**, **Format settings**, and **Task Delegation**.

![My settings page showing the General, Security, and Notification settings categories](/migration-assets/personal-info-format-settings-and-task-delegation/4.png)

## Personal info

The **Personal info** page lets you fill in your details, upload a profile picture, add a signature, and manage your email address.

### Change your password

1. Click **Change password**.
2. Enter your current password and your new password.
3. Click **Save**.

### Reset a forgotten password

1. Click **Change password** > **Forgot password?**
2. Click **Send email**.

You'll receive an email with instructions to reset your password.

 
 The reset link in this email expires after 24 hours. If it expires before you use it, restart the **Forgot password?** flow to get a new one.
 

## Format settings

Format settings control how data and time-based values are displayed for your own account. They're separate from the acco

nodes.label: Two-factor authentication
nodes.snippet:
# Two-factor authentication

 

Two-factor authentication (2FA) adds an extra layer of security to your Kissflow account by requiring more than just a password to sign in. Once 2FA is turned on for your account, you're prompted to enter a code along with your username and password.

## 2FA enrollment

Kissflow [IAM Admins](/docs/admin/users/user-roles-and-permissions#iam-admin) control 2FA enrollment for every user in the account, and can set it to one of three states:

| Enrollment | What it means |
| ----------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Recommended | Users can choose whether to turn 2FA on for their own account. This is the default for new users. |
| Mandatory | 2FA is required to sign in. |
| Disable | 2FA is unavailable for the user. |

 
 New users start with 2FA set to **Recommended**, not disabled — you can already turn 2FA on for your own account unless your IAM Admin has changed it.
 

![Admin view of bulk 2FA enrollment options for users](/migration-assets/two-factor-authentication/3.gif)

## Enabling 2FA for your account

If your IAM Admin has made 2FA recommended or mandatory, you'll see the 2FA widget under **My settings** > **Security**. Turn on the widget, then choose how you want to receive your sign-in code:

- **Email** — the code is sent to your email address. Nothing else needs to be configured.
- **Authentication app** — the code is generated by an authenticator app on your mobile device or computer.

![My settin

nodes.label: User notification settings
nodes.snippet:
# User notification settings

 

**Notification settings** lets you customize how and when you're notified about activity in your Kissflow account. You can control which channels you receive notifications on, and fine-tune notifications for individual processes, boards, datasets, apps, or portals.

## Global notifications

1. Click your profile picture > **My settings** > **Notification settings**.

 ![My settings menu with Notification settings highlighted](/migration-assets/user-notification-settings/1.png)
 

2. Under **Global notifications**, turn the following toggles on or off:
 - **Email notifications** — turn this ON to receive notifications via email.
 - **Web app notifications** — turn this ON to receive notifications within the web application.
 - **Push notifications** — turn this ON to receive push notifications. When you enable this for the first time, your browser may prompt you to allow desktop notifications from Kissflow — if you deny the prompt, push notifications won't work until you re-enable them in your browser settings.
 - **Bot notifications** — turn this ON to receive notifications from the Kissflow bot.

 
 Essential system notifications — such as those related to account security and user management — are prioritized and sent regardless of your channel settings, to make sure you don't miss critical updates.
 

 
 The examples below illustrate the kind of system notifications that bypass your channel settings. The exact list may vary and isn't limite

nodes.label: Viewing notifications
nodes.snippet:
# Viewing notifications

 

Kissflow notifies you whenever there's activity on items assigned to you, mentions, and other updates across your account. You can review these from the notification bell in the top navigation bar, or open the full-screen notifications page to see everything in one place.

## View your notifications

Click the bell icon (![Bell icon](/migration-assets/viewing-notifications/5.svg)) in the upper-right corner of the screen to open your notification pop-up.

![Top navigation bar with the notification bell icon highlighted](/migration-assets/viewing-notifications/1.png)

From the pop-up, you can scroll through all your notifications. New notifications are highlighted, and an unread count appears next to the bell icon. Click a notification card to go to the page associated with it.

![Notification pop-up showing the list of notifications with unread items highlighted and the unread count badge](/migration-assets/viewing-notifications/2.png)

 
 Notification order is set by the server, so it may not always match the exact order events occurred in.
 

## Manage your notifications

- **Mark a notification as read** — Hover over a notification card and click the grey-filled circle that appears on its right side. The circle turns into an outline once the notification is read; click it again to mark it unread.
- **Mark all as read** — Click **Mark all as read** at the top of the pop-up. This link only appears when you have at least one unread notification, and

nodes.label: Filtering items in a process
nodes.snippet:
# Filtering items in a process

 

Instead of scrolling through every record, apply a filter to your process page to see only the items that match specific conditions. You can filter items inside the **Draft**, **In progress**, **Completed**, **Withdrawn**, and **Rejected** tabs.

## Add a filter

A filter can be a single condition, or a set of conditions grouped together. Only items that satisfy the filter are shown on the process page.

1. Click the **Filter** button on your process page.

 ![Filter button on the process page toolbar](/migration-assets/managing-filters/5.png)
 

2. Click **+ Add a filter**.
3. Select a field from the **Select field** dropdown, then define the condition:
 - Choose an operator. Operators are contextually populated based on the field you select — for example **Equal to**, **Not equal to**, or **Contains** for a text field, and **Is empty** where applicable.
 - Select a field or enter the value to check the condition against.

 ![Operator dropdown populated based on the selected field's type](/migration-assets/filtering-items-in-a-process/6.gif)
 

4. To add more conditions to the same group, click **Add new condition** and choose whether the group uses **AND** or **OR** logic.

 
 A filter group can use only one logical operator throughout. If a group has five conditions, they must all be joined with AND, or all with OR — you can't mix AND and OR within the same group.
 

5. Click **+ Add new filter** to add as many filter groups as you need.

nodes.label: Member actions in a process
nodes.snippet:
# Member actions in a process

 

As a process member, you initiate items and act on the steps assigned to you. This article covers where to find your items, the actions available on them, and how to perform bulk actions, duplicate items, and print a form.

## Accessing your items and tasks

Every process has two primary tabs: **Created by me** and **Assigned items**.

### Created by me

The **Created by me** tab shows all the items you've been active on. For each section, you can:

- Search items.
- Apply filters.
- Control which fields are shown or hidden from the **Show/hide fields** ( ![Show/hide fields icon](/migration-assets/member-actions-in-a-process/1.png) ) menu.
 

 
 Table fields cannot be shown or hidden, and their associated data cannot be used for searching or filtering.
 

Click an item to open it and check its progress. You can also see the fields in the form that are made visible at your particular step.

![Item opened from the Created by me tab showing its fields and progress](/migration-assets/member-actions-in-a-process/2.png)

Items are categorized in this order:

| Category | What it shows |
| ---------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Drafts** | Items you've started filling out but haven't submitted. You can edit values and delete, save, or submit the item. In table v

nodes.label: Watching a process item
nodes.snippet:
# Watching a process item

 

Watchers can stay informed about a process item without participating in its workflow. As an item initiator, if you need someone to have visibility into an item's progress but don't need them to approve, reject, or act on it, you can add them as a watcher.

 
 For example, an employee submits a purchase request and wants their manager to stay informed about its progress and final outcome, without the manager taking part in the approval workflow. Adding the manager as a watcher lets them track the item and join the conversation without being assigned any workflow tasks.
 

Before anyone can add watchers to an item, a process admin must turn on **Allow process members to watch items** under the process's [Security settings](/docs/admin/processes/security-settings-for-flows). Only users who are already part of the process — in any role — can be added as watchers.

## Add a watcher

Only the item's initiator can add watchers to it.

1. Open the process item.
2. Click the **Watchers** button on the item form.

 ![Watchers icon on the item form header](/migration-assets/watching-a-process-item-inside-apps/1.png)
 

3. Search for and select a process user.
4. Click **Add**.

![Animation of adding a watcher to a process item](/migration-assets/watching-a-process-item/3.gif)

 
 An item can have a maximum of 10 watchers.
 

## Remove a watcher

The item's initiator can remove any watcher; a watcher can only remove themselves.

As the item initiator:

1. O

nodes.label: Action required: Deprecation of Kissflow Employee Management Table
nodes.snippet:
# Action required: Deprecation of Kissflow Employee Management Table

Effective **mid-January 2024**, Kissflow deprecated the Employee Management Table. After this date, employee details are no longer accessible through that table.

## What changed

All employee details previously available in the Employee Management Table are now accessible through the User Management table.

Access is more restricted than before: only User admins, IAM admins, and Super admins can view user details there. Previously, employee details could be shared with a wider set of users.

The `_employee()` system field is no longer available for new use. Existing fields that referenced `_employee()` were automatically replaced with `user()`. Use `user()` in new workflows going forward.

## What to do

If you were sharing employee details with other users through the Employee Management Table, let them know about this change and direct them to a User admin, IAM admin, or Super admin for anything they still need.

 
 This change applies to accounts on the KF Digital Workflow, KF Process, KF Project, KF Workflow Lite, KF Workflow Suite, and KF Workflow plans.
 

If you have questions, [contact Kissflow support](/docs/whats-new/important-notices/slas-and-support-models#how-to-reach-support).

nodes.label: Action required: End of support for Kissflow Chat connector in Integrations
nodes.snippet:
# Action required: End of support for Kissflow Chat connector in Integrations

Effective **20 December 2023**, Kissflow Integrations no longer supports the Kissflow Chat connector. Any integration that depends on this connector stopped working after that date.

## What to do

If you have integrations built on the Kissflow Chat connector, switch them to another supported chat platform. Kissflow Integrations currently supports connectors for Google Chat, Microsoft Teams, and Slack — reconfigure your integration to use one of these instead.

If you need help with the transition, [contact Kissflow support](/docs/whats-new/important-notices/slas-and-support-models#how-to-reach-support).

nodes.label: Important Announcement: Deprecation of Self-Serve Option in Kissflow Subscription & Billing
nodes.snippet:
Effective end of March, Kissflow discontinued the self-serve support option in the **Subscription & billing** section for the following plans:

- **KFDW** — Free, Basic, Advanced, Fully Loaded
- **KF WORKFLOW** — Basic, Advanced, Fully Loaded

## What changed

The **Manage plans** button, previously used to purchase additional user licenses directly, has been removed. A **Chat for help** button now appears under **Subscription details** in the **Subscription & billing** section. Use it to reach support for additional licenses, plan changes, or billing-frequency adjustments.

**Subscription details page after deprecation:**

![](/migration-assets/important-announcement-deprecation-of-self-serve-option-in-kissflow-subscription-billing/1.png)

 
 Card and billing details are no longer shown on the Subscription & billing page. Invoices can still be viewed and downloaded by super admins and billing admins.
 

If you have questions about this change, contact support through the **Chat for help** button.

nodes.label: Important reminder: Deprecation of Kissflow Chat and Spaces
nodes.snippet:
# Important reminder: Deprecation of Kissflow Chat and Spaces

Effective **1 July 2024**, Kissflow Chat and Kissflow Spaces were discontinued.

## What changed

All functionality for both services was removed on this date, and access is no longer available.

## What to do

If you have questions about this change, [contact Kissflow support](/docs/whats-new/important-notices/slas-and-support-models#how-to-reach-support).

nodes.label: Enhancing stability with static IP reservations
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow sends outbound calls — including integration calls to external applications — through [Google Cloud NAT](https://cloud.google.com/nat/docs/overview). Before January 7, 2024, these calls used dynamic public IPs that could change over time, which meant customers who safelisted Kissflow IPs in their firewalls occasionally had to update their rules.

## What changed

On **January 7, 2024**, Kissflow switched Cloud NAT to manual IP allocation, giving outbound traffic a fixed set of reserved static IPs. Firewall rules that safelist these IPs no longer need to be updated as Kissflow's underlying infrastructure changes.

## Who was affected

This change only affected customers who had safelisted Kissflow IPs in their firewall to allow Kissflow's integration calls into their network. Customers without that configuration were not affected.

Affected customers needed to safelist the new static IPs alongside the existing ones before the cutover, then remove the old IPs afterward. The current, authoritative list of IPs to safelist is maintained in [Safelisting IPs for external data connections](/docs/build/external-data/safelisting-ips-for-external-data-connections) — use that page for your region's IPs rather than any list from this notice.

## Change window

The Cloud NAT migration was implemented on Sunday, January 7, 2024, during a period of minimal traffic. Integrations that were actively triggering during the cutover could have seen a disruption of less than one minute.

If

nodes.label: Important information: Intermittent access issues due to security challenges
nodes.snippet:
You may occasionally experience access issues in Kissflow due to network-level security checks on certain domains.

## What happens

Kissflow uses a network-level security layer to protect against malicious traffic and automated attacks. As part of this, incoming requests can be subjected to challenge mechanisms, such as browser validation or traffic inspection.

When a challenge fails, the request is blocked before it reaches Kissflow. This can cause the application to not load, or an action to fail, without a clear error message.

## What this means for you

- Pages may fail to load or appear unresponsive.
- Certain actions may not complete successfully.
- These issues can occur intermittently, depending on network conditions, IP reputation, or security policies.

## What you can do

 
 Try these steps in order.
 

1. Refresh the page to retry the request.
2. Disable any VPN or proxy that may be affecting request routing.
3. Ask your IT team to review your network or firewall configuration.
4. If the issue persists, contact [Kissflow support](/docs/reference/troubleshooting).

nodes.label: Important update regarding Kissflow's SSL certificate
nodes.snippet:
Sectigo, Kissflow's SSL certificate provider, is migrating its signing infrastructure to a new root certificate: **Sectigo Public Server Authentication Root R46**. This replaces the older trust chain built on **AAA Certificate Services** and **USERTrust RSA Certification Authority**.

Most users won't notice this change — modern browsers and operating systems already trust the new root. It only affects systems that can't receive regular OS or browser updates.

## Who is affected

You may be affected if you access Kissflow from:

- Legacy servers, embedded devices, or other isolated environments that don't receive regular OS/browser updates.
- Older browser or OS versions that predate native support for the new root (see the version table below).

Affected systems will start seeing SSL trust errors once the old chain is no longer supported:

**In legacy environments or servers:**
- SSL handshake failure
- SSL connection failed
- `ERR_SSL_PROTOCOL_ERROR`

**In older browsers:**
- `NET::ERR_CERT_AUTHORITY_INVALID`
- "Your connection is not private"

![SSL error shown in a browser](/migration-assets/important-update-regarding-kissflows-ssl-certificate/1.png)

These errors happen because the affected system still relies on the old trust path — AAA Certificate Services → USERTrust RSA Certification Authority → Sectigo — which is being retired.

## Deadlines

 
 After these dates, only the new Sectigo root (R46) will be trusted. Systems that haven't updated will lose access to Kissf

nodes.label: Multi-language support in Kissflow
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow's interface is available in the following 20 languages.

| Language | Native name |
| --- | --- |
| English | English |
| Simplified Chinese | 简体中文 |
| Italian | Italiano |
| Korean | 한국어 |
| Japanese | 日本語 |
| Spanish | Español |
| Brazilian Portuguese | Português (Brazil) |
| French | Français |
| German | Deutsch |

| Vietnamese | Tiếng Việt |

| Czech | Čeština |
| Ukrainian | Українська |
| Hindi | हिन्दी |
| Romanian | Română |
| Persian | فارسی |

 
 Arabic is only available if your account has been enabled for it. [Contact Kissflow support](/docs/whats-new/important-notices/slas-and-support-models#how-to-reach-support) if you need Arabic turned on for your account.
 

Every other language in the table is available to all accounts by default — no additional setup is required to select it.

## Changing your interface language

Your personal language preference overrides your organization's default language, which admins set account-wide. See [Personal info, format settings, and task delegation](/docs/use/personal-settings/personal-info-format-settings-and-task-delegation) for how to change your own setting.

nodes.label: SLAs and support models
nodes.snippet:
## How to reach support

Kissflow Community is the primary channel for support. Access it in-product by clicking the **(?)** icon next to your profile picture, or go directly to [community.kissflow.com](https://community.kissflow.com/). Use the **Get Help** section to post questions or report issues and defects — this is the main channel Kissflow support monitors, and questions about using the product get a prompt response.

Live chat is a separate, secondary channel for account-specific issues or defects. Open it from the same **(?)** icon in the top navigation bar — it launches a standalone chat widget rather than a thread inside Community. Chat availability can vary depending on your product and plan.

 
 Only use email ([support@kissflow.com](mailto:support@kissflow.com)) when you can't log in to Kissflow, for example during downtime. Emails are routed into the same support chat system and handled by support specialists.
 

## Status page

Kissflow's systems are actively monitored, and any issues are reported on the [Kissflow status page](https://status.kissflow.com/). Check this page first if you're seeing service availability issues or downtime.

## Chat response times

| Metric | Target |
| --- | --- |
| Conversations answered | Within 24 hours on business days |
| Average response time | About 30 minutes during business hours |

## Defect SLAs

Once a product specialist confirms a defect, the following SLAs apply based on severity. All timelines exclude Saturdays and
