Parameters: Accept (header); page_number (query) — This is the specific page number of an item list and is an optional parameter. The page number can be specified as 1, 2, 3, etc.; page_size (query) — This is the number of items that can be listed in a page and is an optional parameter. It can be 10, 25, 50, 100, etc.; fields (query) — Specify the fields that you require in the API response. You can specify multiple fields as comma separated values.; account_id (path) — (Required) Kissflow Account ID. Learn how to retrieve an [Account ID](https://community.kissflow.com/t/35h4az8/api-authentication#account-id) from Kissflow.; group_id (path) — (Required) The unique ID of a Kissflow group. Responses: 200, 401, 403, 404

nodes.snippet:
Kissflow API — Processes
GET /process/2/{account_id}/pwa/{process_id}/myitems
Get list of my items
Retrieves all the items that you have initiated and participated in within your process.
Parameters: Accept (header); page_number (query) — The specific page number of an item list. It can be specified as 1, 2, 3, etc.; page_size (query) — The number of items that can be listed in a page. It can be assigned any integer.; account_id (path) — (Required) Kissflow Account ID. Learn how to retrieve an [Account ID](https://community.kissflow.com/t/35h4az8/api-authentication#account-id) from Kissflow.; process_id (path) — (Required) The unique identifier for each process. You can find the process ID from the web URL. For instance, in the URL, `https://thestarkcorporation.kissflow.com/view/process/Leave_request/myitems/Draft`, `Leave_request` will be the process ID. Contact customer support if you need further assistance.
Responses: 200, 401, 403, 404

nodes.snippet:
Kissflow API — Processes
GET /process/2/{account_id}/admin/{process_id}/item
Get all items (Admin)
Retrieves the list of all items from a  process. Only Process Admins can call this endpoint.
Parameters: Accept (header); page_number (query) — The specific page number of an item list. It can be specified as 1, 2, 3, etc.; page_size (query) — The number of items that can be listed in a page. It can be assigned any integer.; apply_preference (query) — The applied preferences like filters, show/hide fields, and number of rows per page.; account_id (path) — (Required) Kissflow Account ID. Learn how to retrieve an [Account ID](https://community.kissflow.com/t/35h4az8/api-authentication#account-id) from Kissflow.; process_id (path) — (Required) The unique identifier for each process. You can find the process ID from the web URL. For instance, in the URL, `https://thestarkcorporation.kissflow.com/view/process/Purchase_reimbursement/myitems/Draft`, `Purchase_reimbursement` will be the process ID. Contact customer support if you need further assistance.
Responses: 200, 401, 403, 404

Contact customer support if you need further assistance.; table_id (path) — (Required) The unique identifier assigned to each table in your process form.; row_id (path) — (Required) The unique identifier assigned to each row of a table in your process form.; field_id (path) — (Required) The unique identifier assigned to each column (field) in your process form’s table.

Get a list of records from a lookup field
Retrieves a list of records from an internal datasource in a lookup field on a board.
Parameters: Accept (header); page_number (query) — This is the specific page number of an item list and is an optional parameter. The page number can be specified as 1, 2, 3, etc. Default value is 1.; page_size (query) — The number of items that can be listed in a page result and is an optional parameter. Default value is 10.; q (query) — This is the search string and is an optional parameter.; account_id (path) — (Required) Kissflow Account ID. nodes.label: POST /form/2/{account_id}/{form_id}/batch — Bulk update dataform records
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow API — Dataforms and dataform views > Dataforms
POST /form/2/{account_id}/{form_id}/batch
Bulk update dataform records
This endpoint performs bulk operations (creation and/or updates) on multiple records within a dataform. You can create/update up to **500 records** at a time.

To use it, send a JSON array in the **request body**. Each object in the array represents a single record operation (create or update). Structure each object based on the intended action:

**1\. To update an existing record:**

- Identify the record using its unique `_id` (string).
    
- Make sure there is no mention of the `_is_created` field and its `boolean` value here.
    
- `JSON example:`  
    `{ "_id": "existing-record-123", "fieldName": "new value", "anotherField": "updated data" }`
    

**2\. To create a new record:**

- Provide all fields required by the dataform, along with their initial values. Optional fields can also be included.
    
- Make sure to add the following field `_is_created` with the value `true` (boolean) to create a new record.
    
- `JSON example:`  
    `{ "_is_created": true, "requiredFieldA": "value A", "requiredFieldB": 123, "optionalFieldC": "some data" }`
    

**Key points:**

- You can mix objects for creation and updating within the same request array.
    
- Ensure your request's field names and data types match the dataform's schema.
Parameters: Content-Type (header); Accept (header); account_id (path) — (Required) Kissflow Account ID. Learn how to r

Retrieves all records of a dataform view.
Parameters: Accept (header); page_number (query) — This is the specific page number of an item list and is an optional parameter. Default value is 10.; q (query) — This is the search filter string, and is an optional parameter.; search_field (query) — This is the search filter string based on specific field and is an optional parameter.; account_id (path) — (Required) Kissflow Account ID. Learn how to retrieve an [Account ID](https://community.kissflow.com/t/35h4az8/api-authentication#account-id) from Kissflow.; form_id (path) — (Required) The unique identifier for each dataform. You can find the dataform ID from the web URL. For instance, if the dataform URL is `https://development-thestarkcorporation.kissflow.com/appbuilder/PACMS_A00/form/Testimonial_Form_A00`, `Testimonial_Form_A00` will be the `form_ID`. If you require additional assistance, please contact customer support.; view_id (path) — (Required) The unique identifier for each dataform view. You can fetch it from the network tab of your browser. This is a mandatory parameter.

nodes.snippet:
Kissflow API — Dataset and dataset views
GET /dataset/2/{account_id}/{dataset_id}/aggregation
Get aggregated details of dataset's fields
Retrieves aggregated details of a dataset's fields.
Parameters: Accept (header); q (query) — This is the search filter string, and is an optional parameter.; page_number (query) — This is the specific page number of an item list and is an optional parameter. The page number can be specified as 1, 2, 3, etc. Default value is 1.; page_size (query) — The number of items that can be listed in a page result and is an optional parameter. Default value is 10.; field_ids (query) — List of field IDs under the current dataset whose aggregation details are to be fetched specifically. It is an optional parameter and when not specified, aggregation for all the dataset fields is returned by default. The field IDs must be comma-separated, hence the query param should be properly URL encoded.; account_id (path) — (Required) Kissflow Account ID. Learn how to retrieve an [Account ID](https://community.kissflow.com/t/35h4az8/api-authentication#account-id) from Kissflow.; dataset_id (path) — (Required) The unique identifier for each dataset. nodes.label: POST /dataset/2/{account_id}/{dataset_id}/batch — Bulk update dataset records
nodes.snippet:
Kissflow API — Dataset and dataset views
POST /dataset/2/{account_id}/{dataset_id}/batch
Bulk update dataset records
This endpoint allows you to create or update up to 500 dataset records per request. It uses the `_id` field to determine the action: providing an existing `_id` updates the current record, while providing a new `_id` creates a new one.
Parameters: Content-Type (header); Accept (header); account_id (path) — (Required) Kissflow Account ID. Learn how to retrieve an [Account ID](https://community.kissflow.com/t/35h4az8/api-authentication#account-id) from Kissflow.; dataset_id (path) — (Required) The unique identifier for each dataset. You can find the dataset ID from the web URL. For instance, in the URL, `https://thestarkcorporation.kissflow.com/view/dataset/Bank_Dataset`, `Bank_Dataset` will be the dataset ID. Contact customer support if you need further assistance.
Request body:
Responses: 200, 401, 403, 404
